observations.recommendation: 17. We recommended, and management agreed that the Inventory Committee to undertake the required procedures in the disposition of non-existing/missing PPEs provided under Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

33. The Municipal Treasurer to complete the RPTAR and Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2023 and update the same regularly;

b. The OMA shall:

34. Review the RPT/ SET Receivable and its Deferred and adjust to its correct balances, upon receipt of the RPTAR and Certified list of Real Property Tax Delinquencies, and

35. Maintain ageing schedules of the RPT and SET Receivables.

38. We recommended, and Management agreed to create an Investigation Committee to determine the collectability of these accounts and conduct proper disposition of dormant receivables per Items 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular 2023-008.
55. GSO to update the IIRUP after disposal of unserviceable properties and furnish copy to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
advice the Municipal Accountant and the PhilHealth Focal Person of the Municipality to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a copy of Notice of Change of Bank Account for ACPS duly received by the PhilHealth, just in case the Municipality opts to replace its ACPS servicing bank;
advice the Municipal Accountant to recognize the cash deposit amounting to ₱15,000.00 that transpired on June 24, 2024 based on snapshot of TB- Trust bank account and inform in writing the Audit Team who made such cash deposit; and
advice the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why payments of TB Medicines amounting to ₱301,098.88 was paid by the Disbursing Officer and why such payments was subsequently recognized as collections and deposited under the newly opened TB- Trust Fund Bank Account.

We recommended that the cash advances to be granted to the Disbursing Officer should not exceed the maximum accountability allowable for the bond she applied for in compliance with the Revised Schedule of Bonds provided by the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the Disbursing Officer II to maintain a separate cashbook – for salaries, wages, allowances, etc. and petty operating expenses to monitor the balances of each account at a given period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer, being the head of Treasury Office, and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by the rules and regulations to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds.

Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts. Impose sanctions, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer direct the preparer of checks to be cautious in entering data in the face of the checks and device a control measure to avoid the frequent errors and waste of government resources. Moreover, all cancelled checks be attached to the RCI and a copy of which be submitted to the auditor’s office for review and verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provides the summary of deposits in addition to the summary of collections in the RCD starting on January 2025. Likewise, the remittances/deposits during a specific day should be certified true and correct by the Liquidating Officer/ Municipal Treasurer.  We further recommend that all deposits must be supported with a validated deposit slip.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
advise the Municipal Accountant to communicate in writing with the Branch Manager of LBP- Roxas Branch and inquire why the four (4) bank accounts noted above were not included and/or acknowledged in the bank confirmation reply sent to the Office of the Auditor;
immediately inform the Audit Team in writing on the result of the inquiry made by the Municipal Accountant; and
advise the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the bank reconciliation statement pertaining to LBP- Bigasan ng Bayan, LBP- DBM/LGSF Trust Funds and LBP- TB Trust Fund.

We appreciated the effort of the Municipality to collect the unliquidated cash advances of the concerned individual, however, we reiterate our recommendation that the Management fast tract the collections to avoid any legal actions that may arise due to non-liquidation of the above-mentioned cash advances.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance between the amount recorded in the books and the decrease of the affected PPE account in the Notes to Financial Statement amounting to ₱474,756.74;
direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to reconcile the variance of ₱9,224,000.00 between the IIRUP and the amount dropped from the books of accounts; and
direct the Disposal Committee to submit the floor price of properties that had been disposed.

We recommend that the Management:
through the Inventory Committee, submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities;
through the Inventory Committee, complete the conduct of physical count of PPE and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE in compliance with Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs; and
through the Municipal Accountant, undertake continuous reconciliation on the result of the physical count with the property and accounting records and adjust the books of accounts and the property records, as warranted, to reflect the correct balances of the affected PPE accounts in order to fairly present the PPE accounts in the financial statement as of year-end.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads/committee for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015:
Municipal Accountant;
Submit the required copies of PPE Local Road Network Ledger Cards (Annex D format) per Item VII a.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015;
Update the yearly required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule regularly for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal Engineer; and
Regularly provides the Municipal Accountant and GSO with complete description and cost segregation of road components.
Municipal General Services Officer.
Maintain and submit Local Road Inventory and Road Maps; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its components and submit the same to the Audit Team (Annex E format) per Item VII b.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
We also recommended that the Inventory Committee submit Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) supported with Local Road Network Maps (Annex C format) per Item VI.3 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required supporting information on the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 1 pertaining to the accounts Due to NGAS, Due to GOCCs and Due to LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-GSO:
to install internal control in processing the PR and PO by properly accomplishing the said forms in accordance with the instructions provided under The NGAS for LGUs;
to submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of any purchase order irrespective of amount, and each and every supporting document, within five (5) working days from issuance thereof; and
at the beginning of the year, prepare a PR for the supplies and material needed for the quarter based on the approved Annual Procurement Plan.

We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the head of office or the department needing the supplies that a Supplies Availability Inquiry (SAI) shall be used to inquire as to the availability of supplies needed from the Office of the Municipal Accountant. If the supplies needed are not available in stock, the requisitioning department shall prepare the PR and attached the SAI as a supporting document.

We recommended that the Management:
to refrain from appropriating fund for expenditures that are part of the LDRRMO’s regular operations and functions under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance;
we further recommend that expenditures that are part of the LDRRMO’s regular operations and functions be charged against the General Fund of the Municipality instead of the LDRRMF; and
submit written justification/ explanation or legal basis showing that Oplan Kontra Dengue and Oplan Kontra Rabies could be considered as disaster prevention and mitigation projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that:
the Local Chief Executive to continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by promptly implementing the priority development projects as reflected in its duly approved local development plans and establish the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries;
the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit the appropriation ordinance pertaining to the additional budget cost of the project “Concreting of Slaughterhouse’s Floor Slab; and
Municipal Engineer to maintain requiring timely implementation of PPAs in coordination with the MPDC under their responsibility at all times.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD database in order for the Municipality to formulate an updated gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.

We recommended that the Management adopt and strictly implement a clear accounting policy on the recording, monitoring and reporting of inventory items that strictly adheres to the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, particularly Sections 114, 119, 121, and 124.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
a. make all the necessary adjustments to the PPE-related accounts in the eNGAS, including the correction of reported account balances and editing of the estimated useful life of individual PPE items to their proper length; and
b. adopt the practice of rendering a detailed review and analysis of the PPE Lapsing Schedule on a regular basis and initiate correspondence with the COA Government Accountancy Sector (GAS) whenever technical concerns are encountered in the eNGAS.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, submit to the Municipal Mayor a proposed schedule to complete the physical count of PPE and stress thereon the importance of the required RPCPPE so that it will be given priority among other duties and responsibilities of the personnel concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the journal entries necessary to transfer the cost of the completed infrastructure project from the CIP to the appropriate PPE account and record the corresponding depreciation incurred thereof since their completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, the GSO and the Municipal Accountant collaborate to establish a detailed work plan with specific target dates, subject to the approval of the Mayor, to finish the inspection and physical count of local roads, submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System (RPCRNS) and record the local road networks and corresponding depreciation in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of Insurance Expense and Prepaid Insurance accounts in the books in accordance with Section 64, Chapter 3 of the NGAS Manual for LGUS, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer review and compare their respective records for CY 2024, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile the same and henceforth adopt the practice of reconciling their records on a regular basis.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) requests authority from COA for the derecognition from the books of accounts of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, as provided under Item 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
The Municipal Accountant prepares adjusting journal entries to revert the derecognition of untraceable PPE made in the accounting records to facilitate the reconciliation process.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Disposal Committee undertakes the required procedures in the disposition of unserviceable PPEs provided under DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 2024-001; and
The Municipal Accountant prepares the adjusting journal entries to revert the derecognition of unserviceable PPE and incidental cost of land made in the accounting records.

We recommended, and the management agreed that to trace the records of the unrecorded land to determine its purchase price or costs, otherwise, coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to determine the FMV of the land, and use the FMV as the initial cost of the land.

We recommended, and the management agreed that, the Municipal Treasurer, GSO, and Municipal Assessor reconcile their records with the book balance maintained by the Municipal Accountant and provide complete information and schedules of the Land amounting to P2.295 million.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare JEV to correct the erroneous transfer of road networks to the barangay and misclassification of accounts; and
Send a letter to the Supplier to process the unpaid portion of the CIP account, to facilitate the closing of the CIP account to the RN account.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Take up the necessary adjustments to correct the affected accounts; and
Review the CIP account simultaneously with the Advances to Contractor account for ongoing and completed projects to ensure that completed projects are timely transferred to its appropriate asset account.

We also recommended and the Municipal Engineering Office agreed to submit the Statement of Work Accomplished and completion report to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Take up the necessary adjustments to correct the affected amounts; and
Update the Lapsing Schedule and PPE Ledgers of the unrecorded depreciation to be reconciled with the total amount of Depreciation Expense for CY 2024 and prior years.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MHRMO to:
Review the Master List of earned leave credits of the officials and employees of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and submit to the Local Finance Committee thru the Office of the Municipal Budget for consideration in the appropriation for the succeeding years until the earned leave credits are backed up by appropriation and funds, prioritizing those retiring/end of term elective officials during the year;
Prepare the quarterly leave credits earned by officials and employees, with peso equivalent and supported with a copy of its respective leave card, as of every end of the quarter, starting CY 2025; and
Submit the Master List and the quarter report of earned leave credits of officials and employees to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books subject to the availability of appropriation and funds certified by the MBO and Treasurer.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Upon receipt of the Master List and the quarter report of earned leave credits of officials and employees from the MHRMO, record in the books the liability duly supported by a certification from the MBO and Treasurer on the availability of appropriation and funds.
Review and ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and IPSAS 20 by providing the required disclosures in the Notes to FS, specifically on the aggregate renumeration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and
Provide disclosure in the notes to FS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Conduct a verification of the actual existence of the FOL Inventories every year-end and reconcile the book balance against the account of the supplier;
Demand from the end-users to submit their FCR timely as basis of dropping the inventories from the books; and
Make necessary adjusting entries for the accounts affected.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare JEV in recording the misstatements mentioned; and
Stop the practice of adjusting the bank reconciling items, such as Outstanding Checks and Deposits in Transits, directly to the Books.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The LCE to issue a memorandum directing all end-users to timely prepare and submit their liquidation reports of distributed goods to the accounting office.
The Municipal Accountant:
Demand from the end-users and facilitators the submission of the liquidation reports for distributed goods to enable verification and ensure accurate recording; and
Prepare JEV to adjust the affected accounts, upon receipt of the distribution list.
The Inventory Committee to ensure the timely preparation of the RPCI, and submit the same to the GSO and Accounting Office for reconciliation of their records efficiently.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and other concerned offices to settle and resolve the disputes on RPT; and
Complete the RPTAR and Certified List of Taxpayers, and provide it to the Municipal Accountant as a proper basis for RPT receivables.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Review and reconcile the book balance against former to come up with accurate RPT/SET Receivable and its Deferred, upon receipt of the RPTAR and Certified list of Taxpayers; and
Maintain ageing schedules of the RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended, and Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare Schedules with complete details for Other Payables account;
Trace source documents of these outstanding balances to identify the payees/source agencies (for TF) and nature of the payables; and
Confirm the existence of these balances from the payees and/or source agencies.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The LCE to:
Issue a memorandum order enjoining all officers and employees with unliquidated CAs to immediately liquidate the balances;
Issue demand letter those employees who transferred from, or are no longer connected with the municipality, and request assistance to the agency they are currently connected with for the collection of the accountable officers’ outstanding cash advances;
The Municipal Accountant to:
Evaluate the probability of collection for CAs outstanding for over 10 years. If collection is deemed unlikely, a formal request should be made to the COA for the write-off of dormant, unliquidated cash advances that have remained non-moving for 10 years or more, in compliance with COA Circular 2016-005; and
Regularly monitor the movement of the cash advances of concerned AOs and ensure that prior to grant of additional cash advances to AOs with unliquidated cash advances, a certification in the face of the DV is made that the AO has not unliquidated CA.

We recommended, and LCE agreed to:
Reconstitute the members of GFPS TWG;
Issue a directive to all LGU department/offices for the preparation of their annual GPB and annual GAD AR as early as January preceding the budget year; and
Require the GFPS TWG to consolidate the gender issues and PPAs proposed by the different departments/offices, prepare GPB and submit the same the PPDO and the DILG Provincial Office not later than March 31 preceding the budget year.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to stop the granting of any additional allowances, incentives, or other financial benefits to officials and employees unless expressly authorized by law.

observations.recommendation:
I. Municipal Accountant:
 
Prepare adjusting journal entries on the errors committed in the recording of transaction involving PPE accounts in the accounting records as presented in Appendix C-1; and

Prepare detailed schedules of all classes of PPE and submit together with the financial statement (FS) to support the PPE account balances reported therein.

II.Inventory Committee

3.	Reconcile Report on Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with accounting records especially those PPEs recognized in the books but not recorded in the RPCPPE and vice versa and inconsistent classification of PPEs between the FS and RPCPPE and submit the corrected RPCPPE after completion of the reconciliation process.

4.	The Office the Municipal Accountant (OMA) and General Service Office (GSO) coordinate with each office and work together to complete the reconciliation process of all accounting and property records of the Municipality by strictly adhering to the reconciliation procedures embodied in paragraph 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the correct PPE balance at the end of the CY 2024.

15. Take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at station and prepare/maintain corresponding PPELCs based on the List of PPEs Found at Station; and

16. Prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS in relation to PPEs found at station and non-existing/missing PPEs.

36. Examine and review the supporting documents of the outstanding payables and communicate with the creditors/payees for possible payments of the claims if determine whether these payables are due and demandable such that deliveries had been made; and

37. If the payables are valid, notify the payees of their claims and submit letter request for payment.

45. We recommended, and the management agreed to instruct the BAC to properly conduct the preliminary examination of bids of bidders outlined in Section 23.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and ensure that bidders rated “passed” submitted the complete eligibility documents such as the legal, technical and financial documents.

46. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the OMA to require the suppliers of goods to post warranty security in accordance with Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184, which shall remain effective for a minimum of three months or one year from the date of acceptance by the Procuring Entity of the delivered supplies, moving forward

47. Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the OMA to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF by preparing a disbursement voucher and the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check and deposit the same to affect the transfer as at year-end instead of a mere as set-up of obligation.

48. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GSO to notify the Audit Team of the deliveries of goods and services within 24 hours after its acceptance thru submission of AIR and its supporting documents such as PO or contracts, delivery receipts/sales invoice/charge invoice, and purchase requests, for proper evaluation thereof.

a. The GSO to:

49. Prepare the Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI), Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC), and Inventory Custodian Slip for all semi-expendable property, especially those derecognized in CYs 2022 and 2023; and

50. Prepare RegSPI for all future issuances of semi-expendable property which shall be the basis in preparing the journal entry voucher (JEV) to recognize the issuance taking into consideration the newly issued COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024, the supplemental guidelines on the implementation by LGUs of COA Circular No. 2022-004.

b. The OMA to:

51. Prepare and maintain Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card for each semi-expendable property and shall be reconciled with the SPC of the GSO; and

52. Prepare JEV to reverse the derecognition of immovable PPEs and its corresponding depreciation.

53. The OMA and GSO to collaborate in the reconciliation of their records and preparation and maintenance of the prescribed forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties.

54. We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the GAD Focal Point System to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.
56. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to commit on completing his cashbooks and reconcile with the records of the Municipal Accountant;

We appreciated the effort of the Municipality to collect the unliquidated cash advances of the concerned individuals, however, we reiterated our recommendation that the Management fast tract the collections to avoid any legal actions that may arise due to non-liquidation of the above-mentioned cash advances.
