observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

Explain or justify the legal basis in fixing the monthly rate paid to Ms. Elsa R. Viray;

Submit the Approved Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports of Ms. Viray from January 2022 to December 2022 to the Office of the Auditor; and

Refrain from hiring private individual whose function is redundant to the respective function of regular employees of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No Management action.
observations.reason: No submitted justification on the monthly rate.

observations.recommendation:
We appreciated that the Municipality of Roxas had passed the required procedural due process for their reprogramming on the above subject projects, however, we recommended to the Management the following:

Formulate more realistic socio-economic and environmental programs and projects and prioritize their implementation according to the needs of the constituents to avoid constant reprogramming and reversion;

Focus on those projects and activities proposed or recommended by the members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council that are endemic to their area of jurisdiction that will fit the genuine capability of the Municipality to undertake such projects directed to yield and precise Annual Investment Plan; and

Require the Executive Committee to ensure that the decisions of the council are faithfully carried out and implemented.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented





Implemented





Implemented
observations.reason: The Management reprogrammed the purchase of lot into the construction of infirmary building.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

To protect its property and interests, the City of Roxas would execute a Contract of Lease with its tenants with specific provisions for collection, damage reparation, penalties and interest; and

To institute methods and strategies to improve its collection, especially concerning the tenants of RCITT, Paseo de Roxas, and Libas Fishing Port.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record procured drugs and medicines and supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize in the books the assets and record the related expense upon its issuance supported by SSMI and RIS.

Direct OIC-Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to update the recording in the Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Cards, respectively, by each type of inventory item to receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective balances monthly.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Provide the Municipal Accountant with certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index as a bases of recording the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred RPT /Deferred SET Income; and	
	
Intensify the collection of RPT/SET Receivable to include delinquent accounts by providing tax amnesty and post at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the municipal hall pursuant to Section 254 of the RA 7160;

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

1.   Establish and record RPT Receivable and SET Receivable, at the beginning of the year, based on the certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index for the current year provided by the Municipal Treasurer using the following entries:

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Review and update the "Chapter 12 - PLAN IMPLEMENTATION" of the LGU's SWM Plan to ensure the sustainability, viability, effectiveness, relevance, intensify the efforts and ensure the implementation of all PPAs relative to the Solid Waste Management Program of the LGU;

Enforce strict compliance with the significant provisions of RA No. 9003 and devise local ordinances relative to proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste; and

Provide the allocation of funds for the acquisition of additional SWM equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer (GSO) reconcile the balance of PPE between the accounting and property records and the GSO prepare Property Cards for each category of Property, Plant and Equipment.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the required supporting information on the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 1 for the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus and disclose the same on the face of the Cash Flow Statement or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Section 29 of IPSAS No. 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present fairly in the Financial Statements the balance of the unliquidated fund transfer from the DSWD as at year-end and the MSWDO explain or justify the delay in the submission of the supporting documents to liquidate the amount already disbursed by the Municipality amounting to ₱695,454.00. We further recommended that the MSWDO immediately submit to the Accounting Office the supporting documents needed to properly liquidate the amount of ₱695,454.00 and for recording purposes. After which, the Accounting Office will officially transmit these documents to the Auditor’s Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile immediately those reconciling items stated in Table I and explain/justify why those reconciling items are not yet reconciled as of December 31, 2023 considering that most of them are long outstanding reconciling items.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all development projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready to complete them within the target completion date to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management:
Formulate more realistic socio-economic and environmental programs and projects and prioritize their implementation according to the needs of the constituents to avoid constant reprogramming and reversion;
Focus on those projects and activities proposed or recommended by the members of the Municipal Development Council that are endemic to their area of jurisdiction that will fit the genuine capability of the Municipality to undertake such projects directed to yield and precise Annual Investment Plan; and
Require that the Executive Committee ensure that the decisions of the council are faithfully carried out and implemented.

We recommended that the Management refrain from appropriating fund for the repair and maintenance of DRRM Equipment and Vehicles, gasoline, oil and lubricants of response vehicle and insurance of the Municipal Building under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) – Disaster Preparedness thematic area to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance. We further recommended that maintenance of emergency vehicles, gasoline, oil and lubricants and insurance of the Municipal Building be charged against the general fund of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit at the beginning of the year to the Audit Team the List of all on-going government projects, programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) within the time frame stated in Section 12.2 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 as reiterated in GPPB Resolution No. 11-2020, dated May 20, 2020 for monitoring and compliance with the provisions of the Procurement Law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from recording transactions under the account Advances to Officers and Employees that are not related to cash advances granted for official travel. We recommended further that cash advances granted and recorded under such account should not be utilized other than the purpose stated therein. Moreover, strictly observe that cash advances granted for local official travel should be liquidated within thirty (30) days after the return of the concerned officials and employees.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain that a refund of cash advances should be reported and deposited to the proper fund of the Municipality.

We appreciated the effort of the Municipality to collect the unliquidated cash advances of the concerned individuals, however, we reiterated our recommendation that the Management fast tract the collections to avoid any legal actions that may arise due to non-liquidation of the above-mentioned cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require those employees who were granted cash advances for PC to submit to the Office of the Auditor the cash book maintained by them as Petty Cash Custodian;
Justify the establishment of Petty Cash amounting to ₱165,000.00 and submit an Ordinance, if any, authorizing the establishment of the said amount; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit to the Office of the Auditor the CAFOA to support the cash advances granted for PC in January 2023.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Report of Collections and Deposits Number under the column “reference” in her cashbook and update her cashbook in hand writing;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Cashbook- Cash in Bank, per fund/bank account; and
Require the Liquidating Officer, all Tellers/Collectors, Field and Market Collectors to maintain a cashbook as required under Section 96, Volume I of GAM for LGUs. All transactions shall be recorded daily in their cashbook.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Treasurer:
Take custody of and exercise proper management of the funds of the Municipality of Roxas as required under the Local Government Code;
Instruct the disbursing officer to refrain from utilizing the petty cash from the General Fund to pay in advance those expenses chargeable against the Trust Fund of the Municipality and utilize the trust fund for which the fund was intended for; and
Instruct the disbursing officer to maintain a separate cashbook – cash advance for trust fund account and petty cash in her custody to monitor the balances of each account at a given period.

We recommended that the Disbursing Officer II rule and foot the balances of her cashbook at the end of each month and accomplish the required certification in the cashbook, as provided by the NGAS Manual. This will facilitate efficient monitoring of accounting balances and prompt detection and correction of errors, as well as the audit thereof. However, we appreciated the disbursing officer for updating her cashbooks using her handwriting.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO):
Furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of contracts and Purchase Orders (PO), irrespective of amount and each of every supporting document, within five working days from the issuance thereof, in compliance with Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Notify the Office of Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of procured items within 24 hours from its acceptance, in compliance with Item A (2) of COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
The Property Unit:
Ensure that the property cards are updated by posting all unrecorded acquisition/receipt, issue/transfer and disposal of PPE, if any;
Prepare a List of PPEs found and submit to the Accounting Unit for recording/verification in the books of accounts; and
The Accounting Unit:
Cause the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the property records and take up necessary accounting entries to ensure that the total balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the City Treasurer to send written notices to the payees at least one month before payment checks become stale as required in Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUS, Volume I; and
Require the City Accountant to review the outstanding checks that are considered stale totaling ₱181,163.29 as of December 31, 2023, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the misstated accounts

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Enhance the monitoring of the implementation of PPAs funded by the National Government and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay all  funded PPAs that are on-going and/or not started to attain the program’s objectives; and
Require the City Accountant to review and analyze the Due to NGAs account in the TF and cause the immediate remittance/return of any unexpended/unutilized amount of fund transfer to the concerned NGAs after project completion pursuant to the aforementioned Circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the CDRRMO to ensure that all identified and prioritized development PPAs funded from the five percent LDRRMF are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Likewise, require the City Engineering Office to expedite its execution to guarantee their prompt accomplishment;
Require the CDRRMO, in coordination with the CEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development PPAs embodied in the LDRRMFIP so that any cause of delay in the project implementation would be immediately addressed and more desired benefits would be delivered to the constituents as planned; and
Require the CDRRMO to evaluate the necessity or relevance of the identified priority PPAs which remained unimplemented as of year-end, and ascertain the viability of its implementation in the ensuing year to better address disaster related concerns of the City.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized PPAs funded under the GAD Program that can be attributed thereto, and address any cause of delay to foster timely implementation; and
Regularly assess the feasibility of its PPAs during the year and if deemed necessary, realign to other priority projects that would best address more prevalent gender issues that could have an impact on the intended beneficiaries, and at the same time address the various needs of the stakeholders.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit a written justification on the incomplete information in the PAPs signboards;
Require the Project Managers/Engineers and contractors to provide the required date in the installed signboards and update them regularly to provide the necessary information as to the status of each project's implementation; and henceforth,
Comply with all the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the program proponent to specifically identify in the Purchase Request and in the Terms of Reference the desired 15-kilo/bag weight, complete with preferred program tags and chronological numbering printed in the bags, and for the BAC to adhere to this specification;
Install CCTV Cameras for physical control in the warehouse for better monitoring and security of the goods in stock; and
Ensure that all funds and resources allocated to the program were properly and efficiently managed and safeguarded against losses in consonance with Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to post the RCDs daily in the Cashbooks based on the latest issuances of Official Receipts for the year;
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Voucher Entry to record the reconciling items in the Subsidiary Ledgers; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile monthly the Cashbooks and Subsidiary Ledgers—Cash - Local Treasury to ascertain the completeness and correctness of account balances as reported in the books.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Make necessary adjustments in the books to correct the balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts;
Return the unutilized balances of fund transfers to the Source Agency as stipulated in the MOA, otherwise, remit the same to the National Treasury; and
Make necessary adjustments regarding stale checks which remained outstanding after six months but within two years from date of its issue.

We recommended and the Management agreed to Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Observe compliance with the perpetual inventory system in the recording of the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implemented by administration by recording all purchases under the account “Construction Materials Inventory”; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer/designate a Municipal GSO who is tasked to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same, and to submit a summary of the utilized Construction Materials Inventory monthly to the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱2.987 million to a special trust fund using the following Journal Entries:
For General Fund Books:
Dr.         Subsidy to other Funds                  xx
Cr.         Cash in Bank                                           xx
For Trust Fund Books
Dr.   Cash in Bank                                         xx
Cr     Trust Liability- DRRMF                                    xx
(with SL for the source and year of transfer)
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF transferred to Special Trust Fund by year of transfer.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to effect the necessary adjusting entries and henceforth, comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 “Prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units”.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Stop charging administrative in nature expenses to the LDRRM Fund as set forth in the limitations for projects/activities provided in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014; and
Direct the DRRMO to submit an explanation/justification for the utilization of the LDRRMF for purposes other than authorized.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements as indicated in Annex K such as detailed engineering drawing/plan, delivery receipts, notice to proceed, DTR, etc.;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check and verify the completeness of supporting documents on various claims before signing; and
Direct all concerned officials/employees to process disbursements vouchers only with complete supporting documents and with the approval of proper/authorized officials to establish its validity/propriety, otherwise, the transaction shall be suspended/ disallowed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the documents including MOA on fund transfers from NGAs on the prescribed period; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the receipts, documentary requirements and utilization of the fund transfers.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Include the LDRRMFIP in a separate caption, the list of activities/PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF previous years;
b. Ensure that the utilization of the STF-LDRRMF is supported with a revised AIP duly endorsed by the MDRRMC in accordance with item 6.5 of the NDRRMC-DBM_DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and
c. Direct LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the monthly and annual Reports on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LCD to COA auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to install signboards in the prescribed project for every infrastructure project; and
Comply with the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the GSO Designate to comply with the required markings “For Official Use Only” on all motor vehicles of the agency measuring three inches, under which should be written the name “Municipality of President Roxas”, to appear on each side, thus, ensuring the exclusive use for official business only; and
Secure government plates for the 11 motor vehicles

We recommended, and management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant:
Prepare adjusting journal entries on the errors committed in the recording of transaction involving PPE accounts in the accounting records as presented in Appendix C-1; and
Prepare detailed schedules of all classes of PPE and submit together with the FS to support the PPE account balances reported therein.
Inventory Committee
Reconcile RPCPPE with accounting records especially those PPEs recognized in the books but not recorded in the RPCPPE and vice versa and inconsistent classification of PPEs between the FS and RPCPPE and submit the corrected RPCPPE after completion of the reconciliation process.
The OMA and GSO coordinate with each office and work together to complete the reconciliation process of all accounting and property records of the Municipality by strictly adhering to the reconciliation procedures embodied in paragraph 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the correct PPE balance at the end of the CY 2024.

We recommended, and management agreed that the OMA to:
Take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at station and prepare/maintain corresponding PPELCs based on the List of PPEs Found at Station
Prepare appropriate disclosures in the NFS in relation to PPEs found at station and non-existing/missing PPEs; and

We recommended, and management agreed that the Inventory Committee to undertake the required procedures in the disposition of non-existing/missing PPEs provided under Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We revised our prior years audit recommendations, and management agreed that:
The OMA and GSO to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of their records to determine the costs and accumulated depreciation of all unserviceable properties of the Municipality to facilitate immediate disposal of the same;
The GSO, through its disposal committee, to conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular 92-386, to prevent further deterioration and loss of additional income; and
The OMA to derecognize/drop from the books of accounts all unserviceable properties after disposal based on the properly accomplished and approved IIRUP, Official Receipts and other pertinent documents and recognize gain/loss on disposal, if any.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Treasurer to complete the RPTAR and Certified list of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2023 and update the same regularly;
The OMA shall:
Review the RPT/SET Receivable and its Deferred and adjust to its correct balances, upon receipt of the RPTAR and Certified list of Real Property Tax Delinquencies, and
Maintain ageing schedules of the RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the OMA in coordination with the GSO to:
Examine and review the supporting documents of the outstanding payables and communicate with the creditors/payees for possible payments of the claims if determine whether these payables are due and demandable such that deliveries had been made; and
If the payables are valid, notify the payees of their claims and submit letter request for payment.

We recommended, and Management agreed to create an Investigation Committee to determine the collectability of these accounts and conduct proper disposition of dormant receivables per Items 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular 2023-008.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the OMA to:
Prepare adjustments of the understated inventory and overstated expense account by P24,248.00;
Supplies ledger card for the Accountable forms inventory for monitoring; and
Conduct timely reconciliation of their records with CRAAF and RPCI, of the Municipal Treasurer, and GSO in order to arrive an accurate balance of the Accountable forms inventory and Expense.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the OMA to:
Prepare Schedules with complete details for Other Payables account;
Trace source documents of these outstanding balances to identify the payees/source agencies (for trust fund) and nature of the payables; and
Confirm the existence of these balances from the payees and/or source agencies.

We recommended, and the management agreed to instruct the BAC to properly conduct the preliminary examination of bids of bidders outlined in Section 23.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and ensure that bidders rated “passed” submitted the complete eligibility documents such as the legal, technical and financial documents.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the OMA to require the suppliers of goods to post warranty security in accordance with Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184, which shall remain effective for a minimum of three months or one (1) year from the date of acceptance by the Procuring Entity of the delivered supplies, moving forward

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the OMA to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF by preparing a DV and the MT to draw a check and deposit the same to affect the transfer as at year-end instead of a mere as set-up of obligation.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GSO to notify the Audit Team of the deliveries of goods and services within 24 hours after its acceptance thru submission of AIR and its supporting documents such as PO or contracts, delivery receipts/sales invoice/charge invoice, and purchase requests, for proper evaluation thereof.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the following:
The GSO to:
Prepare the RegSPI, SPC, and ICS for all semi-expendable property, especially those derecognized in CYs 2022 and 2023; and
Prepare RSPI for all future issuances of semi-expendable property which shall be the basis in preparing the JEV to recognize the issuance taking into consideration the newly issued COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024, the supplemental guidelines on the implementation by LGUs of COA Circular No. 2022-004.
The OMA to:
Prepare and maintain SPLC for each semi-expendable property and shall be reconciled with the SPC of the GSO; and
Prepare JEV to reverse the derecognition of immovable PPEs and its corresponding depreciation.
The OMA and GSO to collaborate in the reconciliation of their records and preparation and maintenance of the prescribed forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties.

12.3 We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the GFPS to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer, being the head of Treasury Office, and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by the rules and regulations to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts. Impose sanctions, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that she would direct the preparer of checks to be cautious in entering data in the face of the checks and device a control measure to avoid the frequent error and waste of government resources. Moreover, she agreed that all cancelled checks be attached to the RCI and a copy of which be submitted to the auditor’s office for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that she would instruct the Disbursing Officer II to maintain a separate cashbook – cash advance for each account in her custody to monitor the balances of each account at a given period.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees concerned to remind them of the existence of check/s which became stale.  After which she shall issue new checks upon presentation/surrender of the cancelled stale check and the corresponding DVs with supporting documents be returned by her to the Municipal Accountant.  The corresponding amounts of cancelled stale checks should be reverted to the book balance.

The Municipal Accountant to furnish the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Audit Team Leader copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) relative to the adjustments made and provide Bank Reconciliation Statements between the Municipal Treasurer’s Cashbook for Cash in Bank Accounts and with that of her Cash in Bank-General Ledger Balances showing that the said accounts are reconciled regularly.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management remind the Municipal Accountant that non-bonded officers and employees should not be granted any cash advances unless if it is for travelling purposes and refrain from processing the disbursement vouchers for a new cash advance for those with previous unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Management:

Determine those receivables where collection is no longer possible and request authority to write-off therefor from COA, duly supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005;

Advise the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in gathering data/details to substantiate the amount deposited under LBP bank account- LGU Roxas with account number 851-088-288 to properly utilize the amount deposited thereon; and

Ensure that all obligations are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 41 of EO No. 292 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to submit yearly to the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers, with the amount due and collectibles, which would be the basis of the latter, to set-up the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the accounting period; likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of the RPT/SET Receivables as at December 31, 2022, amounting to ₱121,861,140.36 and immediately inform the Auditor on the reconciling items, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management submit immediately the Approved Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports of Mr. John Lago, Mr. Victor Dungo and Ms. Arminda Rubis from January 2022 to December 2022 to the Office of the Auditor and explain or justify the legal basis in fixing the monthly rate paid to them.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management thru the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects.

Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign, employ a realistic and effective collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the taxpayers to pay their tax on time and to attain one hundred per cent 100% collection efficiency so that more programs, projects and activities can be implemented for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, as part of management action, justify the delays noted in the implementation of the projects together with the submission of the Statement of Work Accomplished as of the expiry date for the computation of liquidated damages and other related documents for further audit evaluation and for the State Technical Audit Specialist’s reference.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer rectify the cracks on the wall and span of concrete pavement of the National Child Development Center and Concreted Local Access Road, respectively and communicate to the Audit Team upon completion for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality complete the equivalent deficient amount through work completion otherwise, the same amount shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality enforce the collection of liquidated damages for the 61 days of delay for the project Construction of Barangay Health Station at Brgy. Doña Concha. If payments were already been made, submit copies of the Official Receipts and validated deposit slips to the audit team for validation. We also recommended that the project Concreting of Access Road connecting Muñoz West, Marcos and Sinamar Main Road be evaluated and adopt the designed rectification of the DPWH on the national road per recommendation of COA-TAS and be effected in the pavement cracks which was rectified and has deteriorated. As such, future infrastructure projects be properly implemented and monitored to avoid delays and other project deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to draw the necessary journal entries to book up the Land properties as basis, if available, the assets’ acquisition costs, otherwise, the assessed values as provided by City Ordinance No. 055-2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the BAC Secretariat to timely and regularly furnish the Roxas City Information Division with the bidding documents for uploading on every procurement that the City Government undertakes; and

Direct the RCID to publish through its official website, the details of procurement being undertaken by the City Government including readily downloadable bidding documents and the minutes of the Pre-bid Conference.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the CA to use the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer's Index Cards submitted by the City Treasurer as the basis for the establishment of RPT/SET Receivables at the start of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded under the LDRRMF so that problems causing a delay in the project implementation would be timely addressed; and

Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, and as deemed necessary, revise and update its program to determine the constraints and reasons for failure to fully utilize the 70 percent LDRRMF and prior year’s unspent/unutilized funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

﻿﻿﻿Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat together with the Accountant to immediately submit all the lacking supporting documents enumerated in Annex K to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the procurements and strictly adhere to subsections of Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;

﻿﻿﻿Direct the BAC Secretariat to submit contracts and their attached documents to the Office of the Auditor within 5 working days in adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

﻿﻿﻿Direct the Accountant to ensure proper and complete supporting documents are attached to all disbursement vouchers.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to reinstate the prematurely written-off receivables from the DPWH under the Due from NGAs account;

Direct the City Accountant to initiate the write-off of long-dormant accounts and undertake the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in Sections 6, 7, and 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and 

Conduct periodic monitoring and analysis of the dormant accounts for the issuance of demand letters and provide Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables account, until they are finally approved for write-off.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the City General Services Officer to submit justification as to why these subject properties were not yet titled in the name of the Roxas City Government; and

Provide appropriate funds for the expenses incidental to secure ownership and prioritize the transfer of the title of the subject properties to protect its interest.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the City Accountant to submit immediately the financial reports and accounts listed in Annex N which are already overdue; 

Implement adequate measures that will facilitate the timely submission of the financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review; and

Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
 
Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized gender-related PPAs funded under the GAD Program and address the specific cause of delay and other prevailing problems to foster timely project implementation; and

Regularly assess the implementability of its PPAs during the year, and if deemed necessary, realign to other priority doable projects that would address more relevant gender issues that could have an impact on the intended beneficiaries and stakeholders.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the City Accountant to demand the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials and employees of the cash advances totaling ₱1.900 million and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts
observations.reason: Management required the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials and employees of cash advances, and stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers/employees with unsettled accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the AO to prepare Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) for the unaccounted cash tickets totaling 2,226 pcs. / ₱11,130.00 which are deemed issued, and remit the said amount to the City Treasurer Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed to enhance closer monitoring activities on fund transfer and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay funded PPAs of all on-going/not started PPAs to attain the program’s objectives;

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Implement immediately the PPAs identified/programmed for the year in the Local School Board Budget, particularly for Capital Outlay (CO) intended for blended/distance learning modalities in order to provide effective and safer learning opportunities in times of pandemic; and

Ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of appropriated funds for priority PPAs and reinforce the budgeting priorities by preparing the SEF Budget based on the DepEd approved School Improvement Plan (SIP) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDP) and consider DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular Nos.1 and 2 in prioritizing SEF PPAs under the new normal set-up.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify strictly compliance to the standard documentary requirements in every government expenditure transaction; 

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reclass Motor Vehicle to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment for proper account name and code and secure Memorandum of Agreement regarding its ownership and maintenance of the Rescue Equipment; and

Direct Municipal Engineer to provide Certificate of completion all projects conducted by administration duly acknowledge by Punong Barangay where the project was undertaken.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.  Direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to verify strict compliance to the standard documentary requirements in every government expenditure transaction; 

b.  Direct Municipal Accountant to reclass DDR Equipment account to Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution account and issue Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to end-user; and

Direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF, through the LDRRMC and LCD, to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the General Supply Officer to prepare Report on Local Road Network and conduct Physical Count of the Road Network annually; 


Direct OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Voucher Entry to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; and

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer complete description and cost segregation of road components for road project.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. 	Direct the OIC-Municipal Municipal to provide allowance for depreciation for Infrastructure   Assets annually; and

b. 	Direct OIC-Municipal Municipal to prepare Journal Voucher Entry to record unadjusted depreciation on PPE pertaining to prior year’s period charged against Prior Period Adjustment.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify strict compliance to the standard procedure in recording Accounts Payable and recognition of stale checks; 

 Direct OIC-Municipal Accountant to reclass stale checks to Accounts Payable after six months of dormancy; and 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at year end for submission to the Accounting Office.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Limit the hiring of JO and Contractual personnel in accordance with the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs to avoid redundant, unnecessary and duplication of work accomplishments; and

Submit accomplishment reports for the work/job performed covering the pay period duly certified by the immediate supervisor.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to account for the difference of 298 vials and to secure duly filled-out copies of RIS from PHO every time goods are received/transferred from other agencies and reconcile all RIS at year end;

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to submit the data to the Municipal Accountant for recording of donated vaccines and for disclosure in the Financial Statements; and

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to prepare and submit Inventory Report of Vaccines as prescribed in Section 6.3 COA Memorandum No. 2021-015 as follows:

dates of receipts and expiration dates;
quantity and cost of vaccines or supplies issued or distributed for inoculation, wastage or spoilage, and remaining balance of vaccines and supplies; and
dates of issuance and inoculation

Direct the Municipal Health Unit to conduct regularly inventory of drugs and medicines to account the number of vaccines received and issued to establish its existence and condition.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. 	Designate an accounts officer who will be assigned to transmit demand letters, communicate with the debtors and/or determine their whereabouts, to retrieve the necessary documents to update existing records;

2. 	Through the designated account officer, issue demand letters to all accountable officials/debtors with outstanding receivables; and

3. 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate/implement collection strategies and exhaust all possible means to enforce collection and initiate appropriate legal actions to recover from officials and employees who were already separated from service.

1. Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the PHO to ask for the JEV of the donated vaccines and supplies and the same be communicated to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government.
2. Moreover, if securing of the required documents is not feasible, management to conduct appraisal of the vaccines and supplies to determine the amount to be booked-up and facilitate immediate recording of the same.
3.	In both circumstances, the transactions are to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:

1. 	Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct and /or restate the balances of affected accounts in the comparative financial statements in the ensuing year; and

2. 	Ensure that the necessary disclosures required in the implementation of the change in accounting policy are presented in the LGU’s Notes to FS.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant:

1. 	To comply the provisions stated in IPSAS 1 paragraphs 70 and 80; and

2. To retrieve information and records of the Malacañang Loan and provide appropriate details and action thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and the accountable officers to prepare and submit monthly RAAF and CRAAF for all accountable forms in accordance with Sections 50 and 51 of Volume II of the New Government Accounting System Manual.

We recommended that management thru the LCE to:
1. 	Direct the municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services, and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; and
2. 	Submit the required GAD AR in correct format.

We recommended that management:

1. 	Direct the LDRRMO to ensure that PPAs to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years in the TF will be included in the revised LDRRMFIP; and

2. 	To refrain charging administrative expenses to the LDRRMF unless only for disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1 and other related issuances to ensure that the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage thereof.
We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB), in allocating the funds of the SEF give priority of expenditures enumerated in Section 272 to achieve the primary objective of the fund.

We recommended that Management:

Determine those receivables where collection is no longer possible and request authority to write-off therefor from COA, duly supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005;

Advise the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in gathering data/details to substantiate the amount deposited under LBP bank account- LGU Roxas with account number 851-088-288 to properly utilize the amount deposited thereon; and

Ensure that all obligations are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 41 of EO No. 292 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No management actions.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant did not request authority to write-off therefor from COA.

Reiterated under Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer, being the head of Treasury Office, and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by the rules and regulations to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts. Impose sanctions, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that she would direct the preparer of checks to be cautious in entering data in the face of the checks and device a control measure to avoid the frequent error and waste of government resources. Moreover, she agreed that all cancelled checks be attached to the RCI and a copy of which be submitted to the auditor’s office for review and verification.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that she would instruct the Disbursing Officer II to maintain a separate cashbook – cash advance for each account in her custody to monitor the balances of each account at a given period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
The Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees concerned to remind them of the existence of check/s which became stale.  After which she shall issue new checks upon presentation/surrender of the cancelled stale check and the corresponding DVs with supporting documents be returned by her to the Municipal Accountant.  The corresponding amounts of cancelled stale checks should be reverted to the book balance.
The Municipal Accountant to furnish the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Audit Team Leader copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) relative to the adjustments made and provide Bank Reconciliation Statements between the Municipal Treasurer’s Cashbook for Cash in Bank Accounts and with that of her Cash in Bank-General Ledger Balances showing that the said accounts are reconciled regularly

We recommended that the Management remind the Municipal Accountant that non-bonded officers and employees should not be granted any cash advances unless if it is for travelling purposes and refrain from processing the disbursement vouchers for a new cash advance for those with previous unsettled cash advances.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management:
determine those receivables where collection is no longer possible and request authority to write-off therefor from COA, duly supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005;
advise the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in gathering data/details to substantiate the amount deposited under LBP bank account- LGU Roxas with account number 851-088-288 to properly utilize the amount deposited thereon; and
ensure that all obligations are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 41 of EO No. 292 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We appreciated the effort of the Municipality to collect the unliquidated cash advances of the concerned individuals, however, we reiterated our recommendation that the Management fast tract the collections to avoid any legal actions that may arise due to non-liquidation of the above-mentioned cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to submit yearly to the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers, with the amount due and collectibles, which would be the basis of the latter, to set-up the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the accounting period; likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of the RPT/SET Receivables as at December 31, 2022, amounting to ₱121,861,140.36 and immediately inform the Auditor on the reconciling items, if any.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management submit immediately the Approved Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports of Mr. John Lago, Mr. Victor Dungo and Ms. Arminda Rubis from January 2022 to December 2022 to the Office of the Auditor and explain or justify the legal basis in fixing the monthly rate paid to them.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
Explain or justify the legal basis in fixing the monthly rate paid to Ms. Elsa R. Viray;
Submit the Approved Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports of Ms. Viray from January 2022 to December 2022 to the Office of the Auditor; and
Refrain from hiring private individual whose function is redundant to the respective function of regular employees of the Municipality.

We recommended management thru the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:
a. Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects.
b.	Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign, employ a realistic and effective collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the taxpayers to pay their tax on time and to attain one hundred per cent 100% collection efficiency so that more programs, projects and activities can be implemented for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, as part of management action, justify the delays noted in the implementation of the projects together with the submission of the Statement of Work Accomplished as of the expiry date for the computation of liquidated damages and other related documents for further audit evaluation and for the State Technical Audit Specialist’s reference.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer rectify the cracks on the wall and span of concrete pavement of the National Child Development Center and Concreted Local Access Road, respectively and communicate to the Audit Team upon completion for evaluation.
We recommended that the Municipality complete the equivalent deficient amount through work completion otherwise, the same amount shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipality enforce the collection of liquidated damages for the 61 days of delay for the project Construction of Barangay Health Station at Brgy. Doña Concha. If payments were already been made, submit copies of the Official Receipts and validated deposit slips to the audit team for validation. We also recommended that the project Concreting of Access Road connecting Muñoz West, Marcos and Sinamar Main Road be evaluated and adopt the designed rectification of the DPWH on the national road per recommendation of COA-TAS and be effected in the pavement cracks which was rectified and has deteriorated. As such, future infrastructure projects be properly implemented and monitored to avoid delays and other project deficiencies.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for General Fund and Trust Fund on the Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account, in order to present fairly the balance of the affected account in the financial statements for General Fund and Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management already established a Trust Fund Books to fairly present the trust funds received in the Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management advise:

The Municipal Accountant (1) disclose in the CY 2021 Notes to Financial Statements the details on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF of the Municipality using the format as required under Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002; (2) transfer the Municipality’s unutilized balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2021 in the Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books under the account Trust Liability- DRRM; and (3) reconcile the unutilized balance of DRRMF between the report on the receipt and utilization of DRRMF amounting to ₱5,143,861.44 and the unspent balance of DRRM- Trust Fund amounting to ₱4,060,557.52 as per disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement; and 

The Municipal Budget Officer maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Quick Response Fund (QRF).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a revised report on the details on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribe format as required under Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002. She also explained that the unreconciled balance of the unutilized DRRMF between the report on the receipt and utilization of DRRMF amounting to ₱5,143,861.44 and the unspent balance of DRRM as disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statement amounting to ₱4,060,557.52 was due to non-inclusion in the Notes to Financial Statement of the unutilized balance of DRRMF for CY 2020 amounting to ₱1,004,116.92 and donations from other government agencies amounting to ₱79,186.00. Likewise, the Municipal Budget Officer already maintained a separate RAAO for QRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs on the fund transfers/ financial assistance received from NIA, PCSO/STL and PAGCOR, in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary journal entries to properly account the Due to Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations and submitted to the Office of the Auditor the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant:

draw the following journal entries on the succeeding payment of fifteen percent (15%) mobilization to the contractors of LGU Roxas:

Dr.	Advances to Contractors				xx
		Cr.	Cash in Bank- Local Currency Current Account 	xx

properly recognize and record in the books on the recoupment of the advances to contractor; and

record in the books of accounts for Trust Fund all private and government monies which have officially come into the possession of the local government to be used for specific purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries
observations.reason: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly utilize/implement the 20% Development Fund PPAs on time to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, Management already implemented the projects funded under the 20% Development Fund, though beyond the target period. However, all projects and programs for CY 2022 funded under the 20% development fund were implemented within the target period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the Appropriation Ordinance on the augmentation of savings from the 20% DF amounting to ₱100,000.00 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance. We further recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate funds exclusively for which they have been appropriated; and if there are savings to be augmented, an Appropriation Ordinance must be passed prior to the obligation of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the required Appropriation Ordinance to augment the amount of ₱100,000.00 for the payment of loan  funded under the 20% development fund.
The concerned contractor already paid the liquidated damages. Likewise, the Management already submitted the necessary documents as required in the observations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a justification/explanation on the disbursement of funds without appropriation. We further recommended that government funds must be disbursed exclusively for which they have been appropriated unless an ordinance had been passed for their realignment.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted the report containing the identified, specific and detailed projects for CY 2022 funded under the 20% DF and justified that the amount of ₱7.5 million pertains to programs and projects identified by them based on the submitted reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management justify/explain the payment of incentives to the drivers of the Provincial Government of Isabela. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Planning and Developmental Council, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, should strictly observe that the appropriation of 20% Development Fund be supported with identified, detailed and specific developmental programs and projects in the Annual Investment Program. We further recommended that for CY 2022, program of works should be prepared for each developmental project to support the expenses charged thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the detailed and specific PPAs funded under the 20% development fund.
The Management already submitted the SWA and submitted all the required documents noted in the observations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the LDRRMO prepare immediately the LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 and include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and submit it immediately to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, ensure timely preparation and submission of LDRRMFIP for the succeeding years and furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the LDRRMFIP for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

explain / justify the payment of the said incentive and submit the legal basis authorizing the payment thereof to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance; and

stop the practice of charging expenses to LDRRMF which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities.
observations.managementAction: The management justified and submitted their legal basis un authorizing such payment. For CY 2022, the Management did not charge any expenses which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Explain / justify the delayed submission of the contracts and its supporting documents of the thirty-eight (38) projects listed above to the Audit Team; and

b. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit and ensure that contracts and purchase orders/job orders irrespective of amount be properly accomplished and submitted to COA within five (5) working days from execution/approval, together with the complete supporting documents, pursuant to Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted the contracts with supporting documents of the projects, programs or activities noted under this observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit immediately to the Auditor’s Office the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other supporting documents for the month of December. Likewise, explain/justify the late submission of the said reports. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary reports on timely basis and observe provisions under COA Circular No. 2009-006 and P.D. 1445 in order to avoid sanctions set by the provision.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office submitted regularly the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other supporting documents within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of the following calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD.
observations.managementAction: The Management made timely remittance on the amount withheld for the month, however, they failed to reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted a specific guideline in granting financial assistance and was duly approved by the Sanggunian Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. The mode of procurement must be clearly indicated in the APP for strict compliance. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Management already prepared and consolidated their PPMP into APP and procured supplies for CY 2022 through public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We appreciated the effort of the Municipality to collect the unliquidated cash advances of the concerned individuals, however, we recommended that the Management fast tract the collections to avoid any legal actions that may arise due to non-liquidation of the above-mentioned cash advances. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the account Advances to Officers and Employees in recording the grant of cash advances for travelling purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to the concerned individuals and collected the amount of ₱646,000.00 for CY 2022.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) install sound internal control on the collection and remittance by requiring the Revenue Collectors to remit their collections daily and avoid keeping a large amount of money in their possession; (b) attach stubs of issued toll fees and used original duplicate copies of AF 54 (Marriage License Certificate) to the RCDs to substantiate toll fee and marriage license certificate collections; (c) ensure that the all accountable forms will be issued to employees who are properly bonded; (d) require the collectors to properly accomplish the Report of Collections and Remittances/Deposits as to report date, date of collection, and signatures of the concerned personnel among others; and (e) submit comments/justification on the above noted observation in the collection and deposit, and issuance and use of accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer conducted stringent control over the remittances.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower assigned to collect fees and due to voluminous transactions in the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 
 
Municipal Mayor as LSB Chairperson prioritize the formulation of the SEF Budget by causing the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget within the prescribed period; 


LSB convene a meeting especially during the formulation of the SEF Budget even without the presence of the LSB Chairperson as long as majority of all the members constitute a quorum with the Co-Chair as the presiding officer; and 

LSB ensure that the LSB Resolution approving the SEF Budget is signed by the Chairperson, the Co-Chair and the majority of all the LSB members.
observations.reason:
SB budget for CY 2021 was formulated and approved prior to the receipt of the AOM. Audit recommendations are to be implemented in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a. Secure authority from the Municipal Mayor designating the LDRRM Officer as Special Disbursing Officer for COVID-19 Related Activities;
b. Ensure that the DVs for subsequent cash advances be supported with certification from the OIC- Municipal Accountant that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books; and
c. Thereafter no cash advance shall be released without securing fidelity bond from Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: The OIC Municipal Accountant forgot to fix the certification in the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a. Require OIC- Municipal Accountant to book-up procured supplies to Inventory Account and use RIS and RSMI to support issuance of Inventory item;
b. Require GSO and other supplies/inventory custodians to undertake the actual physical inventory at least once a year and to submit RPCI to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 each year; and
c. Enjoin the GSO to reconcile with inventory custodians and OIC-Municipal Accountant the inventory balances in the books of account vis-a-vis its existence and actual conditions whether still serviceable or unserviceable.
observations.reason: Lacking personnel to do the task.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. The mode of procurement must be clearly indicated in the APP for strict compliance. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: No management actions.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 19

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the ensuing department
heads the enumerated courses of actions:
To the Municipal Accountant:
a) Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper adjusting
entries to correct the same;
b) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner, through
proper time management and sound internal control, in order to achieve a fair
presentation of financial statements;
To the Municipal General Service Officer:
c) Conduct complete physical count of all LGU properties together with other
members of the Inventory Committee of the LGU and report the same using the
prescribed RPCPPE form in NGAS Manual for LGUs;
d) Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the stock cards
and property cards in compliance with the applicable rules and regulations;
e) Conduct proper procedures to dispose of the unserviceable properties, in
coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the
books of the agency; and
f) Institute measures in the monitoring and controlling of the properties of the LGU.
Moreover, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the
concerned personnel to implement the “one-time cleasing” of PPE account,
following the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to
establish the reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a) Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA
Circular 97-002;
b) Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or satisfactory
explanation is received within thirty (30) days, cause the withholding of salary due
to the accountable officer/s; and
c) Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers who have
previous unsettled cash advances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), Municipal Planning and Development
Officer (MPDO), Municipal Accountant and other officers involved in the
implementation of the LDRRMF to fully implement the PPAs under the LDRRMF,
esspecially the 70% Mitigation Fund which were allocated for disaster preparedness
and to use the 30% Quick Response Fund for the COVID-related emergencies.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the vouchers and
other financial reports to the Office of the Auditor, and for the Local Chief
Executive to issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments
to adhere and commit to the action plan and proposed improvements;
b) Submit the remaining disbursement vouchers together with its supporting
documents and the RCDs with the duplicate copies of Official Receipts and
Validated Deposit Slips; and
c) Strategize to get a copy of the bank statement as of each prescribed period by
requesting from its depository banks. Moreover, ensure that bank reconciliation is
done and submitted within the prescribed period as stated in the above-cited
regulations.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer reconcile their cash
records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per general
ledger and cashbooks and provide justification on the cash deficit incurred in
Trust Fund totaling ₱28.36 million; and
b. Municipal Accountant exert effort to prepare and submit the bank
reconciliation statements for all bank accounts, and maintain subsidiary
ledgers for all bank accounts and regularly reconcile the amounts with their
respective control account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) recognize the discounts
for prompt and advance payments of RPT and SET and penalties, if any, in the
books of accounts of the Municipality in the period to which they accrue, (b)
reconcile the RPT and SET Receivables with the record of the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office and the accounts RPT, RPT Receivable, Deferred RPT Income,
SET, SET Receivable and Deferred SET Income, and make appropriate
adjustments, if warranted, (c) ensure that collection of SET be accrued to SEF
books on the period they were earned instead of recording in the period the cash
was actually transferred, and (d) adhere to the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,
Volume I on the proper recording of the set-up and collections of receivables from
RPT and SET and recognition of income in the respective books of the General and
Special Education Funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exert continuous effort
in the retrieval of records for the unaccounted balance of Advances for Operating
Expenses, Advances to Officers and Employees, and Other Receivable accounts by
coordinating with the Acting Municipal Treasurer and other concerned personnel to
facilitate the preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers pursuant to Section
10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; (ii) refrain from recording the
cash advances of disbursing officers directly to the expense accounts and properly
monitor its liquidation; and (iii) conduct a thorough review and analysis of the
Advances for Officers and Employees under GF and TF and reclassify to its proper
accounts, if warranted, pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant adopt accrual basis of accounting pursuant to
Paragraph 7 of IPSAS 1, Presentation of Financial Statements and institute a
system of control relative to the recording of receivables including
surcharges from Public Market stallholders and maintain corresponding
subsidiary ledgers for the account; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer reconcile the
receivable balances from the operations of Public Market and ensure that
income and receivable balances were accurately presented and recorded in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to Inventory
account upon purchase and Expense account upon issuance thereof pursuant
to the asset method of accounting for recording purchases of supplies and
materials as prescribed under Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs, and ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and IPSAS
12 in recording transactions in the books of the Municipality and effect the
necessary adjustments, if warranted;
b. GSO-designate conduct physical count of Inventories and prepare the RPCI
per account in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate maintain supplies ledger card
and supplies stock card with necessary complete details for each stock of
inventory to account the issuance of its inventories and conduct regular
reconciliation between the Accounting and GSO records for inventory
accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as
provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come
up with a reliable PPE balance;
b. Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count and technical
valuation of the property, plant and equipment to establish the existence,
ownership and condition of the properties owned by the Municipality
especially for the local road network to determine the current value of the
road components and facilitate the preparation of the RPCPPE and
RPCLRN, and ensure that the required details in the reports were accurately
and completely filled out;
c. Municipal Accountant and GSO allot time and effort to (i) coordinate and
prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the
property accounts, especially the previous year’s PPE acquisitions, (ii)
prepare and maintain property records and reports for all PPE accounts
especially for the Road Networks, and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal
Engineer to complete the maintenance of a road map with a complete
