description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
d. GSO prepare and maintain complete Property Cards and Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components and the Local Road
Inventory and Road Map to account for the Local Roads and reconcile with
the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office,
and collate the documents of the land owned by the Municipal Government
that remained uncovered by a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT);
e. Municipal Accountant (i) keep and maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards for
each category of PPE and subsidiary records for roads and components for
every road network, (ii) ensure proper valuation/measurement of
transactions in accordance with the IPSAS and classification of accounts in
accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs as prescribed by
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 and (iii) review the
entries made relevant to the above transactions and effect the necessary
adjustments, if warranted;
f. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with a
complete description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects; and
g. Appraisal and Disposal Committee account all determined unserviceable
properties to facilitate the preparation of required supporting documents for
disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the
Office of the Municipal Treasurer or other concerned offices for the retrieval of
records of all outstanding accounts payable; (ii) maintain appropriate subsidiary
records pursuant to Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II
and; (iii) ensure that the entries in the Accounts Payable accounts were made in
accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exert continuous effort
to collate the information/details on the previous years’ Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs
and Due to Other Funds account by coordinating with the Municipal Treasurer and
other concerned personnel to facilitate the preparation and maintenance of
subsidiary ledgers pursuant to Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,
Volume II; (ii) conduct a thorough review and analysis of the Due to LGUs account
under TF and reclassify to its proper account the funds that were not sourced from
the LGUs, if warranted, pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 1, 2015; (iii) review and analyze the details of transactions regarding the
negative balance in the Due to Other Funds account to identify the cause of the
negative balance and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted; and (iv)
reconcile the Due to/from accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts to ensure that all the identified differences are resolved and/or
adjusted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that income from
grants received by the Municipality and disbursements relative thereto are properly
recognized and classified in the books of accounts for fair presentation,
transparency and accountability of the funds utilized.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant review
the entries made under the OMOE account and effect the necessary adjustments, if
warranted, and ensure that the expenditures of the Municipality are properly
classified for fair presentation in the financial statement, in accordance with the
Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 1, 2015. Moreover, ensure proper monitoring of charges under the
account which should include maintenance of complete details and records of the
transactions.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) install sound
internal control on the collection and remittance by requiring the Revenue
Collectors to remit their collections daily and avoid keeping a large amount of
money in their possession; (b) attach stubs of issued toll fees and used original
duplicate copies of AF 54 (Marriage License Certificate) to the RCDs to substantiate
toll fee and marriage license certificate collections; (c) ensure that the all
accountable forms will be issued to employees who are properly bonded; (d)
require the collectors to properly accomplish the Report of Collections and
Remittances/Deposits as to report date, date of collection, and signatures of the
concerned personnel among others; and (e) submit comments/justification on the
above noted observation in the collection and deposit, and issuance and use of
accountable forms.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) require the
concerned officers and employees to liquidate the CAs as soon as the purpose
for which it was granted has been served in compliance with Section 4.1.3 of
COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) strictly monitor accountable officers with
outstanding cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash
advances to an employee prior to settlement of his/her previous cash
advances; and (iii) ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002
and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the complete documentation of
liquidation of CAs and reimbursement of traveling expenses; and
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure that the liquidation of the accountable
officers is within the reglementary period in compliance with the prescribed
guidelines under Sections 5.1.1 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat to comment on the (i) lapses in the
invitation to observers; (ii) incomplete data on posting of advertisement
requirements; (iii) undated bid evaluation report; and (iv) delayed posting of
NOA and NTP in PhilGEPS;
b. Municipal Accountant and the concerned municipal officials (i) provide
explanation on the noted non-compliance to DILG Memorandum Circular
No. 2020-088 and LBC No. 134; (ii) provide justification on the noted
deficiencies in the supporting documents; and (iii) submit the lacking
documents for audit purposes; and
c. BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and its RIRR
within which certain stages of the procurement process must be completed
and adopt the latest procurement process and forms prescribed by the GPPB
in conformity with the requirements of Section 6 of the RIRR on
standardization of government procurement.

We recommended that the:
a. MHO provide justification on the deficiencies noted in the liquidation of
COVID-19 Benefit grant and ensure the completeness and veracity of
documents before payments are made;
b. Municipal Accountant ensure that payments for AHDP, SRA and other
allowances were supported with complete documentation; and
c. Concerned Municipal Officials comment on the observations noted on the
procurement process undertaken by the Municipality during the COVID-19
situation and submit the lacking documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat to comment on the (i) lapses in the
invitation to observers; (ii) undated bid evaluation reports; (iii) lapses in
posting of advertisement requirements; (iv) lapses in the execution of NOA
and Performance Security; and (v) lapses in the posting of NOA and NTP in
PhilGEPS;
b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and other concerned municipal
officials provide explanation on the (i) lapses in the grant of suspension
orders, (ii) failure to impose liquidated damages, and (iii) deficiencies noted
in the supporting documents and submit the lacking documents for audit
purposes; and
c. BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and its RIRR
within which certain stages of the procurement process must be completed
and adopt the latest PBDs and forms prescribed by the GPPB in conformity
with the requirements of Section 6 of the RIRR on standardization of
government procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials
and employees to provide justification on the observations noted relative to the
ineligible and doubtful claims under the SEF, and refrain from the practice of
reimbursement and abide in the proper manner of government disbursement as
provided by Section 93 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRM Officer (i) monitor and facilitate the implementation of the PPAs
under the LDRRMF by monitoring the period of its implementation and
ensure that all projects appropriated in LDRRMF be implemented during the
year and optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the Municipality to
strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during
disasters; and (ii) evaluate the PPAs in the continuing and special trust fund
and make appropriate actions thereto by reprogramming to another MDRRM
PPAs that can help in reducing exposure to the potential impact of hazards
and related disasters in the Municipality;
b. MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide specific, measurable,
achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each
PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund;
c. Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer provide justification on the
observations discrepancies noted on the amounts of the Trust Liabilities –
DRRMF in the trial balance and RSULDRRMF, and ensure that the amount
reflected in the reports represents the accurate data of MDRRM Fund; and
d. Municipal Accountant review the entries made relative to the DRRM funds
received from other sources and effect necessary adjustments, if warranted,
and ensure that the DRRM funds for disaster relief and management purposes
received by the Municipality from other sources be classified under the special
trust liability account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the year-end trial
balance and financial statements for the 20% Development Fund and waterworks
system, market and slaughterhouse pursuant to Section 110 of the Manual.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) instititute
appropriate action regarding the delinquent market stall holders for collection of
their unpaid bills to boost their revenue and eliminate delinquencies or be kept to a
minimal amount; (b) strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of the New Revenue
Code of the Municipality; (c) impose surcharges to delinquent market stallholders
in accordance with Section 5A.05 (a) of the Revised Revenue Code of 2011 of Roxas,
Oriental Mindoro; and (d) periodically provide the list of delinquent market
stallholders to the Municipal Accounting Office for the latter’s basis of recording of
the corresponding receivables.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) trace the
unidentified and unremitted tax liabilities and facilitate their remittance to
the BIR; (ii) ensure reconciliation of respective records pertaining to taxes
due to the BIR; and (iii) ensure that proper control and segregation of duties
are in place in the recording, preparation of schedules, filing, and payment of
taxes due to the BIR to eliminate the occurrence of any unidentified and
unremitted tax liabilities; and
b. Municipal Accountant revisit the computation of taxes withheld from the
contractors/suppliers and effect necessary adjustments, if warranted, and
ensure that the withholding tax on the payments made to contractors is in
accordance with the BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No 5-2006 dated
November 2, 2005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person
and GAD key personnel to:
a. review gender issues and PPAs identified and ascertain its budgetary and
implementing requirements to ensure the timely realization of its purpose
and benefits;
b. facilitate the preparation of the GAD Agenda as well as continuous updating
of data for the GAD database as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB
and GAD AR;
c. ensure and monitor attendance of the GPFS members to GAD required
trainings to capacitate and enhance their knowledge and build their
competencies on GAD-related laws and GAD-related tools, among others;
and
d. prepare the implementing rules and regulations of the GAD Code of the
Municipality to effectively implement the provisions contained therein.

We recommended that the MT (i) trace and identify the discrepancies and immediately effect corrections in the cashbook; (ii) review or double-check the recording and computation of amounts and balances in the cashbooks before certifying the balances; (iii) regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the bank statements and Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) at least every end of the month or as the need arises.
We further recommended that the MT take advantage of the technology for easier and efficient recording of transactions by using software applications like Microsoft Excel or other similar applications that performs data storing and calculations as support tool in recording cash transactions. Adoption of such applications will not only help to calculate accurately the balances and minimize, if not totally eliminate, errors before manually recording cash transactions in the official cashbook but will also serve as back up in storing data and most importantly, a convenient tool to assist in recording and reviewing records, analyzing data and identifying errors particularly during reconciliation.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) develop accounting estimate for impairment of receivables based on the management’s knowledge and experience on the settlement of its receivables taking into account the age and collectability of the balances, identified bad accounts eligible for write-off and other historical data such as settlement/liquidation and collection experience; (ii) assess at the end of each year or each reporting date whether there is evidence that the receivable accounts are impaired; (iii) support the assessment, if possible, with evidence enumerated in paragraph 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; (iv) calculate the allowance for impairment in accordance with the International Public Sector Accounting Standards and recognize the computed allowance in the books to reflect the net realizable value of the receivable accounts in the Financial Statements; and (v) submit to the Local Chief Executive the recommended list of eligible accounts for write-off; and
Local Chief Executive (i) file a request for write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005, if warranted, but continue to exert effort to collect the written-off accounts; and (ii) develop policies and guidelines outlining the methods and strategies to improve the collection and settlement of past due accounts and thereafter ensure strict adherence thereto to cleanse the books for the fair presentation of receivable accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRMO:
prepare the LDRRMFIP, apart from the AIP, in accordance with the reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF of Local Government Units as required under Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 12 (c) (7) of the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction Management Act of 2010 or Republic Act (RA) No. 10121;
ensure timely preparation of LDRRMFIP for the succeeding years and furnish a copy thereof to the AT; and
ensure that all department heads are well informed of all the policies, procedures and rules and regulations by regularly furnishing them with complete and updated copies of circulars issued by the national government oversight agencies and constitutional commissions applicable to their respective offices in fulfilling their duties and responsibilities and make sure that the provisions therein are properly carried out.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with Section 5 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and (ii) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits of the intended beneficiaries;
Municipal Engineer (i) require concerned project engineer, officers and employees to be prudent in overseeing the timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility; (ii) assist the MPDC in identifying which among those not yet completed nor started projects could be considered for possible realignment or reprogramming; and (iii) provide satisfactory explanation on why the projects were not yet completed or implemented; and
MPDC (i) ensure close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for proper monitoring of the implementation of the projects funded by the 20% DF; and (ii) conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine if the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise reprogram the identified PPAs to other priority PPAs; and (iii) continue to identify projects that are doable and beneficial to the constituents.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Engineer or Department Heads concerned, ensure that all requirements, documentations, clearances, permits and licenses necessary for the smooth implementation of a project are complied with before the commencement of work/construction; and
Bids and Awards Committee (i) ensure that the complete scope of the project and relevant aspect of the contract such as terms of payment is clearly and completely disclosed in the bid documents; and (ii) discuss during pre-bid conference any additional conditions, scope, terms and information that may affect the usual implementation of a project so that both parties are made aware of every conditions particularly on changes that were made out of the ordinary practice in executing a contract.
Further, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause investigation of the deficiencies incurred and issue warning or, if necessary, impose disciplinary action to the responsible officials if it was determined that the delay or deficiencies were due to their negligence which will also set the precedent in regard to neglect of duty by any officials causing detrimental effect on the implementation of MGR’s PPAs.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) classify the nature of expenditures incurred and record the same on the appropriate account to reflect the correct account and balances in the Financial Statement; and (ii) record under PS only the salaries, wages and benefits of government officials and employees. Wages of other personnel with no employer-employee relationship shall be recorded under MOOE; and
HRMO assess the organizational structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality and make recommendation to the Local Chief Executive whether there is need to create additional plantillla position or simply employ casual/contractual employees who will perform regular and administrative or clerical functions to prevent conflicts with existing rules on the proper hiring of personnel and assignment of work as well as in providing appropriations therefor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) trace the discrepancies and prepare the necessary DV to  remit the accounted amount or prepare correcting entries if identified differences were due to errors or deficiencies in recording; (ii) support the SL sub-accounts with list of names of contractor and supplier for final tax withheld and names of employees for the compensation tax and other salary deductions with the corresponding amount withheld to be compared to the supporting list provided in the disbursement vouchers for remittances; and (iii) in coordination with the MT, ensure that amounts due to the BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth are remitted in full on time to avoid possible incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges.

We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive
Create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members and representatives from the Accounting and Property Division/Unit;
The Inventory Committee
Conduct a complete physical count of PPE based on the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
Prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and reconcile the same with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records.
Property Unit/GSO
Prepare and maintain Property Cards for all items of PPE;
Prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) using the prescribed format for all PPE found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed; and
Work together with the Office of the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PCs with the latter’s PPELCs.
Accounting Unit/Office of the Municipal Accountant
a.  Prepare schedules of all classes of PPE and submit together with the financial statements;
Prepare and update PPELCs for all classes of PPE;
Take up necessary adjusting entries based on the results of the physical count; and
Work together with the GSO to reconcile the PPELCs with the latter’s PCs.

5.11	We recommended Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1.	Exert diligent efforts to trace the whereabouts/location/address of accountable officers/debtors and update existing records by issuing demand letters to all accountable officials/debtors with outstanding receivables; and
2.	If conditions are met, expedite the filing of request for authority to write-off the remaining dormant receivable accounts after complying with the documentary requirements to support the request provided in COA Circular 2016–005.

7.8	We recommended that Management to Direct the coordination of Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer to:
1.	Specify the details of the currently identified projects, programs and activities charged against the 20% EDF indicating the project location, specific implementation schedule and physical target.
2.	Initiate the preparation of Program of Works to support the estimate of projects funded under the 20% Economic Development Plan before budget preparation; and
3.	Develop and recommend other procedures to enhance the current practice regarding planning and budgeting of the 20% Economic Development Fund to avoid generic and lump-sum projects.

8.3	We recommended management to:
a.	Require the LDRRMO to submit the LDRRMFIP based on the format in Annex A; and
b.	Submit monthly and annually the Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

10.7	We recommended that Management:
a. 	Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and
b.  	Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted therein.
11.4	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to exert effort, that checks are released to payee for its immediate submission to COA.

13.3	We recommended that management thru the LCE to Create and establish a Monitoring and Evaluation Team in in consonance with the provisions of item five of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to implement the GAD PPAs efficiently and effectively.

14.8	We recommended that management to:
1.	Coordinate with the BFP, re-conduct evaluation and inspection on the municipal buildings’ fire safety standards and implement fire preventive measures to avoid the risk of losing lives and properties in cases of man-made and/or natural fire calamities;
2.	Consider acquisition of appropriate fire truck for industrial fire hazard; and
3.	Provide the necessary and applicable firefighting equipment/materials installed on conspicuous places, and post as well an emergency evacuation plan where anyone can likewise easily access.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to prepare the PIP in coordination with
the CGSO and CAO, and submit the PIP approved by the City Mayor to the
Audit Team at least 10 CD before the scheduled start of inventory taking
activities;

b. Require the Inventory Committee to timely plan the conduct of thorough
and complete physical inventory count of all PPEs in accordance with the
approved PIP, and prepare and submit the properly accomplished RPCPPE
to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to
Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise,
assistance by other offices be extended to CGSO in the conduct of
inventory;

c. Cause the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the property records and the
accounting records, determine the respective costs of those properties to
which none have been assigned in the RPCPPE;

d. Prepare IIRUP for the damaged/ unserviceable PPE and dispose of these in
accordance with applicable rules and regulations on Supply and Property
Management. Then, drop the same from the books of the City; and

e. Require the City Accountant and City GSO to prepare and maintain the LCs
and PCs, respectively, to facilitate the required regular reconciliation of the
PPE accounts as required under Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:

a. Project Managers/Engineers and contractors should be instructed that the
installed signboards be provided with relevant data and updated regularly
so as to provide the necessary information as to the status of each
project’s implementation; and henceforth,

b. The City Government should comply with all the transparency
requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA
Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Require the City Accountant to draw journal entry vouchers to record all
valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL to
establish the accurate balances of CIB-LCCA and other related accounts,
following the prescribed accounting entry:

a.1 For Unrecorded Deposit:
CIB-LCCA XXX
Income/Payable Account XXX

a.2 For Unrecorded Service Charge:
Expense Account XXX
CIB-LCCA XXX

a.3 For Erroneous Entry of Bank Interest:
Income Account XXX
CIB-LCCA XXX

a.4 For Stale Check:

a.4.1 Check Cancellation
CIB-LCCA XXX
Payable Account XXX

a.4.2 Check Replacement
Payable Account XXX
CIB-LCCA XXX
b. Require the OIC-City Treasurer to send written notices to payees at least
one month before payment checks become stale pursuant to Section 59 of
the Manual on the NGAS for LGUS, Volume I; and

c. Require the City Accountant to review the outstanding checks that are
considered stale totaling ₱2.320 million as of December 31, 2021, for proper
treatment and undertake the necessary accounting procedures in the
recording of stale checks as provided in Section 59 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 97 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the City Accountant
to:

a. Demand the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials and
employees of the cash advances totaling ₱1.900 million and refrain from
granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with
unsettled accounts; and

b. Cause the withholding of salaries of concerned officials and employees
who are still active in service, effective immediately, in settlement of their
unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱1.508 million granted in prior years.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Require the AO to prepare Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) for the
unaccounted cash tickets totaling 2,226 pcs. / ₱11,130.00 which are
deemed issued, and remit the said amount to the City Treasurer Office;

b. Direct the AO to stop the issuance of the cash tickets to collectors with no
fidelity bond to safeguard the government’s interest; and

c. Cashbook should properly be maintained and updated daily by the AO, and
to be recorded with the accountability of unissued accountable forms with
money value, which shall be permanent record book containing all
information to be kept for control purposes.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require/direct the City
Accountant to:

a. Initiate the write-off of long dormant accounts and undertake the specific
guidelines and procedures as provided in Sections 6, 7 and 8 of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;

b. Conduct periodic monitoring and analysis of the dormant accounts for
issuance of demand letter and initiate write-off if the borrowers are
insolvent, could no longer be located, or dead;

c. Recognize/reclassify grants that were previously recorded as loans
receivable-others;

d. Ensure that the dormant receivables were securely filed/ maintained to
validate/support the claim; and

e. Disclose appropriately on the Notes to Financial Statements, the pending
application/approval of request for write-off.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Direct the City Accountant to institute a sound Perpetual Inventory System
pursuant to the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to account for additions to and
subtractions from Inventory;

b. Require the City Accountant to record inventories upon receipt of procured
supplies and materials, recognize the corresponding expenses only upon
consumption or distribution, maintain subsidiary ledger cards and properly
classify the asset and expense accounts;

c. Direct the CGSO to require the CHO to submit monthly RIS as basis of
RSMI to establish the cost of sale or cost of service and to ensure proper
accounting of the sold/consumed inventories of drugs and medicines and
medical, dental and laboratory supplies;

d. Require the Supply Officer to update and reconcile the inventory records
and stock cards at month end to ascertain the value or cost of the
inventory.

e. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of drugs
and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies semestrally and
reconcile its records periodically with the accounting records;

f. Direct the CGSO to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) by type of inventory to determine the cost of inventory items and to
restore the correct inventory account balances as of December 31, 2021
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and

g. Establishment of warehouse facility for the procured inventories.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Direct the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO) to set the
criteria and procedures for proper identification of the program’s targeted
recipients;

b. Properly man the distribution of welfare goods, and resolve the identified
problem regarding manpower to see to it that the program distributes its
deliverables on time;

c. Come up with a set of plans and guidelines to warrant the program’s
efficient and effective implementation;

d. Procure only the needed welfare goods to avert possible wastage and
storage problem, and to ensure the quality of the rice being distributed;

e. Keep a detailed record of the stock in and out of rice to ensure proper
recording, custody and accountability of rice bags purchased and
distributed; and

f. Henceforth, the City Government, in all its future programs, should ensure
equal and fair coverage for all its constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Enhance closer monitoring activities on fund transfer and direct the Focal
Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately
without delay funded PPAs of all on-going/not started PPAs to attain the
program’s objectives;

b. Require the City Accountant to review and analyze the Due to NGAs
account for Trust Fund and cause the immediate remittance/return of any
unexpended/unutilized amount of fund transfer to the concerned NGAs
after project completion pursuant to the aforementioned Circular; and

c. Secure prior authority from the Secretary of Public Works and Highways on
the implementation by administration of PPAs costing over ₱5.000 million
up to ₱20.000 million, otherwise, PPAs are to be undertaken through
straight contract.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Ensure that all identified and prioritized development PPAs funded by 20%
DF are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Likewise,
expedite execution of these to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of
the same;

b. Review unimplemented PPAs in CYs 2009-2020 and ascertain the viability
of implementation for the ensuing year, otherwise, re-appropriate or
reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs to better address the
constantly evolving development needs of the targeted beneficiaries;

c. Revert the unexpended balances of the 20% DF to the unappropriated
surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year, except those
balances referring to continuing appropriations items;

d. Provision of warehouse facility for the procured construction materials;

e. Direct the supplier concerned to immediately deliver the unused
construction materials for the ongoing PPAs covered by Notice of
Suspension to the GSO for proper accounting, custody, and safekeeping
considering that these had been duly paid. Otherwise, refund the amount
paid;

f. Require the City Accountant to transfer the completed PPA from the
Construction in Progress account to the appropriate Property, Plant and
Equipment account and provide depreciation. Further, require the Project
Engineer to submit a written justification why the project was 100%
completed when there was no obligated amount or actual disbursement for
the labor component;

g. Closely monitor the implementation of PPAs to ensure that the interest of
the LGU and the government, in general, are safeguarded, expected
deliverables are submitted timely and payments are made only after
satisfactory acceptance of deliverables by an authorized person, and
compliance is made with payment conditions;

h. Submit the original invoices reflecting the plate number, date, and time of
delivery, and stating the actual volume of the delivery of the paid sands,
gravels, and aggregates; and

i. Secure prior authority from the Secretary of Public Works and Highways on
the implementation by administration of PPAs costing over ₱5.000 million
up to ₱20.000 million, otherwise, PPAs are to be undertaken through
straight contract.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly adhere to the
provisions of Section 4.0 of the NEDA/DBM/DILG JMC 2013-1 with respect to
the allocation of the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly observe the
limitation provided by Section 21, Paragraph 3 of R.A. 10121.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the LDRRMC and
LDRRMO to:

a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the
identified and prioritized development Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs)
funded under the Calamity Fund so that problems causing a delay in the
project implementation would be timely addressed; and

b. Study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, and as deemed necessary, revise and
update to cope up with the constraints brought by the present condition.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the GAD Office and
Focal Point Person:

a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the
identified and prioritized development PPAs funded under the GAD
Program and any cause of delay of prevailing problems be addressed to
foster timely project implementation; and

b. Regularly assess the implementability of its PPAs during the year, so that,
if deemed necessary, realign to other priority implementable projects that
would address more prevalent gender issues and could have an impact on
the intended beneficiaries or address the various needs of the stakeholders
and intended beneficiaries in times of crises.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. To protect its property and interests, the City of Roxas would execute a
Contract of Lease with its tenants with specific provisions for collection,
damage reparation, penalties and interest; and

b. To institute methods and strategies to improve its collection, especially
concerning the tenants of RCITT, Paseo de Roxas, and Libas Fishing Port.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct OIC-Municipal Accountant to reclassify all completed projects to its appropriate Asset accounts, specifically for Market as described as follows:
Code Account Title Description
1-07/04/040 Markets Debit this account is used to record the cost
incurred in the purchase of
construction or fair
value, if acquired through donation or transfers
without cost, of markets for income
generation.
Credit this accounts for derecognition of the
carrying amount of the replaced portion in
major repairs, or disposal.
1-07-10-030 Construction in Debit this account to record the accumulated
Progress-Building cost or other appropriate value of
and Other value of infrastructure assets which are still in the
Structures process of construction or
development.
Credit this account for reclassification to the
appropriate buildings and Other Structures
account upon completion.
b. Recognized depreciation expenses based on the estimated useful life of an asset as provided in Table A of COA Circular No. 2003-007 dated December 11, 2003, effective a month after its date of completion.

We recommended and the management agreed to stop/desist Construction of Infrastructure Projects or any structures or any related/similar activities on the questioned lot where the Municipal Public Market is located until a final decision from the Court is rendered.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Supply Officer to fast track the documentation and titling of donated properties of the Municipality to protect its LGU’s interest from third Party claims; and
b. Come up with budget for titling, survey, relocation and legal expenses and make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the appropriation of funds necessary to pursue the claim and transfer of Torrens Title in its name of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer agreed to strictly monitor the collectors’ turnover, cause the remittance of their collections intact daily and strictly adhere with the provision of Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS Vol. I to lessen the risk of misuse and loss of fund.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Supply Officer to maintain a record of repair and maintenance history of each vehicle owned by the Municipality to track down the frequency of repair and to avoid recurring expenditures;
b. OIC - Municipal Accountant to attach the history/record of repair to the disbursement vouchers as additional supporting documents for repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles; and
c. Appraisal Committee to assess the remaining life of the vehicle and determine if such vehicle is beyond repair or not to avoid unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended and the management agreed to stop charging to LDRRMF expenditures that are not valid/allowable expenses to LDRRMF and submit written justification on the purpose why Administrative expenditure was charged to LDRRMF instead of Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the General Fund.
Henceforth, direct the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to include only activities and valid expenses that adhere to guidelines in the preparation and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommend and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to furnished the Municpal Accountant, the documents necessary to record the reciept and distribution of vaccines Inventory Reports or its equivalent in relation to the vaccination program; and
b. Direct the Municpal Accountant to record using the perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the LGU to DOH-CHD and draw journal entry voucher for the applicable inventory accounts to record the remaining vaccines in stock at December 31, 2021.

We recommended to the Management that the Municipal Accounting Office together with the General Services Office refer to the Office records in reporting the properties. Also, the Management adhere to the procedures in the physical count of derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee, together with the Office of the Municipal General Services, both agreed to conduct a complete physical count of all properties of this municipality to ensure that records between the GSO and Accounting will be reconciled. Moreover, the committee and the concerned offices assured to continue to work hand in hand to fully or to lessen these unreconciled balances as per record between the GSO and Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts for their proper disposition and make adjustments in the books to attain accuracy in the presentation of the financial statements. Moreover, prepare the necessary documents pertaining to the dormant accounts and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant will exert continuous effort to analyze those balances and will prepare the necessary documents and will submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: The office concerned is still retrieving those documents related to those balances especially those with material balances. Moreover, mostly of the debtors can no longer be traced and are no longer connected to the Local Government Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a refund to the source agency and close the bank account with dormant deposit.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to properly withhold the contractor the amount of tax due to the contractor in the projects implemented by the LGU in compliance with the tax laws in the Philippines. b. The Local Finance Committee, moving forward, create a well-defined budget especially in supporting the special projects that the LGU endeavor to implement to help in achieving efficiency and effectiveness in the allocation of resources of the Municipality. c. The Local Government Unit, through the Municipal Engineering Office, institute remedial action to comply with the required number of furniture and equipment to be used for the project.
observations.managementAction: a. The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to properly withhold the contractor the amount of tax due to the contractor in the projects implemented by the LGU in compliance with the tax laws in the Philippines. b. The Local Finance Committee, moving forward, create a well-defined budget especially in supporting the special projects that the LGU endeavor to implement to help in achieving efficiency and effectiveness in the allocation of resources of the Municipality. c. The Local Government Unit, through the Municipal Engineering Office, institute remedial action to comply with the required number of furniture and equipment to be used for the project.
observations.reason: The office already send a letter to the contractor to inform and demand payment of the unwithheld tax. However, as of date, no payment has been made. The Local Finance Committee created well-defined budget. The Crisis Center was used as isolation facility or quarantine area for COVID infected individuals. To avoid risk exposure to the area and to follow the protocol, it would be safe if the installation will be done after the area will be decontaminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management the following: a. Submit a valid justification on the disbursements without an appropriation ordinance; and b. Ensure that all disbursements are made in pursuance of an appropriation ordinance or law as mandated in R.A. No. 7160 and P.D. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The LCE should direct the Municipal Accountant to: i. Coordinate with other concerned department heads and determine the status of implementation of various fund transfers and effect their disposition thereof. ii. Reconcile the balances of fund transfers with the source agencies and return the excess or unutilized balances to concerned source agencies. iii. Determine the composition of unreconciled accounts and unsubstantiated balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books to attain fair representation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will retrieve those documents related to those balances and reconcile the accounts. The Office of the Municipal Accountant is on the process of retrieving those documents to support the reconciliation of the unreconciled accounts and unsubstantiated balances.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant is on the process of retrieving those documents to support the reconciliation of the unreconciled accounts and unsubstantiated balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accountant will issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulations; b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; and c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will ensure that elected officials are not granted with cash advances other than those expressly granted by the existing laws, rules and regulations and only permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as disbursing officers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to review the collectability of this account to ensure its net realizability. If such receivable will be proven to be deemed uncollectible after exerting all effort and means to collect, the management plans to submit a request for write-off to COA pursuant to the existing rules and regulations. Moreover, they ensured to implement the withholding of salaries of those currently employed accountable officers and to strictly observe the proper granting and liquidation of cash advances. The Office of the Municipal Accountant is sending notice in the form of demand letter in a quarterly basis to enforce the liquidation of cash advances. Salary deduction will be implemented after the issuance of final demand in accordance with the regulation. The Office continues to exert effort to enforce the liquidation of Accountable Officers. Nevertheless, some of the Accountable Officers are no longer connected with the LGU.
observations.reason: Some of the Accountable Officers are no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the physical count of inventories. Likewise, the Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor. b. The Bids and Awards Committee should fast track the procurement of the rescue response equipment. c. The LDRRMFIP should include the lists of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and ensure proper recording and reporting. d. Ensure that medicines, goods and other supplies procured out of LDRRMF are properly recorded.
observations.managementAction: The Management acknowledged the audit observation and would take the necessary actions to improve the implementation and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund. The LDRRMO, MPDO, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer were already instructed to fully implement the PPAs under LDRRMF e.g. the 70% mitigation fund for DRRM preparedness and the 30% QRF for Covid-related emergencies so as not to defeat its purpose but rather, to properly, efficiently and effectively utilize said fund to enhance the LGU’s capability in responding to calamities and disasters.
observations.reason: As of date, the management is still awaiting approval from the DILG Central Office for certain procurements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated prior year’s recommendation for the inventory committee to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and immediately reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.reason: Still on the process for titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/ employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand or stop the granting of additional cash advance. Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for General Fund and Trust Fund on the Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account, in order to present fairly the balance of the affected account in the financial statements for General Fund and Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Management advise:
The Municipal Accountant (1) disclose in the CY 2021 Notes to Financial Statements the details on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF of the Municipality using the format as required under Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002; (2) transfer the Municipality’s unutilized balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2021 in the Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books under the account Trust Liability- DRRM; and (3) reconcile the unutilized balance of DRRMF between the report on the receipt and utilization of DRRMF amounting to ₱5,143,861.44 and the unspent balance of DRRM- Trust Fund amounting to ₱4,060,557.52 as per disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement; and
The Municipal Budget Officer maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Quick Response Fund (QRF).

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs on the fund transfers/ financial assistance received from NIA, PCSO/STL and PAGCOR, in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Determine those receivables where collection is no longer possible and request authority to write-off therefor from COA, duly supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005;
Advise the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in gathering data/details to substantiate the amount deposited under LBP bank account- LGU Roxas with account number 851-088-288 to properly utilize the amount deposited thereon; and
Ensure that all obligations are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 41 of EO No. 292 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant:
draw the following journal entries on the succeeding payment of fifteen percent (15%) mobilization to the contractors of LGU Roxas:
Dr.	Advances to Contractors				xx
Cr.	Cash in Bank- Local Currency Current Account 	xx
properly recognize and record in the books on the recoupment of the advances to contractor; and
record in the books of accounts for Trust Fund all private and government monies which have officially come into the possession of the local government to be used for specific purpose.

We commended the collective efforts of the Municipal officials and employees on the effective and efficient disbursement of COVID19 funds. Likewise, we recommended that the Management continue their control mechanism towards the spread of COVID19 virus.
We recommended that Management strictly utilize/implement the 20% Development Fund PPAs on time to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management submit the Appropriation Ordinance on the augmentation of savings from the 20% DF amounting to ₱100,000.00 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance. We further recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate funds exclusively for which they have been appropriated; and if there are savings to be augmented, an Appropriation Ordinance must be passed prior to the obligation of funds.

We recommended that the Management submit a justification/explanation on the disbursement of funds without appropriation. We further recommended that government funds must be disbursed exclusively for which they have been appropriated unless an ordinance had been passed for their realignment.

We recommended that the Management justify/explain the payment of incentives to the drivers of the Provincial Government of Isabela. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Planning and Developmental Council, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, should strictly observe that the appropriation of 20% Development Fund be supported with identified, detailed and specific developmental programs and projects in the Annual Investment Program. We further recommended that for CY 2022, program of works should be prepared for each developmental project to support the expenses charged thereon.

We recommended that the Management instruct the LDRRMO prepare immediately the LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 and include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and submit it immediately to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, ensure timely preparation and submission of LDRRMFIP for the succeeding years and furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy thereof.

We recommended that the Management:
explain / justify the payment of the said incentive and submit the legal basis authorizing the payment thereof to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance; and
stop the practice of charging expenses to LDRRMF which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities.

We recommended that the Management:
Explain / justify the delayed submission of the contracts and its supporting documents of the thirty-eight (38) projects listed above to the Audit Team; and
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit and ensure that contracts and purchase orders/job orders irrespective of amount be properly accomplished and submitted to COA within five (5) working days from execution/approval, together with the complete supporting documents, pursuant to Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Management submit immediately to the Auditor’s Office the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other supporting documents for the month of December. Likewise, explain/justify the late submission of the said reports. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary reports on timely basis and observe provisions under COA Circular No. 2009-006 and P.D. 1445 in order to avoid sanctions set by the provision.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of the following calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 6(b) of RA 8291 and Rule VII Section 3 of IRR of RA 9679.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Management establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. The mode of procurement must be clearly indicated in the APP for strict compliance. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We appreciated the effort of the Municipality to collect the unliquidated cash advances of the concerned individuals, however, we recommended that the Management fast tract the collections to avoid any legal actions that may arise due to non-liquidation of the above-mentioned cash advances. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the account Advances to Officers and Employees in recording the grant of cash advances for travelling purposes.

We recommended that the: MDRRM Council, through the MDRRMO, comment on the observations noted on the budgeting process of the DRRM fund and provide for the lacking budgetary documents, if any, for audit purposes; Municipal Accountant prepare the year-end trial balance and financial statement for the SAGF-BGCM in compliance with the rules and regulation set under Section 107 of Chapter 6, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for fair presentation, transparency and accountability of the funds utilized; Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer and other personnel concerned strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 14, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 in the utilization of cash and in-kind donations received by the Municipality; Concerned employees of the Mayor’s Office submit to the Audit Team the acknowledgement receipts of in-kind donations received and proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; Concerned employees of the Mayor’s Office, as the implementing office for the relief operation, and other personnel concerned submit the complete and original copies of distribution lists accounting the procured and distributed relief goods during the pandemic crisis; and Concerned Municipal Officials (i) comment on the procurement process undertaken by the Municipality during the COVID-19 situation; (ii) submit the lacking documents for audit purposes; and (iii) ensure that future claims are supported with complete supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the SAGF BGCM financial report to the Audit Team. The BAC submitted some of the lacking documents. The Mayor submitted justification and clarification on the implementation of Bayanihan grants.
observations.reason: Difficulty to collate the lacking supporting documents due to voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO together with the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking pictures and SAC to substantiate the complete liquidation of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a soft copy of the pictures taken. The SAC forms were forwarded to DSWD. However, when returned to MSWDO, some of the SAC forms were incomplete.
observations.reason: The concerned employees were still in the process of collating the lacking SAC forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per general ledger and cashbooks and provide justification on the cash deficit incurred in Trust Fund totaling ₱18,238,737.85; and Municipal Accountant exert effort to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements for all bank accounts, and maintain subsidiary ledgers for all bank accounts and regularly reconcile the amounts with their respective control account.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant prepared BRS for the 20% Development Fund account.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was hampered due to voluminous transactions in the office. The concerned employee is loaded with responsibilities and lack of sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) recognize the discounts for prompt and advance payments of RPT and SET and penalties, if any, in the books of accounts of the Municipality in the period to which they accrue, (b) reconcile the RPT and SET Receivables with the record of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the accounts RPT, RPT Receivable, Deferred RPT Income, SET, SET Receivable and Deferred SET Income, and make appropriate adjustments, if warranted, (c) ensure that collection of SET be accrued to SEF books on the period they were earned instead of recording in the period the cash was actually transferred, and (d) adhere to the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the proper recording of the set-up and collections of receivables from RPT and SET and recognition of income in the respective books of the General and Special Education Funds.
observations.managementAction: The discount on the RPT was recognized in the books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant was still in the process of reconciling the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was hampered due to voluminous transactions in the office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: GSO (i) conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the property, plant and equipment to establish the existence, ownership and condition of the properties owned by the Municipality especially for the local road network to determine the current value of the road components and facilitate the preparation of the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and ensure that the required details in the reports were accurately and completely filled out; Municipal Accountant and GSO allot time and effort to (i) coordinate and prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts, especially the previous year’s PPE acquisitions, (ii) prepare and maintain property records and reports for all PPE accounts especially for the Road Networks, and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to complete the maintenance of a road map with a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; GSO prepare and maintain complete Property Cards and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components and the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to account for the Local Roads and reconcile with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office, and collate the documents of the land owned by the Municipal Government that remained uncovered by a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT); Municipal Accountant (i) keep and maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE and subsidiary records for roads and components for every road network, (ii) ensure proper valuation/measurement of transactions in accordance with the IPSAS and classification of accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 and (iii) review the entries made relevant to the above transactions and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted; Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and Appraisal and Disposal Committee account all determined unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of required supporting documents for disposal.
observations.managementAction: GSO (i) conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the property, plant and equipment to establish the existence, ownership and condition of the properties owned by the Municipality especially for the local road network to determine the current value of the road components and facilitate the preparation of the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and ensure that the required details in the reports were accurately and completely filled out; Municipal Accountant and GSO allot time and effort to (i) coordinate and prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts, especially the previous year’s PPE acquisitions, (ii) prepare and maintain property records and reports for all PPE accounts especially for the Road Networks, and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to complete the maintenance of a road map with a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and time to facilitate the other audit recommendation on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer or other concerned offices for the retrieval of records of all outstanding accounts payable; (ii) maintain appropriate subsidiary records pursuant to Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II and; (iii) ensure that the entries in the Accounts Payable accounts were made in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared subsidiary ledgers and lapsing schedules for payable accounts for current year.
observations.reason: Lack of documents and records to substantiate prior years’ balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make representation with the bank for the reconciliation of the record of the Loans payable under Term Loan 6-Construction of the third floor of Municipal Building (Legislative and Executive Offices) – Phase II and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipal Government’s accounting books, if warranted; and Municipal Treasurer provide immediately and regularly the copy of the debit memoranda and/or snapshot from the bank to the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording the payment of loan amortization. Moreover, consider utilization of online banking features in monitoring the transactions and movement of fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested certification from Landbank of full payment of Term Loan 6 - Construction of the third floor of Municipal Building (Legislative and Executive Offices) – Phase II and make the necessary adjustments in the books as warranted; and made follow-up for the debit memoranda/snapshot.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous transactions in the Accounting Office, tracing of prior years’ transactions was hampered. Further, some of the records from prior years was not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exert continuous effort to collate the information/details on the previous years’ Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs and Due to Other Funds account by coordinating with the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned personnel to facilitate the preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers pursuant to Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; (ii) conduct a thorough review and analysis of the Due to LGUs account under TF and reclassify to its proper account the funds that were not sourced from the LGUs, if warranted, pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; (iii) review and analyze the details of transactions regarding the negative balance in the Due to Other Funds account to identify the cause of the negative balance and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted; and (iv) reconcile the Due to/from accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all the identified differences are resolved and/or adjusted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started preparing ledger for Due to LGUs account for current year and exerted extra effort to collate the information/details on the previous years’ Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs and Due to Other Funds account by coordinating with the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned personnel to facilitate the preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Due to lack of turn-over of prior years records and voluminous transactions in the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant review the entries made under the OMOE account and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted, and ensure that the expenditures of the Municipality are properly classified for fair presentation in the financial statement, in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Moreover, ensure proper monitoring of charges under the account which should include maintenance of complete details and records of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that they will classify and record expenditures based on the prescribed rules and regulations. The Municipal Budget Officer to review the budget for the succeeding years covering the lump sum appropriations of OMOE and make revision of the budget in accordance with the results of the review following the account description mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous transactions in the Municipal Accounting Office, thorough review of the accounts could not be done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (i) require the concerned officers and employees to liquidate the CAs as soon as the purpose for which it was granted has been served in compliance with Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) strictly monitor accountable officers with outstanding cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to an employee prior to settlement of his/her previous cash advances; and (iii) ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the complete documentation of liquidation of CAs and reimbursement of traveling expenses; and Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure that the liquidation of the accountable officers is within the reglementary period in compliance with the prescribed guidelines under Sections 5.1.1 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer conduct regular reconciliation of cash balances and cash advances. The balance of advances to its employees were collected from their salaries. The livelihood program was discontinued. The Municipal Accountant ensured that they will classify and record expenditures based on the prescribed rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Lack of staff from the Municipal Accounting Office and MTO to reconcile the accounts. Reconciliation of records between the offices is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: (a) BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat to comment on the (i) lapses in the invitation to observers; (ii) incomplete data on posting of advertisement requirements; (iii) undated bid evaluation report; and (iv) delayed posting of NOA and NTP in PhilGEPS; (b) Municipal Accountant and the concerned municipal officials (i) provide explanation on the noted non-compliance to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-088 and LBC No. 134; (ii) provide justification on the noted deficiencies in the supporting documents; and (iii) submit the lacking documents for audit purposes; and (c) BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and its RIRR within which certain stages of the procurement process must be completed and adopt the latest procurement process and forms prescribed by the GPPB in conformity with the requirements of Section 6 of the RIRR on standardization of government procurement.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with responsibilities and lack of sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat to comment on the (i) absence of document evidencing the conduct of pre-procurement conference; (ii) absence of Certification of BAC Secretariat in the posting of ITB in any conspicuous place in the premises of the LGU; (iii) undated bid evaluation report; (iv) absence of Notice to Losing Bidders; (v) delayed posting of the NOA in PhilGEPS and non-posting in conspicuous place; (vi) delayed contract signing and undated Performance Security; and (vii) lacking documents contrary to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. Henceforth, ensure that documents are properly accomplished and dated and abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 particularly the prescribed period of compliance for future transactions; and BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the concerned municipal officials submit the lacking documents for audit purposes and ensure that future claims are supported with complete supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. Moreover, ensure compliance to the provisions of relevant rules and regulations regarding the implementation of projects funded by NGAs.
observations.managementAction: BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the concerned municipal officials submitted some of the lacking documents for audit purposes and provided justifications.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with responsibilities and lack of sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (i) plan the procurement and implementation of the identified priority development projects under the 20% DF to ensure the completion of the PPAs; (ii) coordinate with the beneficiaries/concerned offices with PPAs that will be included in the 20% DF AIP to determine the specific works to be done in each PPAs and to avoid delays in the implementation; and (iii) evaluate the feasibility of the particular PPAs and observe timeliness in their implementation to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; Municipal Engineer submit the list of completed projects to the MBO and MAccO to facilitate the reversion and re-appropriation of savings/unexpended balances from completed projects under the 20% DF to eligible development projects, and follow strictly the implementation period as indicated in the AIP/PPMP; and Municipal Engineer and the BAC provide periodically the MBO and the MAccO with the status of projects and the procurements undertaken by the Municipality to assist the concerned offices in the periodic review and monitoring of the balances of current and continuing appropriations and inform immediately the MBO and MAccO whenever there are changes on the awarded PPAs and problems in the project’s implementation to make the necessary adjustment in the financial reports relative to the affected PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning Development Coordinator were instructed to coordinate with each other. Municipal Engineer submitted the list of completed projects to the MBO and MAccO to facilitate the reversion and re-appropriation of savings/unexpended balances from completed projects under the 20% DF to eligible development projects.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with responsibilities and lack of sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: MDRRM Officer (i) monitor and facilitate the implementation of the PPAs under the LDRRMF and ensure that all projects appropriated in LDRRMF be implemented during the year and optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the Municipality to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters; and (ii) evaluate the PPAs in the continuing and special trust fund and make appropriate actions thereto by reprogramming to another MDRRM PPAs that can help in reducing exposure to the potential impact of hazards and related disasters in the Municipality; MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund; Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer provide justification on the observations discrepancies noted on the amounts of the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF in the trial balance and RSULDRRMF, and ensure that the amount reflected in the reports represents the accurate data of MDRRM Fund; and Municipal Accountant review the entries made relative to the DRRM funds received from other sources and effect necessary adjustments, if warranted, and ensure that the DRRM funds for disaster relief and management purposes received by the Municipality from other sources be classified under the special trust liability account.
observations.managementAction: MDRRM Officer submitted revised LDRRMFIP. Municipal Accountant prepared general ledger and subsidiary ledger for the Trust Liability – DRRMF account and submitted justification on the observations.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with responsibilities and lack of sufficient time to comply with other Audit Team’s recommendations. Reconciliation and reclassification of misclassified accounts were still ongoing.
