observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the required monthly financial reports on timely basis and strictly comply with COA Circular No 2009-006 and Section 122 of PD 1445 so as not to violate its provisions with corresponding sanctions set therein.
observations.managementAction: The management submits the monthly disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other supporting documents on time, however, for the month of December 2021, they failed to submit the documents on time.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 16

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, PAGIBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of each calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 6(b) of RA 8291 and Rule VII Section 3 of IRR of RA 9679.
observations.managementAction: The Management already reconciled the Due to BIR account, however, the Due to GSIS, PHIC and Pag-IBIG were still unreconciled. The current year remittances were remitted in full. The unreconciled differences were attributed to prior years.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 17

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the GAD Focal Person fast track the establishment of GAD sex-disaggregated database to support the gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.
observations.managementAction: The Management already have their GAD database for submission to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management through the Municipal Treasurer impost and collect the proper tax in the issuance of Community Tax Certificate (CTC) in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 as well as input all the necessary information in the CTC, otherwise, she will be held liable for the under assessments and collections through our issuance of Notice of Charge (NC). Likewise, educate the Barangay Treasurers, being the Deputized Collectors of CTC, in the computation and collection of tax.
observations.managementAction: The Management already followed the correct assessment of Community Tax Certificate.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management install sound internal control to those businesses operating without securing business permit through an ordinance as well as impose strictly the interest and penalties to those businesses who filed their business permits beyond the prescribed period set by the Revenue Code so as to increase the revenue collection of the Municipality that can be used for programs/projects and activities for the benefits of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management already installed internal control over granting of business operating permits and licenses.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the required monthly financial reports on timely basis and strictly comply with COA Circular No 2009-006 and Section 122 of PD 1445 so as not to violate its provisions with corresponding sanctions set therein.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the required monthly financial reports to the Office of the Auditor, however, not within the prescribed period set by the regulation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. The mode of procurement must be clearly indicated in the APP for strict compliance. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: No management actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the GAD Focal Person fast track the establishment of GAD sex-disaggregated database to support the gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.
observations.managementAction: The management action due to the COVID19 pandemic as constraint to data gathering procedure in the formulation of GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Municipal Treasurer impost and collect the proper tax in the issuance of Community Tax Certificate (CTC) in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 as well as input all the necessary information in the CTC, otherwise, she will be held liable for the under assessments and collections through our issuance of Notice of Charge (NC). Likewise, educate the Barangay Treasurers, being the Deputized Collectors of CTC, in the computation and collection of tax.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management install sound internal control to those businesses operating without securing business permit through an ordinance as well as impose strictly the interest and penalties to those businesses who filed their business permits beyond the prescribed period set by the Revenue Code so as to increase the revenue collection of the Municipality that can be used for programs/projects and activities for the benefits of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: No management action

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

1.	The Municipal Budget Officer conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments towards attaining the targets set; and
2.	The Municipal Treasurer and other Department Heads involved in generating revenues endeavor to meet its targets.  Periodic review should be made by the concerned officials in the Municipality to ascertain whether collection targets are attained.  The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analyzed and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future.
observations.managementAction: Due to the COVID19 pandemic, collections from other source of income does not meet the projected amount.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a) Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical count of inventories and submit the RPCI to
the Audit Team; 

b) Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each
commodity/stock;

c) personnel in charge of inventories to (i) maintain stock cards for supplies which should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit; and (ii) consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate recognition of expenses in the books of accounts; and 

d) Municipal Accountant, thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments in the books to
reflect the correct/actual balances of the inventory in the financial statement.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to conduct a complete physical count of inventory

This is reiterated in Observation No. 2 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in Part II of this AAR, finding no. 9, pages 64-65)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects funded out of 20% Development Fund  and from external sources. The OIC-Municipal Accountant exert effort in recording the remaining 20% Local Roads Networks in the Municipality’s books 	of accounts.

We recommended that the Management make a reconciliation on the total number of sacks of rice purchased with the total number distributed rice and submit a report thereof to our Office for audit. Moreover, we recommended that the Management revisit their relief operation system and have regular reporting and documentation of every procedure to enhance accountability and transparency.

We recommended that the Management pay only the actual goods received by the recipients and demand for the refund of excess payments made to its suppliers to avoid disallowance thereof in audit. Likewise, submit the redeemed vouchers and list of recipients as support for the payment to Mallig Midtown Pharmacy for the goods vouchers of 1,300 and 3,000, respectively for further audit verification.
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant fully scrutinize the documents supporting every payment to ensure its propriety and avoid excessive payments.
We recommended that the Management submit a full accounting of the 444 sacks of purchased rice intended for the TODA.

We recommended that the Management implement the following:
Reclassify the recorded balance of Intra-Payable account to Trust Liability
If no records available to support the balance of Accounts Receivable and Due from NGAs, request to the Commission on Audit for write-off and/or adjustment of the account balance
Record the subject Land under Inventory Held for Sale and make the necessary adjustments regarding the sale
Conduct a physical count of inventory for accountable forms as of the reporting date to support the balance recorded in the Financial Statements, also adopt the perpetual inventory method of recording of Inventory as required in New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units Volume 1
Complete the submitted Report on Physical Count of PPE and make a reconciliation on the recorded balance.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of report on sources and utilization of DRRM fund as well as to the provisions of COA Circular 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting of cash donations for DRRM.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the LDRRMO incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/programs/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular NO. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the Management stop giving such incentives without any legal basis because it is considered and irregular expenditure pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the Disbursing Officer liquidate the cash advances and submit to the Auditor’s Office the necessary documents to liquidate the cash advances and to monitor the issuance of cash advances properly. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant to update the balance of the account of the Disbursing Officer showing the balance of unsubmitted vouchers and to record the liquidation of cash advances only when they received the complete liquidation report with supporting documents from the Disbursing Officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit daily collections in full to the depositary bank as required under provisions of Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
We recommended that the Management establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the required monthly financial reports on timely basis and strictly comply with COA Circular No 2009-006 and Section 122 of PD 1445 so as not to violate its provisions with corresponding sanctions set therein.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. The mode of procurement must be clearly indicated in the APP for strict compliance. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, PAGIBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of each calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 6(b) of RA 8291 and Rule VII Section 3 of IRR of RA 9679.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the GAD Focal Person fast track the establishment of GAD sex-disaggregated database to support the gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management through the Municipal Treasurer impost and collect the proper tax in the issuance of Community Tax Certificate (CTC) in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 as well as input all the necessary information in the CTC, otherwise, she will be held liable for the under assessments and collections through our issuance of Notice of Charge (NC). Likewise, educate the Barangay Treasurers, being the Deputized Collectors of CTC, in the computation and collection of tax.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management install sound internal control to those businesses operating without securing business permit through an ordinance as well as impose strictly the interest and penalties to those businesses who filed their business permits beyond the prescribed period set by the Revenue Code so as to increase the revenue collection of the Municipality that can be used for programs/projects and activities for the benefits of the constituents.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Treasurer (i) trace and identify the discrepancies and immediately effect corrections in the cashbook; (ii) ensure that records of the individual financial transactions in the cashbooks are correct and up-to-date by recording transactions daily or as they occur; (iii) review or double-check the posting and computation of amounts and balances in the cashbooks; (iv) review deposit slips to ensure correctness of amounts and account numbers being used; and (v) regularly reconcile the cashbook balances  with the accounting subsidiary records at least every end of the month or as the need arises; and
The Municipal Accountant (i) immediately draw adjusting entries for valid reconciling items identified during the preparation of BRS; and (ii) make correcting entries for the erroneous deposit in 2017 amounting to ₱34,000.00.

We recommended that the:
Inventory Committee exert efforts to complete the physical count of all the municipal supplies and property and prepare the physical inventory reports;
General Service Officer facilitate the proper disposal of unserviceable supplies and property;
Municipal Accountant reconcile the physical inventory reports with the ledger balances and make necessary adjustment in the books to reflect the correct balances of supplies and property accounts balance and to provide adequate disclosure of significant information in the Notes to Financial Statement; and
Municipal Mayor (i) immediately secure title for those land owned by the MGR  not yet transferred in the name of the Municipality; (ii)  create a special body, if necessary, such as Land Titling Committee to focus and expedite the application for titling of the said real properties and an Appraisal Committee that will be responsible in determining the correct value of the unrecorded land for subsequent recognition in the books of accounts; (iii) ensure that acquisition of real properties are supported by proof of transfer of ownership in favor of the MGR; and (iv) stop making advance payment for undelivered services or goods except for those authorized by law and submit justification on the initial payment of
₱1,000,000.00 for the subject land.
Further, we recommended that the MGR avail of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 that will help the MGR in determining its PPE balances in order to make it more verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MSWDO and the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit the duly accomplished SAC Forms, barangay certifications, printed copy of properly labeled pictures of beneficiaries during actual receipt of cash assistance; and (b) ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation prior to payment.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) trace and identify the discrepancies and immediately effect corrections by making adjustments in records or draw necessary correcting entries in the books for the identified errors; and (ii) regularly reconcile related accounts of STF; and
Municipal Treasurer (i) transfer the amounts of ₱5,115,446.43 and ₱233,123.89 from the STF to TF-RA (include remitted tax withheld from the transactions); and (ii) exercise prudence to ensure the proper charging of disbursements or withdrawals to its corresponding fund.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) continue its efforts in the full and optimum utilization of the 20% DF by spearheading the effective and efficient implementation of the prioritized projects to maximize the developmental benefits of the intended beneficiaries pursuant to Section 5 of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and (ii) instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in determining the actual status of the identified unimplemented Programs
and Projects from the previous years’ appropriation;
Municipal Engineer maintain close coordination with the MPDC for proper monitoring of project implementation to ensure the correctness and completeness of information being reported in the Status Report of Government PPAs and in the FDP Form No. 7 on the 20% component of the IRA utilization in order to comply with the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG and transparent information to the public; and
MPDC (i) ensure that the information provided in the Status Report of Government PPAs are complete before its submission to the Audit Team; (ii) coordinate with the MEO and the Municipal Accounting Office in order to obtain the necessary information for the Status Report of Government PPAs; (iii) continue efforts in identifying projects that are doable and most beneficial to the constituents; and (iv) consider projects related to environmental management.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor as LSB Chairperson prioritize the formulation of the SEF Budget by causing the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget within the prescribed period;
LSB convene a meeting especially during the formulation of the SEF Budget even without the presence of the LSB Chairperson as long as majority of all the members constitute a quorum with the Co-Chair as the presiding officer; and
LSB ensure that the LSB Resolution approving the SEF Budget is signed by the Chairperson, the Co-Chair and the majority of all the LSB members.

We recommended that the Management provide (i) at least 1% of the annual budget appropriations for the SC and PWD fund; (ii) at least 1% of the Internal Revenue Allotment for the LCPC; (iii) prepare and submit the LDPC and LIPC to ensure the full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC; and (iv) ensure optimal utilization of the fund to fully uphold the goal of addressing the concerns of senior citizens and persons with disability and the promotion of children’s rights.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal GAD
Focal Point System Executive Committee; Municipal Budget Office; and the Municipal Accountant (a) cause the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the GPB, GAD AR and other GAD related reports and documents for CY 2020; and (b)henceforth, ensure proper and timely submission of GAD related reports and documents in accordance with Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the (i) Management evaluate all existing LEEs and make the necessary changes to comply with DBM LBC No. 111; (ii) thereafter, Municipal Accountant shall set up and maintain a special account in the GF for each LEE; and (iii) MEEDO develop 5-Year Staffing Plan and Staff Transition Strategy and 5-Year Business Plan for each LEE.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) trace the discrepancies and prepare the necessary DV to  remit the accounted amount or prepare correcting entries if identified differences were due to errors or deficiencies in recording; (ii) support the SL sub-accounts with list of names of contractor and supplier for final tax withheld and names of employees for the compensation tax and other salary deductions with the corresponding amount withheld to be compared to the supporting list provided in the disbursement vouchers for remittances; and (iii) in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, ensure that amounts due to the BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth are remitted in full on time to avoid possible incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges.

1.3 We recommended that Management:
a. In coordination with the GSO, instruct the offices to conduct
a semestral physical count of inventories and said report
would be submitted to the auditor’s office on or before July
31 and January 31;
b. Direct the Accounting office to update the supplies ledger
card according to IPSAS; and
c. Require the GSO to maintain a stock card for each stock of
inventory for proper monitoring.

2.3 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and MTO to commit in conducting
periodic reconciliation of the Treasurer’s Cashbook and the
Accounting Ledgers in order to arrive to an accurate and reliable
balance of Cash in Bank;
b. Consider the closing of the depository accounts in the Trust Fund f
or those completed projects;
c. Direct the MTO to gather the missing and lacking data of the
cashbook with unreconciled balances; and
d. Direct the MTO to prepare cashbook for all the depository bank
account of the Municipality.

3.3 We revised our prior recommendations that Management enhance its
compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset
Management System:
a. Directing the GSO and Accounting Office to use the reporting
formats provided in the Circular in reporting the inventories of
Local Road Network;
b. Directing the Accounting office to maintain Local Road Network
Ledger Card (LRNLC) as prescribed in the circular); and
c. Directing the GSO to maintain the Local Road Network Property
Card (LRNPC) for proper monitoring of Road Network and its
component.

4.6 We revised our prior year recommendations that the Municipality enhance
its compliance with RA 9003 to:
a. Direct management to issue a cease and deceased order to stop
the operation of an open dumpsite; and
b. To install electrical and water utilities in the MRF Area for it to be
fully operational.

5.7 We revised our prior audit recommendations that management to upgrade
the internet connection of the BAC office.

6.5 We recommended that management would:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant in verifying the completeness of
attachments that will support the claims against the LGU;
b. Direct the GSO to contact suppliers and claimants with valid claims
and request for explanation as to why their claims are still
outstanding; and
c. Secure the approval from the Sanggunian Bayan on the reversion
of accounts payable existing for more than two (2) years to
unappropriated surplus

7.10 We revised our prior years audit recommendation that the Municipality
enhance its compliance with the policies and procedures by:
1. Completing the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Properties that shall be used as basis in the Disposal Procedures
and submit a copy to the office of the Team Leader.
2. Disposal committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and
unusable officer/other supplies and equipment using the
appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by
the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the
sale thereof, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces
occupied by these items

8.6 We revised our prior years audit recommendation that management:
a. Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the
officers/indebted individuals especially those who are no longer
connected to the municipality;
b. Cash Advances marked as deceased and current status falls to
the requirement stated in COA Circular 2016-005 for immediate
write off proceedings thereof; and
c. Consider the withholding the salary of the accountable officers
who are still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate
their cash advances for one (1) year and over until finally settling
their accounts.

9.6 We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial
Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

10.14 We recommend management to:
1. Discontinue granting Special Cash Advance to undesignated
disbursing officer;
2. Submit all the liquidation documents regarding on the BGCM grant;
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the correctness and
completeness of the supporting liquidation documents before
recording in the books of the Municipality; and
4. Direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with
the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the cash advance
liquidation with complete documentation.

11.4 We recommend management to:
1. Submit all the liquidation documents on cash advance granted under
LDRRMF within the prescribed period;
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the correctness and
completeness of the supporting liquidation documents before
recording in the books of the Municipality; and
3. Direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with
the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the cash advance
liquidation with complete documentation.

12.9 We revised our prior years audit recommendation that management to:
a. Require the LDRRMO to include the unexpended balances of the
LDRRMF to the LDRRMFIP for the upcoming years;
b. Update the LDRRMFIP 2021 that will include the unexpended
balances for the 2020 year-end;
c. Follow the prescribed format for the LDRRMFIP in Annex A of
COA-Circular 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to GSO in the conduct of inventory.
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the City GSO to immediately maintain PC per category of PPE pursuant to Section 119of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to:
Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials and employees of the cash advances totaling ₱5.015 million and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts; and
Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱1.727 million granted in prior years.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Require the City Accountant to record inventory to its appropriate account;
Require the City General Service Officer to prepare the monthly SSMI based on RIS; and
Submit Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) Held for Distribution and for Consumption by type of inventory to determine the cost of inventory items issued and to restore the correct inventory account balances as December 31, 2020, specifically for Welfare Goods for Distribution valued at ₱24.536 million, Drugs and Medicines for Distribution valued at ₱184,698.00 and for Operation valued at ₱2.168 million.

We recommended that thereafter the Local Development Council prepare and submit the Supplemental Investment Program to cover all the COVID-19 related PPAs to be approved by the local sanggunian as provided in Section 2.6 of LBC No. 124 and Section 2.3 of DILG/DBM JMC No. 01.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to implement immediately, when possible, the priority identified/planned PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year and to maximize unused balances of LDRRMF to attain the objectives of current priorities this time of the COVID-19 pandemic pursuant to the above-mentioned circular.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate implementation of identified priority programs/projects/activities funded from the 20% Development Fund. Likewise, prioritize implementation of PPAs related to COVID-19 response to address the imminent threat of the pandemic to public health and economy.

We recommended that the OIC–City Treasurer directly and closely monitor the submission of RAAF as of end of the month by the AOs to provide better control on the custodial function of all accountable officers under her supervision.

We recommended that Management:
Enhance closer monitoring activities on fund transfer and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay funded programs of all on-going/not started projects to attain the program’s objectives; and
Direct the City Accountant to return/remit any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to the aforementioned circulars.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Implement immediately the PPAs identified/programmed for the year in the Local School Board Budget, particularly for Capital Outlay intended for blended/distance learning modalities in order to provide effective and safer learning opportunities in times of pandemic; and
Ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of appropriated funds for priority PPAs and reinforce the budgeting priorities by preparing the SEF Budget based on the DepEd approved School Improvement Plan (SIP) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDP) and consider DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular Nos.1 and 2 in prioritizing SEF PPAs under the new normal set-up.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:

a. Serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel who was granted cash advance for operating expenses and for other receivable 

b. Exert more efforts to retrieve records on file for Due from NGAs, GOCCs, Other LGUs  and  Due from Officers and Employees and request for write-off from the books for all dormant accounts,  in case full efforts prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

c. Make necessary adjusting entries to the Accounts affected once the request for write-off is granted.

We recommended that: 

 OIC-Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand  settlement to personnel  with unliquidated cash advances aged 1 year and above; 


Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects funded out of 20% Development Fund  and from external sources. The OIC-Municipal Accountant exert effort in recording the remaining 20% Local Roads Networks in the Municipality’s books 	of accounts.

We recommended that:

Property Officer prepare and submit Property Card, IIRUP and WMR to the OIC Municipal Accountant to facilitate dropping of demolished Public Market from the agency’s books of accounts.

Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in the preparation of the reports.

c. Henceforth, strictly comply with applicable laws, rules and regulations pertaining to disposal of government property.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant; a) adjust in the books the reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statement so as to depict the adjusted cash in bank balance and take up necessary adjusting entries in the noted stale checks, b) adopt the perpetual inventory method of recording inventories as well as have a physical count of inventory at least quarterly, c) reconcile the actual physical inventory of properties with the accounting records and drop those properties found to be unserviceable and missing, d) request for write-off from COA to those dormant receivable accounts with no available details and after satisfying the needed documents provided in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; and e) reconcile the Intra-Agency Receivable and Payables and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated Financial Statements, so as not to cast doubt on the validity and reliability of the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management already adjusted the balance of cash in bank account reflecting the adjusted bank balance. Also conducted a physical count of inventory and PPE, however, they only conducted physical count on office supplies and medicines, they don’t include the accountable forms. The Report on Physical Count of PPE does not reflect the balance presented in the Financial Statements. The management does not request for write-off during the year. There was still unreconciled balance of Intra-Agency accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their books at least quarterly so as to satisfy the provisions under COA Circular No. 97-002, in order to adjust any differences noted on time.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented our audit observation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the MHRMO and Municipal Accountant require the concerned job order workers to submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 to ensure that the wages paid were proper and necessary.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented our audit observation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Accountant compel Ms. Sarmiento to present the official receipt supporting the payment of the said premium in order to liquidate the cash advance and record the necessary expense.
observations.managementAction: Ms. Sarmiento return the amount to the management and subsequently remitted the same to GSIS

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through e the General Services Officer strictly comply with the requirements set forth in Section 361 par (e) and (g) of GAAM Vol 1.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented our audit observation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat post the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract within the prescribed period set in by the provisions of RA 9184 to achieve transparency and to avoid any doubts on the propriety of bidding activity.
observations.managementAction: The management already posted the NOA, NTP and the approved contract to the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat invite observers in the post-qualification phase of the procuring process and issue the letter of invitation within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 13 of RA9184 to enhance transparency of the procuring process.
observations.managementAction: The management already invited observers throughout the bidding process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the BAC require Camberwell Construction & Supplies to replace the warranty security posted in accordance to the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management already communicated to the contractor the deficiency in the warranty security and already change the warranty security following the rates required in RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the LDRRMO incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/programs/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular NO. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The management does not include to their LDRRMFIP the PPAs charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund be remitted/returned immediately to the National Treasury consistent with the Supreme Court ruling and COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The management made a reconciliation on the amount of PDAF and found out that the fund was not from PDAF of Sen. Honnasan but a fund transfer from the Provincial Government of Isabela.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period, otherwise, they shall be held personally liable for amount of tax not withheld plus penalties.

observations.recommendation:

 Municipal Treasurer (i) exert diligent effort in identifying and reconciling the variance of ₱60,352,995.85 with the accounting records to support correctness of the account balances; and (ii) henceforth, regularly reconcile the cashbooks for the CIB-LCCA with accounting subsidiary records in compliance with 32 Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual so that discrepancies or errors could be immediately identified and corrected; and

b. Municipal Accountant (i) retrieve/locate the supporting documents of the reconciling items and facilitate the recording thereof in the accounting books to reflect the correct balances of CIB-LCCA accounts in the financial reports; and (ii) ensure the timely preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written
notice to all concerned accountable officers and employees to demand
settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute the necessary legal
action against those who failed to settle their accountabilities;

Municipal Accountant review the balances of the outstanding CA and gather
evidence such as death certificate, previous demand letters issued and other
relevant documents intended to support the request for write-off, if warranted
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

c. In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must
ensure that all provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,
1997 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at
the end of the year.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Accountant 
issued demand letters and withhold the salaries of those who failed to
liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period.

Cash
Advances granted from CY 2005-2008 to former employees who are on AWOL status and no

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and
responsible person;

b. GSO maintain complete and updated property cards for PPE with complete information and ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of third
year after issue; and

c. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property subsidiary ledgers
contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation
with the inventory report of the inventory committee.
observations.managementAction:
GSO maintained property cards for PPE and all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE 

The Municipal Accountant prepared property subsidiary ledgers with details/ description of the PPE.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to conduct a complete physical count of inventory

This is reiterated in Observation No. 2 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure the optimal
utilization of the 20% DF by fast-tracking the project implementation of on-going
projects, reviewing those that may no longer be utmost priority and, if necessary,
reprogram the same to other more important PPAs that are economically beneficial to
the constituents; and (b) render explanation on the deficit or insufficiency of the cash in bank for 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: Most PPAs under the current 20% DF were completed.
observations.reason:
Programs and Projects from previous years’ appropriation for the 20% DF amounting to ₱6,037,892.12 were not yet utilized.

This is reiterated in Observation No. 6 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor (a) ensure that the required reports on projects/ programs/ activities are submitted within the period prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, as amended by COA Circular No. 2015- 006 dated August 20, 2015; and (b) instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of
all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented for CY 2020.

We recommended that the Management direct the Local Chief Executive to:

a) submit the approved revised Implementation and Procurement Plans together with the relevant documents for validation of the Audit Team; and

b) call the attention of the Municipal Project Management and Implementation Unit (MPPMIU) to ensure the (i) timely execution of the Work and Financial Plan of GEF subprojects; and (ii) completeness and adequacy of documentary requirements upon submission for payment to fast track the disbursement process and prevent accumulation of the unexpended fund balance at year end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare and
submit the required SCBAA for CY 2019 pursuant to Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2016- 004, Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and Item 14 of PPSAS 24.

a.	Three-Way Reconciliation between the Municipal Engineer, GSO, and Accounting as stated on each responsibility on the COA Circular 2015-008;

b.	GSO should obtain a report from the Municipal Engineer regarding the details of each RN then reconcile it with their RPCPPE on RN.
a.	Coordination with the Accounting and GSO for reconciling the reports on the actual physical count and accounting records and make the necessary adjusting entry;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as inventory, except those purchased through petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and an inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.

c.	Exercise strong political will and intensify campaign calling for active participation of all barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes.

a.	Demanding the immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advances;

b.	Enforcing strictly the period of liquidation as prescribed above and require the immediate refund/return of any excess amount thereof; and

b.	Require the LDRRMO to categorized the PAPs for LDRRM as to the Four Thematic Areas; and

c.	Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month.

a.	Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) ensure that the list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details is prepared, maintained and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year; and

b.	Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer coordinate with each other to prepare and submit the quarterly report of status of all projects, programs and activities to the Office of the Auditor with complete details as provided in Annex B of COA Circular 2013-004.

c. Cause the Accounting office to maintain LRNLC for accurate details of RN; and

d.	Provide and maintain by the GSO the LRNPC for proper monitoring of Road Network and its component.

c. GSO/Property Custodian to report all obsolete and unserviceable PPE in the Inventory and Inspection   Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) duly signed   by the Head of Accounting Unit, Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by Head of Agency or authorized representative, and submit the approved IIRUP for proper disposition; and

d.	Disposal committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable officer/other supplies and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items.

c. Exercise strong political will and intensify campaign calling for active participation of all barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes.
c. Withholding the salary of the accountable officers who are still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate their cash advances for one (1) year and over as of December 31, 2019.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

b.	Require the LDRRMO to categorized the PAPs for LDRRM as to the Four Thematic Areas; and

c.	Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month.

We recommended that Management:

Refrain from drawing a cash advance in lieu of direct payment to suppliers except in highly justifiable instances where payment of checks is impractical; and





Strictly comply with COA Circular 97-002 and RA 9184 insofar as disbursements is concerned.

We recommended that Management:

Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for failure to fully utilize the 70 percent LDRRMF; and






Effectively, efficiently and economically implement the priority identified/planned PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to the above-mentioned circular.

We recommended that Management: 

Prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than                  January 31 of each year in compliance with the above-cited provisions. The RPCPPE should be certified correct by the Inventory Committee, approved by the Head of the Agency and reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted; and

Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

b.1	Creation of Inventory Committee;
b.2	Property Identification and Coding by the Inventory Committee;
b.3	Issuance of Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to employee receiving the property; and
b.4	Preparation and submission to COA of prescribed Inventory Reports.

We recommended that Management:

Enhance closer monitoring activities on fund transfer and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay funded programs of all on-going/not started projects to attain the program’s objectives; 



Direct the City Accountant to conduct regular periodic verification and reconciliation of the unexpended balance of fund transfers Due to NGAs account, determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims, and cause the immediate return/remittance of any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular 94-013; and

Direct the City Accountant to book-up completed projects in the General Fund at acquisition cost.

We recommended that Management:

Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2019 to the Audit Team for review/validation; and
 

Henceforth, monitor GAD activities and require the GAD Focal Person to perform constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report and submit a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year in strict compliance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management:

Direct the City Accountant to initiate the write-off of dormant accounts and undertake the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in Sections 6.0, 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; 



Conduct periodic monitoring and analysis for issuance of demand letter and initiate write-off if the borrowers are insolvent, could no longer be located and dead; and


Pending application/approval of request for write-off, appropriate disclosure should be made on the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:

Submit immediately the financial reports and accounts listed in Annex A which are already overdue; and




Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on the timely submission of financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommended that Management:

a.	Submit the MOA and Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses for fund transfers made during the year; and

b.	Refrain from granting fund transfers to other agencies without the required documents as provided for in Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular                       No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:

a. Serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel who was granted cash advance for operating expenses and for other receivable 

b. Exert more efforts to retrieve records on file for Due from NGAs, GOCCs, Other LGUs  and  Due from Officers and Employees and request for write-off from the books for all dormant accounts,  in case full efforts prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

c. Make necessary adjusting entries to the Accounts affected once the request for write-off is granted.

We recommended that: 

 OIC-Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand  settlement to personnel  with unliquidated cash advances aged 1 year and above; 


Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting cash advances.

We recommended that:

Property Officer prepare and submit Property Card, IIRUP and WMR to the OIC Municipal Accountant to facilitate dropping of demolished Public Market from the agency’s books of accounts.

Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in the preparation of the reports.

c. Henceforth, strictly comply with applicable laws, rules and regulations pertaining to disposal of government property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/programs/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular NO. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the concerned contractors of the aforementioned projects to (i) repair the defects and other installed items or unperformed works noted totaling ₱9,076,618.66 or deduct the amount of the cost deficiencies from any money claim or retention fee due them or other securities posted by them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a) the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund by fast-tracking the project implementation of on-going projects, reviewing those that may no longer be utmost priority and reprogramming the same to other more important PPAs economically beneficial to the constituents; and b) the Municipal Development Council and the MPDC closely monitor and strongly pursue the timely and full implementation of all PPAs and regularly submit status reports to the Municipal Mayor so that appropriate measures can be instituted.
observations.reason: Programs/Projects/Activities appropriated under the 20% are not fully implemented; reiterated in Observation No. 5 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality enhance its GAD Focal Point System by:
 conducting gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the municipality in terms of GAD compliance; 

Creating capacity building for GFPS to have in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD, such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and

3. To revisit and comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 and other provisions of law.

We recommended that the Municipality address the root cause of long outstanding accounts payable and availability of its supporting documents by communicating to the claimants on the status and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts once verified and found to be without valid claims, as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipality should address root cause long outstanding receivable by:

 ascertaining correctness and possibility of collection and exert effort which includes sending demand letters to persons who have known address; and

preparing request for write-off for account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management submit valid written justification on the realignment/reprogramming made. Otherwise, administrative and/or civil sanctions shall be instituted against the persons held liable. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provision of Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 on the use of appropriated funds and savings.

b. Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant is still trying to locate the concerned individual before requesting to write-off the cash advances.

observations.recommendation: 1. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct per department/office annual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and GSO/Supply Officer prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be approved by the Municipal Mayor;
observations.reason: Due to inadequate records turn-over to the new GSO/Supply Officer

observations.recommendation: 2. Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and
observations.reason: Inventory reports unfinished due to incomplete costing of the property

observations.recommendation: 3. Reconciled RPCPPE with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.
observations.reason: Difficulty of reconciling due to non- maintenance of Property Card and Property ledger card.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant; a) adjust in the books the reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statement so as to depict the adjusted cash in bank balance and take up necessary adjusting entries in the noted stale checks, b) adopt the perpetual inventory method of recording inventories as well as have a physical count of inventory at least quarterly, c) reconcile the actual physical inventory of properties with the accounting records and drop those properties found to be unserviceable and missing, d) request for write-off from COA to those dormant receivable accounts with no available details and after satisfying the needed documents provided in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; and e) reconcile the Intra-Agency Receivable and Payables and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated Financial Statements, so as not to cast doubt on the validity and reliability of the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the required monthly financial reports on timely basis and strictly comply with COA Circular No 2009-006 and Section 122 of PD 1445 so as not to violate its provisions with corresponding sanctions set therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their books at least quarterly so as to satisfy the provisions under COA Circular No. 97-002, in order to adjust any differences noted on time.

We recommended that the Management through the MHRMO and Municipal Accountant require the concerned job order workers to submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 to ensure that the wages paid were proper and necessary.

We recommended that the Management through the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Accountant compel Ms. Sarmiento to present the official receipt supporting the payment of the said premium in order to liquidate the cash advance and record the necessary expense.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through e the General Services Officer strictly comply with the requirements set forth in Section 361 par (e) and (g) of GAAM Vol 1.

We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat post the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract within the prescribed period set in by the provisions of RA 9184 to achieve transparency and to avoid any doubts on the propriety of bidding activity.

We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat invite observers in the post-qualification phase of the procuring process and issue the letter of invitation within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 13 of RA9184 to enhance transparency of the procuring process.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. The mode of procurement must be clearly indicated in the APP for strict compliance. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.
We recommended that the Management through the BAC require Camberwell Construction & Supplies to replace the warranty security posted in accordance to the provisions of RA 9184.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the LDRRMO incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/programs/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular NO. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We reiterated our recommendation that the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund be remitted/returned immediately to the National Treasury consistent with the Supreme Court ruling and COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period, otherwise, they shall be held personally liable for amount of tax not withheld plus penalties.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, PAGIBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of each calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 6(b) of RA 8291 and Rule VII Section 3 of IRR of RA 9679.

We recommended that the Management through the GAD Focal Person fast track the establishment of GAD sex-disaggregated database to support the gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Engineer demand to the concerned contractor to rectify the noted cracks in order to prevent further damage on the structure and will be subject to revalidation after rectification, otherwise, the corresponding amount of the deficiency noted shall be disallowed in audit. Moreover, the Municipal Engineer to monitor closely the construction of projects so as not to cause deficiency that would affect the serviceability and convenience of its constituents.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Engineer and Project Manager / In-Charge implement an efficient monitoring system for all on-going projects. We also recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant impose liquidated damages on the claims of the contractor of the above-mentioned project if the delays cannot be validly justified to avoid audit disallowance.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Treasurer impost and collect the proper tax in the issuance of Community Tax Certificate (CTC) in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 as well as input all the necessary information in the CTC, otherwise, she will be held liable for the under assessments and collections through our issuance of Notice of Charge (NC). Likewise, educate the Barangay Treasurers, being the Deputized Collectors of CTC, in the computation and collection of tax.

We recommended that the Management install sound internal control to those businesses operating without securing business permit through an ordinance as well as impose strictly the interest and penalties to those businesses who filed their business permits beyond the prescribed period set by the Revenue Code so as to increase the revenue collection of the Municipality that can be used for programs/projects and activities for the benefits of the constituents.

We recommended that:
1.	The Municipal Budget Officer conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments towards attaining the targets set; and
2.	The Municipal Treasurer and other Department Heads involved in generating revenues endeavor to meet its targets.  Periodic review should be made by the concerned officials in the Municipality to ascertain whether collection targets are attained.  The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analyzed and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer (i) reconcile their cash records and
settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per general ledger and
cashbooks, and (ii) install a strong internal control in checking the accuracy
and reliability in the transfer of the 20% of IRA and submit the lacking
supporting documents; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) exert effort to prepare and submit the bank
reconciliation statements for all bank accounts, and (ii) maintain subsidiary
ledgers for all bank accounts and regularly reconcile the totals with their
respective control account.
On June 24, 2020, the Municipal Accountant as approved by the Municipal
Mayor requested for an extension to submit justification and required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) identify the nature of
the recorded Due from LGUs and NGOs/POs in the books of accounts, and
enforce the liquidation of such transfer; (ii) maintain appropriate subsidiary
ledgers for each account as presented in the financial statements pursuant to
Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; (iii) reclassify all
financial assistance to LGUs and NGOs/POs recorded as expense to the
appropriate receivable accounts (Due from LGUs and Due from NGO/PO) to

reflect the true financial position of the Municipal Government, and (iv) ensure
that all financial assistance given to LGUs and NGOs/POs were treated as a

COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, COA Circular No. 94-013
dated December 13, 1994 and COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25,
2007.

We recommended that the:
a. Designated-GSO and Inventory Team conduct the physical count of property
and equipment in every office to facilitate the complete inventory of all
property and equipment under the General and Special Education Funds,
and prepare the RPCPPE and reconcile the same with the records of the

c. Municipal Accountant (i) analyze the nature of transactions and make an

d. GSO-Designate prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map to account for

e. Municipal Engineer maintain road map and provide the Muncipal
Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects; and
f. Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count and technical
valuation of the local road network to determine the current value of the
37
road components to facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN.
On June 24, 2020, the Municipal Accountant as approved by the Municipal
Mayor requested for an extension to submit justification and required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) provide explanation
on the noted difference in the accounts payable recorded in the GL and in the
submitted year-end trial balance; (ii) coordinate with the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer or other concerned offices for the retrieval of records of all
outstanding accounts payable; (iii) maintain appropriate subsidiary records

(iv) age the outstanding accounts payable and provide the Audit Team a copy of
