On June 24, 2020, the Municipal Accountant as approved by the Municipal
Mayor requested for an extension to submit justification and required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant make representation with the bank for the
reconciliation of the record of the Loans payable under Term Loan 6-
Construction of the third floor of Municipal Building (Legislative and
Executive Offices) – Phase II and make the necessary adjustments in the
Municipal Government’s accounting books, if warranted; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to provide immediately and regularly the copy of the
debit memoranda and/or snapshot from the bank to the Municipal
Accountant to record the payment of loan amortization on time.
On June 24, 2020, the Municipal Accountant as approved by the Municipal
Mayor requested for an extension to submit justification and required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and
maintain subsidiary ledgers, schedules, and reports/documents to support the
recorded amounts of Due to NGAs, LGUs and Other Funds accounts; (ii)
review and analyze the details of transactions regarding the negative balance
in the Due to Other Funds account to identify the cause of the negative balance
and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted; and (iii) reconcile the Due to/from
accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to
ensure that all the identified differences are resolved and/or adjusted.
On June 24, 2020, the Municipal Accountant as approved by the Municipal
Mayor requested for an extension to submit justification and required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that transactions be recorded to its
appropriate account in consonance with the revised chart of accounts as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (ii)
retrieve from the Audit Team the paid disbursement vouchers charged
against the intelligence fund of the Municipality, and submit the same to
COA-ICFAU following the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015; and
b. The Municipal Budget Officer to review the budget for the succeeding years
covering the lump sum appropriations of OMOE and make revision of the
budget in accordance with the results of the review following the account
description mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015.
On June 24, 2020, the Municipal Accountant as approved by the Municipal
Mayor requested for an extension to submit justification and required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer (i) exert diligent effort in identifying and reconciling the variance of ₱60,352,995.85 with the accounting records to support correctness of the account balances; and (ii) henceforth, regularly reconcile the cashbooks for the CIB-LCCA with accounting subsidiary records in compliance with Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual so that discrepancies or errors could be immediately identified and corrected; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) retrieve/locate the supporting documents of the reconciling items and facilitate the recording thereof in the accounting books to reflect the correct balances of CIB-LCCA accounts in the financial reports; and (ii) ensure the timely preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written notice to all concerned accountable officers and employees to demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute the necessary legal action against those who failed to settle their accountabilities;
b.  Municipal Accountant review the balances of the outstanding CA and gather evidence such as death certificate, previous demand letters issued and other relevant documents intended to support the request for write-off, if warranted pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
c. In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must ensure that all provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person;
GSO maintain complete and updated property cards for PPE with complete information and ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of third year after issue; and
The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property subsidiary ledgers contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (a) Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical count of inventories and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team; (b) Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock; (c) personnel in charge of inventories to (i) maintain stock cards for supplies which should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit; and (ii) consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal  Accountant to facilitate recognition of expenses in the books of accounts; and (d) Municipal Accountant, thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct/actual balances of the inventory in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF by fast-tracking the project implementation of on-going projects, reviewing those that may no longer be utmost priority and, if necessary, reprogram the same to other more important PPAs that are economically beneficial to the constituents; and (b) render explanation on the the deficit or insufficency of the cash in bank for 20% DF.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor (a) ensure that the required reports on projects/programs/activities are submitted within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015; and (b) instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented for CY 2020.

We recommended that the Management direct the Local Chief Executive to:
a) submit the approved revised Implementation and Procurement Plans together with the relevant documents for validation of the Audit Team; and
b) call the attention of the Municipal Project Management and Implementation Unit (MPPMIU) to ensure the (i) timely execution of the Work and Financial Plan of GEF subprojects; and (ii) completeness and adequacy of documentary requirements upon submission for payment to fast track the disbursement process and prevent accumulation of the unexpended fund balance at year end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare and submit the required SCBAA for CY 2019 pursuant to Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2016-004, Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and Item 14 of PPSAS 24.

1.8	Recommendations:
1.8.1	We reiterated that the Municipal Government enhance its compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:
a.	Three-Way Reconciliation between the Municipal Engineer, GSO, and Accounting as stated on each responsibility on the COA Circular 2015-008;
b.	GSO should obtain a report from the Municipal Engineer regarding the details of each RN then reconcile it with their RPCPPE on RN.
c.	Cause the Accounting office to maintain LRNLC for accurate details of RN; and
d.	Provide and maintain by the GSO the LRNPC for proper monitoring of Road Network and its component.
1.9      Management Comment:
1.9.1	The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no comment has been received yet.

2.3	Recommendations:
2.3.1	We recommended that the Municipality enhance its compliance with the policies and procedures in:
a.	Coordination with the Accounting and GSO for reconciling the reports on the actual physical count and accounting records and make the necessary adjusting entry;
b.	GSO/Property Custodian to report all obsolete and unserviceable PPE in the Inventory and Inspection   Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) duly signed   by the Head of Accounting Unit, Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by Head of Agency or authorized representative, and submit the approved IIRUP for proper disposition; and
d.	Disposal committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable officer/other supplies and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items.
2.4      Management Comment:
2.4.1	The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no comment has been received yet.

3.10	Recommendation:
3.10.1	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as inventory, except those purchased through petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and an inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.
3.11     Management Comment:
3.11.1	The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no comment has been received yet.

4.8	Recommendations:
4.8.1	We recommended that the Municipality enhance its compliance with RA 9003 to:
a.	Stop the operation of open dumpsite;
b.	Putting the Materials Recovery Facility into full operation to receive, sort, process, and store compostable and recyclable material efficiently and in an environmentally sound manner; and
c.	Exercise strong political will and intensify campaign calling for active participation of all barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes.
4.9     Management Comment:
4.9.1	The municipality is on the process of implementing a closed dumpsite through the funding.  Implementation of the project pending due to the present situation but rest assured that it will be implemented as soon as possible because we have already a funding.
4.10	COAs rejoinder
4.10.1	The Audit Team note managements comments and enjoined full compliance of the recommendations which will be monitored in CY 2020

6.7	Recommendations:
6.7.1	We recommended that management institute control mechanism by:
a.	Demanding the immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advances;
b.	Enforcing strictly the period of liquidation as prescribed above and require the immediate refund/return of any excess amount thereof; and
c.	Withholding the salary of the accountable officers who are still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate their cash advances for one (1) year and over as of December 31, 2019.
6.11      Management Comment:
6.11.1   The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no
comment has been received yet.

7.4	Recommendations:
7.4.1	The Management is recommended to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the LDRRMF by:
a.	Requiring the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan to be reviewed and updated annually;
b.	Require the LDRRMO to categorized the PAPs for LDRRM as to the Four Thematic Areas; and
c.	Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month.
7.5	Management Comment:
7.5.1   The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no
comment has been received yet.

8.5	Recommendation
8.5.1	We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.
8.6	Management Comment:
8.6.1   The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no
comment has been received yet.

9.5	Recommendations:
9.5.1	We recommended that management require the:
a.	Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) ensure that the list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details is prepared, maintained and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year; and
b.	Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer coordinate with each other to prepare and submit the quarterly report of status of all projects, programs and activities to the Office of the Auditor with complete details as provided in Annex B of COA Circular 2013-004.
9.6	Management Comment:
9.6.1   The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no
comment has been received yet.

10.6	Recommendation:
10.6.1	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.
10.7	Management Comment:
10.7.1	The management duly acknowledged the AOM, however, no
comment has been received yet.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱10.798 million;
Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱1.740 million granted in prior years and impose administrative sanctions like withholding of emoluments due them for those who have outstanding unsettled obligations;
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 14 of E.O. 298 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and COA Circular No. 2012-004 on the Demand for Immediate Liquidation and Settlement of All Cash Advances Outstanding as of December 31, 2011. In particular, send demand letter to accountable officials/employees and institute appropriate action against them.

We recommended that Management:
Refrain from drawing a cash advance in lieu of direct payment to suppliers except in highly justifiable instances where payment of checks is impractical; and
Strictly comply with COA Circular 97-002 and RA 9184 insofar as disbursements is concerned.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for failure to fully utilize the 70 percent LDRRMF; and
Effectively, efficiently and economically implement the priority identified/planned PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to the above-mentioned circular.

We recommended that Management:
Enhance closer monitoring activities on fund transfer and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay funded programs of all on-going/not started projects to attain the program’s objectives;
Direct the City Accountant to conduct regular periodic verification and reconciliation of the unexpended balance of fund transfers Due to NGAs account, determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims, and cause the immediate return/remittance of any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular 94-013; and
Direct the City Accountant to book-up completed projects in the General Fund at acquisition cost.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2019 to the Audit Team for review/validation; and
Henceforth, monitor GAD activities and require the GAD Focal Person to perform constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report and submit a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year in strict compliance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the City Accountant to initiate the write-off of dormant accounts and undertake the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in Sections 6.0, 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005;
Conduct periodic monitoring and analysis of the dormant accounts for issuance of demand letter and initiate write-off if the borrowers are insolvent, could no longer be located and dead; and
Pending application/approval of request for write-off, appropriate disclosure be made on the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Submit immediately the financial reports and accounts listed in Annex A which are already overdue; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on the timely submission of financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land;
b. Require the General Service Officer to maintain Property Cards for each category of
assets;
c. Direct the Inventory/Appraisal Committee to assess the value of the assets
submitted per office/.division and prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE
to be approved by the Municipal Mayor;
d. Transfer all unserviceable, damaged PPE to equipment pool for proper
accountability/disposal if proven beyond repair and to clear the area for future
usage;
e. Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of
each year; and
f. Reconciled RPCPPE with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments
if there are discrepancies noted.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-08 (2019) dated February 19, 2020.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:
a. Serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel who was granted
cash advance for operating expenses and for other receivable
b. Exert more efforts to retrieve records on file for Due from NGAs, GOCCs, Other
LGUs and Due from Officers and Employees and request for write-off from the
books for all dormant accounts, in case full efforts prove futile, after complying
with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;
and
c. Make necessary adjusting entries to the Accounts affected once the request for
write-off is granted.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-07 (2019) dated February 19, 2020.

We recommended that:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand
settlement to personnel with unliquidated cash advances aged 1 year and above;
b. Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of
outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements
of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should
strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting cash
advances.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-01(2019) dated January 29, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit complete description and cost
segregation of road components for all road projects funded out of 20% Development
Fund and from external sources. The OIC-Municipal Accountant exert effort in
recording the remaining 20% Local Roads Networks in the Municipality’s books
of accounts.
Henceforth, Municipal Engineer and OIC-Municipal Accountant strictly adhere with
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-03 (2019) dated January 28, 2020.

We recommended that:
a. Property Officer prepare and submit Property Card, IIRUP and WMR to the OIC
Municipal Accountant to facilitate dropping of demolished Public Market from the
agency’s books of accounts.
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in the preparation of the reports.
c. Henceforth, strictly comply with applicable laws, rules and regulations pertaining
to disposal of government property.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-04 (2019) dated January 29, 2020.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate implementation of
identified priority programs/projects/activities funded from the 20% Development
Fund.
Henceforth, Management should regularly monitor the status of implementation of
PPAs and adopt contingency measures that will be resorted to in cases where
certain delaying factors are encountered.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-06 (2019) dated February 19, 2020.

We recommended that:
a. Stop issuing check in the name of the School Principal-in-charge of the District or
to Schools Division Superintendent without issuing Official Receipt or AF # 51;
b. Disbursement of SEF which can be directly paid to the payees should not be
granted as Financial Assistance to the School Principal, rather, the LGU will
undertake the disbursement; and
c. OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements against Special Education
Fund be properly reviewed and are supported with complete documentation in
order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-09 (2019) dated February 19, 2020.

We recommended the following:
a. Management make sure that the building/structure be utilized solely as
Municipal Crisis Center;
b. MSWDO facilitate the physical and documentary requirements needed to
qualify for the accreditation with the DSWD, RO 6; and
c. Management appropriate additional funding, if needed, to improve the
current facilities.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-02 (2019) dated January 28, 2020.

We recommended the following:
a. Management advise the Agency Authorized Officer to be more diligent in
approving the Loan Applications of employees, and ensure that loan
amortizations be deducted monthly without sacrificing the P5,000.00 minimum
net take home pay, as required by the law; and
b. OIC – Municipal Accountant to monitor the strict implementation of related
provisions and restrictions of the law.
The observation and recommendations herein cited were covered by
AOM No. 2020-05 (2019) dated January 30, 2020.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall cause the immediate remittance of
PDAF amounting to ₱431,292.27 to the National Treasury in compliance
with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013.
b. The LCE should direct the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Draw accounting journal entries to close the completed projects
recorded in the Construction in Progress to corresponding PPE
accounts and transfer the completed projects in the books of accounts
under the General Fund;
ii. Coordinate with other concerned department heads and determine the
status of implementation of various fund transfers to and effect their
disposition thereof.
iii. Reconcile the balances of fund transfers with the source agencies and
return the excess or unutilized balances to source agencies concerned.
iv. Draw accounting journal entries to correct the entries made on the fund
transfers received from the Provincial Government of Cotabato, DILG
and DSWD. Henceforth, appropriate accounts should be used upon
receipt of funds transfers from various sources.
v. Determine the composition of unreconciled accounts and
unsubstantiated balances and make appropriate adjustments in the
books to attain fair presentation of the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant will issue the final notice of demand to the
accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees
who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after
the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the
regulation;
b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s
certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before
processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; and
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will ensure that
elected officials are not granted with cash advances other than those
expressly granted by the existing laws, rules and regulations and only
permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as
disbursing officers.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. The LDRRM Council should:
i. Prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should
include both the current appropriations and the unexpended prior
year balances.
ii. Include in the preparation of LDRRMFIP the establishment of
evacuation centers and stockroom for the stockpiling of basic
eergency supplies, equipment and early warning device.
iii. Judiciously plan the programs, projects and activities to be
implemented and utilized optimally the available resources to realize
the purpose of the fund.
b. The Municipal Accountant should record all procurement and issuance of
supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged
against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of
balances.
c. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer,
must conduct the physical count of inventories. Likewise, the Municipal
General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and submit the
reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor.
d. The Bids and Awards Committee should fast track the procurement of the
rescue and response equipment.
We recommended that the municipal administrators discharge its duties and responsibilities through efficient and effective management of the Municipality’s finances to avoid incurrence of overdrafts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality collected P 10.353 million from the outstanding Cash Advances of the former Municipal Mayor and install an efficiency measures in their spending to cope up with their cash overdraft in the prior year. As of December 31, 2019, the Municipality has a cash savings amounting to P 6.816 million.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

The Municipal Engineer  provides the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The General Services Officer  maintains Local Road Inventory and Road Map and  keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Accountant  prepares the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already booked up 100% Municipal Roads.
The Management takes physical inventory of supplies, however no physical inventory on medicines and other supplies.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and the GSO  reconcile the listings of physical count of property against the subsidiary ledgers, in case of deficiencies/discrepancies, make the necessary adjustments/corrections of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The GSO takes physical inventory of properties, however, no reconciliation of accounting records on actual physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management  ensure that the 20% DF of the LGU be allotted and used for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic, development and environmental management as prescribe under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented our audit recommendation and install a control measure to ensure that the 20% DF were utilized as intended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit to the office of the auditor the status of the continuing appropriation for 20% Development Fund and include in the yearly report on utilization of 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: The utilization of  continuing appropriation under  20% DF were already included in the report of utilization of 20% DF in the current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reportorial requirements.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund on a timely basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management comply with the proper use of Petty Cash Fund through Section 48, Vol. 1 of New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stop the use of petty cash fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund totaling P10,249,265.42 be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and subsequently remit to the Department of Finance the said amount pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already remitted P 5 million of unused PDAF. The remaining P 5.249 million was remain unremitted due to non-recognition of actual fund transfer to the Municipality of the said fund. As of year-end, the Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling the fund to its source.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should see to it that contracts of service of all hired Job Order employees are properly signed both by the Local Chief Executive or his representative and the hired personnel before allowing the latter to start their work and payments thereof be made for the services rendered. Furthermore, copy of the said documents should be attached to the claim in order to support its legality and validity.
observations.managementAction: The Job Order Contracts were already duly acknowledged by the concerned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the collections of field collectors and require  them  to remit their collections daily to the Treasury Office so as to be included to the daily deposit of collections to avoid the risk of loss of government funds thru misuse, robbery or theft.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already install internal measures requiring all collectors to remit their daily collections to the Liquidating Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  maintain a separate cashbook for each account for proper control and monitoring of collections and deposits for each account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already have separate books on each account fund of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management evaluate the status of the project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by the NGAs and to fast track the implementation of the programs/projects for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: All projects funded by National Government Agencies was already implemented as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management limit its expenditures  to what is appropriated. The Municipal Budget Officer should maintain a registry to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer already install effective measures to ensure that all budgets were utilized/expended as intended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management monitor and ensure the full implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The 20% DF of the Municipality was fully utilized for the current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management limit its expenditures  to what is appropriated. The Municipal Budget Officer should maintain a registry to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer already install effective measures to ensure that all budgets were utilized/expended as intended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management  ensure that the LDRRMF be properly disbursed according to what is  prescribed under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer already install effective measures to ensure that all budgets were utilized/expended as intended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer  reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, PAGIBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of each calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 6(b) of RA 8291 and Rule VII Section 3 of IRR of RA 9679.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality were in the process of reconciliation on which account were unremitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written notice to all accountable officers and employees to demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute the necessary legal action against those who failed to settle their accountabilities.

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must ensure that all provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The salaries of active accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances were already withheld to compel them to settle their accountabilities; Demand Letters were sent to accountable officers.
observations.reason: The accountable officers still failed to liquidate their cash advances; This is reiterated in Observation No. 2 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person.

The GSO should maintain complete and updated property cards for PPE with complete information and ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of third year after issue.

The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property SLs contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable-Domestic account pursuant to paragraph 127 of IPSAS No. 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already included the details of the Loans Payable- Domestic account in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2019 financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant a) transfer the remaining funds received from various NGAs to the Trust Fund books as provided for in Section 309(b) of RA No. 7160, Section 4.3.1 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3, Section 4.3.1 of DILG MC No. 2018-61 and Section 3.8 of DBM LBC No. 114; and b) reconstruct the SLs, reconcile the same with the List of Projects Sourced from NGAs and make the necessary adjustments to the Due to NGAs account in accordance with Section 111(1) and 112 of PD No. 1445;

The Municipal Mayor ensure that the aforementioned required reports for projects funded from ADM, AM and FA to LGUs are submitted to oversight agencies, copy furnished the Audit Team; and


The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer return or remit all unutilized BUB balance of ₱44,270.19 to the BTr in compliance with Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the funds received from various NGAs to the Trust Fund books; the required reports for projects funded from ADM and AM are submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Not all excess funds after completion of projects were returned to the issuing NGAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management evaluate all existing LEEs and make the necessary changes to comply with DBM LBC No. 111; b) thereafter, the Municipal Accountant shall set up and maintain a special account in the General Fund for each LEE; and c) the MEEDO develop 5-Year Staffing Plan and Staff Transition Strategy and 5-Year Business Plan for each LEE.
observations.managementAction: The Management started completing the requirements in the establishment of the LEEs; There is an on-going preparation of the 5-year staffing plan and staff transition strategy and 5-year business plan for each LEE.
observations.reason: There is no legislation issued in the establishment of the LEEs yet pending the completion of the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor execute a contract of lease with all occupants of the market stalls at the RPM and commercial space at the RTT in accordance with Article IV, Sections 2(1) and 2(7) of the Market Code and Article 4, Section 13 of the Land Transport Terminal Code to formally outline the conditions and duties and responsibilities to which both parties are legally bound.
observations.managementAction: Contracts of Lease have already been secured from the stall owners at the Public Market.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor award the lease privilege to the actual stall occupants in accordance with the provisions of the Market Code. The Market Supervisor should cause the immediate cancelation of Certificate of Award/Occupancy of those awardees who sub-leased their stalls in accordance with Article V, Section 11 of the Code.
observations.managementAction: The Management has stopped the practice of sub-leasing stalls to third party occupants in the Public Market.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Market Inspector and the Terminal Manager conduct inspection of business establishments and stalls to ensure collection of Mayor’s Permit fees and surcharges in compliance with the provisions of the Revised Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensured the renewal of business permits of the stall occupants for the coming year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a) the Market Committee maintain permanent record of stallholders and inventory of properties in the RPM for monitoring purposes; b) the Market Supervisor strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the operations of the public market and penalize all violators in accordance with Article V, Section 11 of the Code; and c) the Municipal Mayor explore feasible means, in view of funding constraints, to appropriate funds for the repairs and maintenance of market structures and facilities and provision of personnel stationed at the MRF.
observations.managementAction: The Management included the repair and maintenance of the public market in the CY 2019 budget.
observations.reason: The Market Supervisor failed to maintain a permanent record of stallholders, especially those who were long time occupants. However, pertinent records of new stallholders were already secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a) the Terminal Manager maintain complete records of business owners/vendors and inventory of properties in the RTT for monitoring purposes; and b) the Municipal Mayor ensure that funds are allocated for the repairs and maintenance of RTT structures and facilities for the convenience and personal necessities of the travelling public and the transport sector.
observations.managementAction: The Management allocated funds for the repair and maintenance of the RTT structures and facilities
observations.reason: The inventory of properties in the RTT are not yet conducted and not maintained for monitoring purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor demand payment from R.C. Tagala Construction for the cost deficiency of ₱2,418,559.75 and liquidated damages of ₱439,716.73, otherwise, concerned officials can be held personally liable for the amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and schedules.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Acting Municipal Engineer submit the Certificate of Final Acceptance issued by the agency to the contractor and the authenticated copy of the warranty security for structural defects and failures as required under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the Certificate of Final Acceptance issued by the agency to the contractor and the authenticated copy of the warranty security
observations.reason: The management failed to demand the amount of cost deficiency of and liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMC, the MDRRMO and other personnel concerned to: (a) update the DRRM Plan with the inclusion of a Public Service Continuity Plan; and (b) explore feasible means, in view of funding constraints, to include in the LDRRMFIP the provision of additional trainings to DRRM personnel, procurement of the aforementioned lacking standby/stock of food packs and hygiene kits, equipment and tools to comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensured that stockpile of goods for immediate relief has been procured.
observations.reason: The management is yet to consider the provision for additional trainings of its personnel in the revised LDRRMFIP that is yet to be approved.; The Public Service Continuity Plan is not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) ensure that adequate funds are provided for the provision of water supply and the construction of walls for the equipment section, as a minimum, and fence for the project site to provide security and proper care and maintenance of all components of CFBW; and b) require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the installation of power supply to the composting facility so that it could start its operations.
observations.managementAction: The electrical wirings have been installed; pending approval of application at the Palawan Electric Cooperative (PALECO) to energize; installed rain water collector to secure water supply
observations.reason: The perimeter fence is not yet constructed due to insufficient funds

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) improve in establishing clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda. The GFPS-TWG should also provide justification for all variances in the GAD AR to facilitate proper evaluation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management has improved the GAD Plan and Budget and ensured that the plan was carried out. GAD accomplishment reports were readily submitted and PPAs were found to be responsive of the GAD agenda.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should address the root cause of the difference by conducting reconciliation of balances between reciprocal accounts Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balance.

We recommended that the Municipality enhance its compliance on setting-up Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable by:

updating the RPT Account Registers/Taxpayer’s index cards and submit the corresponding duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables; and

submitting on time to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers.

We recommended that the Municipality enhance its reporting compliance by:

instituting monitoring control on the officials and employees concerned on prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made; and

addressing the root cause of delay by directing the concerned personnel to submit copies of duly perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five (5) working days from perfection thereof for review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management will implement and to effect reconciliation of records between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to address the root cause of unreconciled amount in their respective books and to prepare the necessary adjustment to reconcile balances per Cashbook and Subsidiary Ledger.

We recommended that Management to:

prioritize and implement the continuing projects of 20% Development Fund and require the Municipal Development Council to identify projects, programs and activities that are workable and implementable within the year to get maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials;

institute control mechanism to address the root cause of delay or non-implementation; and

enhance monitoring control to facilitate timely corrective measure during the implementation cycle of the projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that the Municipality should make a detailed review of the accounts with inappropriate balances, determine the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances.  Henceforth, exercise prudence in recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommended that Management to:

Direct the LDRRMO to facilitate local emergency responses through the implementation of P/P/As to reduce the level of risk or effects of disasters and geared towards the prevention of loss of life and reduction of loss of property brought by natural and human-induced disaster.

institute control mechanism to address the root cause of delay or non-implementation of Projects, Programs and Activities under the LDRRM Investment Plan; and

enhance monitoring control to facilitate timely corrective measure during the implementation cycle of the projects, programs and activities.
We recommended that Municipality direct the persons responsible in the issuance of Purchase Orders to include all the pertinent data to facilitate proper monitoring on the delivery of procured goods.

We recommended that the Municipality enhance its compliance with RA 9003 by:

instituting its monitoring mechanism to fully comply and address the root cause of non-compliance of various requirements of R.A. 9003.

coordinating efforts as to integration of relevant costs for the implementation of the SWMP for consideration in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan. 

implementing waste segregation at source and create a perimeter fence around the disposal site to implement the minimum considerations under Section 39, Article 6 of RA No. 9003 in the proper establishment and maintenance of a controlled dumpsite.

putting the Materials Recovery Facility into operation to receive, sort, process, and store compostable and recyclable material efficiently and in an environmentally sound manner.

We recommended that management institute control mechanism by:

 suspending the salary of the accountable officers who are still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate their cash advances for one (1) year and over as of December 31, 2018; and

 requesting for an authority to write-off cash advance that satisfies compliance of the requisites embodied by the rules.

We recommended that Management:

Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for failure to fully utilize the 70% LDRRMF; and



Effectively, efficiently and economically implement the priority PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular                No. 2012-73.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMC to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 6.5 of NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and Sections 4.4, 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that:

Submit the needed documents for the evaluation of delivered heavy equipments: dumptrucks, hydraulic excavator and bulldozer pursuant to Annexes F & G of COA Circular 2009-001 as follows: 



Certificate of Exclusive Distributorship, if applicable
Performance/Quality Test Result ( in good condition/operational)
Copy of Brochures/Manuals


Submit other needed documents, to wit:

Results of Test Analysis
Such other documents like:
b.2.1.  Certificate of Registration from Land Transportation Office
b.2.2.  Warranty Certificate

Authority from the Good Fortune International, Inc. for Mr. Edgar Torrecampo and Ms. Rona Ampane to receive payment, sign the Disbursement Vouchers and issue Official Receipt.

Submit Company ID of Ms. Rona Ampane, as applicable.

Henceforth, Management is directed to comply strictly with the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184, Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular 2012-001 and COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that Management:

Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to the Audit Team for review/validation; and
 

Henceforth, monitor GAD activities and require the GAD Focal Person to perform constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report and submit a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in strict compliance with Sections 4.8 and 5.2 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW JC No. 2004-1 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended the following:

Direct the City Administrator to review LEE activities to ensure profitability and sustainability of its operations so that quality services could be assured to benefit the public.



Prepare updated Lease Rental Agreement with tenants based on realistic rates to protect the interest of both parties.  



Procure the needed facilities to ensure desirable services to its clients by utilizing the appropriated funds for Capital Outlay of LEEs.




Acquire equipment and improve the LEE’s facilities as contemplated in             Section 5.1 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 Manual on the Setting-up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises that profits and income derived from LEE operation shall be applied to following priorities.

First, cost of improvement, repair and related expenses of the LEE;
Second, return of advances received by the LEE to the General Fund (GF); and
Any excess shall form part of the GF.

We recommended that Management: 

Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted;

Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identified by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.

Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)

An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.

Inventory Reports

The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.

Direct the City GSO to secure and submit Certificate of Land Title on real properties and Certificate of Registration on motor vehicles, dump trucks and heavy equipment in the name of the City of Roxas on all assets acquired during the year and taken up in the books to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.

We recommended that management:

Direct the City Accountant to initiate the write-off of dormant accounts and undertake the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in Sections 6.0, 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; 


Conduct periodic monitoring and analysis for issuance of demand letter and initiate write-off if the borrowers are insolvent, cannot be located and dead; and



Pending application/approval of request for write-off, appropriate disclosure should be made on the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to:

Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary corrections/ adjustments in the books of accounts; and

Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions.

We recommended that Management:

Submit a valid written justification on the charging made to 20% DF, which could have been a valid expenditure to Capital Outlay of the General Fund; and



Refrain from formulating programs, projects and activities which are not among those enumerated in Section 3.0 DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Human Resource Officer to review the staffing requirements of each department and recommend hiring of qualified personnel for regular appointment, if necessary, to regulate the employment of personnel under Job Order; and
	
Hire only JO personnel in accordance with the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs and assess the individual  assignments of all job order personnel to avoid redundant and duplicating work accomplishments.
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel with unliquidated cash advances aged 3 years and above;
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting cash advances.
a. Require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check become stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check;

b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to evaluate outstanding checks aging above six months which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of October 31, 2018 and make necessary adjustments and prepare JEV to record the transactions as Accounts Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I;

c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the ICO- Municipal Treasurer in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation statements so that any reconciling items can be taken-up in the books;
d. Maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger to facilitate processing of future claims.

a. Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex A which are already overdue; and Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that all unexpended balances in the Trust Fund will be remitted back to the source national government agencies or reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund as soon as the project has been completed.

a. Direct the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to prepare statement of accounts for the Lessees, and require to pay the corresponding unpaid monthly rentals and penalty/ies before any renewal of the contract in their favor;
b. Require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to monitor strictly the collection of market rentals; and
c. Strictly enforce compliance with the terms and conditions stipulated in the Lease contract.
b. Create Disposal Committee to ensure proper disposal of LGU’s property.
d. Henceforth, strictly comply with applicable laws, rules and regulations pertaining to disposal of government property.

We reiterated prior year’s recommendation for the inventory committee to conduct complete Report on physical count of all PPE and immediately reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to examine/analyze the discrepancies of the accounts and thereafter prepare necessary adjusting/correcting journal entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended beneficiaries at the soonest time possible. Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Barangay Health Centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government’s goal of providing preventive health care services to the indigents and similarly situated constituents will be optimized thus achieving the fund’s goals and objectives in the most opportune time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer has coordinated with the Barangay Health Centers to determine their needs particularly on providing health care services to those indigents and to those seeking health assistance. This is to ensure proper utilization of fund based on the actual needs of the community especially those in the far-flung areas.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and MPDC Officer comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required quarterly information of PPAs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as the withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand or stop the granting of additional cash advance. Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person.

The General Services Officer (GSO) should maintain complete and updated property cards for PPE with complete information and ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment which shall be renewed every January of third year after issue.

The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property subsidiary ledgers contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee.

In case of loss of property, the officer immediately accountable thereof should file an application for relief from accountability. The request should be accompanied by the documents required under Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Management created an inventory committee by virtue of Executive Order No. 230, series of 2018. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO were required to submit before the end of CY 2018 the status of physical inventory and reconciliation thereof with the accounting records.
observations.reason:
Management has not yet submitted the status of its inventory count. Moreover, the incomplete inventory report and the absence of updated property cards and PPE ledger cards hindered proper reconciliation between the physical count of PPE and accounting records.

To ensure full implementation, this observation is reiterated in Finding No. 3 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: Require the LDRRMC to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan.
