Require the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check become stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check;
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant to evaluate outstanding checks aging above six months which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2017 and make necessary adjustments and prepare JEV to record the transactions as Accounts Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I; and
Maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger to facilitate processing of future claims.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and to prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be approved by the Municipal Mayor;
Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and
Reconciled RPCPPE with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.
We recommended that the municipal administrators discharge its duties and responsibilities through efficient and effective management of the Municipality’s finances to avoid incurrence of overdrafts.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Engineer provides the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The General Services Officer maintains Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Accountant prepares the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that that the  Management through the Municipal Accountant and the GSO  reconcile the listings of physical count of property against the subsidiary ledgers, in case of deficiencies/discrepancies, make the necessary adjustments/corrections of the accounts.

We recommended that  the Management  ensure that the 20% DF of the LGU be allotted and used for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic, development and environmental management as prescribe under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
We recommended that the Management submit to the office of the auditor the status of the continuing appropriation for 20% Development Fund and include in the yearly report on utilization of 20% DF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/programs/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular NO. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reportorial requirements.
We recommended that the Management comply with the proper use of Petty Cash Fund through Section 48, Vol. 1 of New Government Accounting System.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary monthly reports regularly and comply with the provisions prescribed under COA Circular No 2009-006 and PD 1445 to avoid being meted with the sanctions provided by law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund totaling P10,249,265.42 be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and subsequently remit to the Department of Finance the said amount pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.
We recommended that Management consolidate the PPMP into APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services. Comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Management should see to it that contracts of service of all hired Job Order employees are properly signed both by the Local Chief Executive or his representative and the hired personnel before allowing the latter to start their work and payments thereof be made for the services rendered. Furthermore, copy of the said documents should be attached to the claim in order to support its legality and validity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to strictly observe and comply the provisions of Section 361 par (e) and (g) of GAAM Vol 1 on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles and henceforth.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the collections of field collectors and require  them  to remit their collections daily to the Treasury Office so as to be included to the daily deposit of collections to avoid the risk of loss of government funds thru misuse, robbery or theft.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain a separate cashbook for each account for proper control and monitoring of collections and deposits for each account.

We recommended that the Management evaluate the status of the project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by the NGAs and to fast track the implementation of the programs/projects for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Management limit its expenditures to what is appropriated. The Municipal Budget Officer should maintain a registry to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments.

We recommended that the Management monitor and ensure the full implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.

We recommended that the Management adhere to the above-cited provisions. If the management tends to increase its contribution, we recommended that the Management set a policy on the limit or maximum amount of government share so to be fair, just and equitable to the low salaried employees.

We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

We recommended that the Management limit its expenditures to what is appropriated. The Municipal Budget Officer should maintain a registry to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments.
We recommended that the Management  ensure that the LDRRMF be properly disbursed according to what is  prescribed under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer  reconcile the account to which the non-remittance apply and immediately remit to the concerned agency the amount due and to regularly remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, PAGIBIG and PhilHealth within 10 days of each calendar month to avoid penalties pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 6(b) of RA 8291 and Rule VII Section 3 of IRR of RA 9679.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written notice to all accountable officers and employees to demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute the necessary legal action against those who failed to settle their accountabilities.
In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must ensure that all provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign a monitoring team to closely coordinate with the NGOs/POs for the liquidation of funds transferred to them in accordance with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001. Likewise, management may institute appropriate actions against NGOs/POs who failed to comply with the provisions contained in the approved MOA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person.
The GSO should maintain complete and updated property cards for PPE with complete information and ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of third year after issue.
The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property SLs contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee.

We recommended that the a) Inventory Committee reconcile the inventory report with the RPI to facilitate the transfer of road networks carried in the registry to the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; b) thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and the GSO maintain ledger cards and property cards, respectively; and c) Municipal Mayor ensure the yearly conduct of physical count of local road network and the preparation of required reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable-Domestic account pursuant to paragraph 127 of IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant a) transfer the remaining funds received from various NGAs to the Trust Fund books as provided for in Section 309(b) of RA No. 7160, Section 4.3.1 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3, Section 4.3.1 of DILG MC No. 2018-61 and Section 3.8 of DBM LBC No. 114; and b) reconstruct the SLs, reconcile the same with the List of Projects Sourced from NGAs and make the necessary adjustments to the Due to NGAs account in accordance with Section 111(1) and 112 of PD No. 1445;
The Municipal Mayor ensure that the aforementioned required reports for projects funded from ADM, AM and FA to LGUs are submitted to oversight agencies, copy furnished the Audit Team; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer return or remit all unutilized BUB balance of ₱44,270.19 to the BTr in compliance with Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that management evaluate all existing LEEs and make the necessary changes to comply with DBM LBC No. 111; b) thereafter, the Municipal Accountant shall set up and maintain a special account in the General Fund for each LEE; and c) the MEEDO develop 5-Year Staffing Plan and Staff Transition Strategy and 5-Year Business Plan for each LEE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor execute a contract of lease with all occupants of the market stalls at the RPM and commercial space at the RTT in accordance with Article IV, Sections 2(1) and 2(7) of the Market Code and Article 4, Section 13 of the Land Transport Terminal Code to formally outline the conditions and duties and responsibilities to which both parties are legally bound.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor award the lease privilege to the actual stall occupants in accordance with the provisions of the Market Code. The Market Supervisor should cause the immediate cancelation of Certificate of Award/Occupancy of those awardees who sub-leased their stalls in accordance with Article V, Section 11 of the Code.

We recommended that the Market Inspector and the Terminal Manager conduct inspection of business establishments and stalls to ensure collection of Mayor’s Permit fees and surcharges in compliance with the provisions of the Revised Revenue Code.

We recommended that a) the Market Committee maintain permanent record of stallholders and inventory of properties in the RPM for monitoring purposes; b) the Market Supervisor strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the operations of the public market and penalize all violators in accordance with Article V, Section 11 of the Code; and c) the Municipal Mayor explore feasible means, in view of funding constraints, to appropriate funds for the repairs and maintenance of market structures and facilities and provision of personnel stationed at the MRF.

We recommended that a) the Terminal Manager maintain complete records of business owners/vendors and inventory of properties in the RTT for monitoring purposes; and b) the Municipal Mayor ensure that funds are allocated for the repairs and maintenance of RTT structures and facilities for the convenience and personal necessities of the travelling public and the transport sector.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor demand payment from R.C. Tagala Construction for the cost deficiency of ₱2,418,559.75 and liquidated damages of ₱439,716.73, otherwise, concerned officials can be held personally liable for the amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and schedules.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Acting Municipal Engineer submit the Certificate of Final Acceptance issued by the agency to the contractor and the authenticated copy of the warranty security for structural defects and failures as required under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the concerned contractors of the aforementioned projects to (i) repair the defects and other installed items or unperformed works noted totaling ₱9,076,618.66 or deduct the amount of the cost deficiencies from any money claim or retention fee due them or other securities posted by them.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer observe strictly the guidelines under DPWH DO No. 197 on the preparation of ABC.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund by fast-tracking the project implementation of on-going projects, reviewing those that may no longer be utmost priority and reprogramming the same to other more important PPAs economically beneficial to the constituents; and b) the Municipal Development Council and the MPDC closely monitor and strongly pursue the timely and full implementation of all PPAs and regularly submit status reports to the Municipal Mayor so that appropriate measures can be instituted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMC, the MDRRMO and other personnel concerned to: (a) update the DRRM Plan with the inclusion of a Business Continuity Plan; and (b) explore feasible means, in view of funding constraints, to include in the LDRRMFIP the provision of additional trainings to DRRM personnel, procurement of the aforementioned lacking standby/stock of food packs and hygiene kits, equipment and tools to comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) ensure that adequate funds are provided for the provision of water supply and the construction of walls for the equipment section, as a minimum, and fence for the project site to provide security and proper care and maintenance of all components of CFBW; and b) require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the installation of power supply to the composting facility so that it could start its operations.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) improve in establishing clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda. The GFPS-TWG should also provide justification for all variances in the GAD AR to facilitate proper evaluation of PPAs.

We recommend that the Municipality enhance its compliance with RA 9003 by:
instituting its monitoring mechanism to fully comply and address the root cause of non-compliance of various requirements of R.A. 9003.
coordinating efforts as to  integration of relevant costs for the implementation of the SWMP  for consideration in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan.
implementing waste segregation at source and create a perimeter fence around the disposal site to implement the minimum considerations under Section 39, Article 6 of RA No. 9003 in the proper establishment and maintenance of a controlled dumpsite.
putting the Materials Recovery Facility into operation to receive, sort, process, and store compostable and recyclable material efficiently and in an environmentally sound manner.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should address the root cause of the difference by conducting reconciliation of balances between reciprocal accounts Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balance.

We recommend that the Municipality enhance its compliance on setting-up Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable by:
updating the RPT Account Registers/Taxpayer’s index cards and submit the corresponding duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables; and
submitting on time to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers.

We recommend that the Municipality enhance its reporting compliance by:
instituting monitoring control on the officials and employees concerned on prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made; and
addressing the root cause of delay by directing the concerned personnel to submit copies of duly perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five (5) working days from perfection thereof for review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that management institute control mechanism by:
suspending the salary of the accountable officers who are still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate their cash advances for one  (1) year and over as of December 31, 2018; and
requesting for an authority to write-off cash advance that satisfies compliance of the requisites embodied by the rules.

We recommend that the municipality enhance its GAD Focal Point System by:
conducting gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the municipality in terms of GAD compliance;
Creating capacity building for GFPS to have in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD,  such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and
to revisit and comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 and other provisions of law.

We recommend that Management will implement and to effect reconciliation of records between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to address the root cause of unreconciled amount in their respective books and to prepare the necessary adjustment to reconcile balances per Cashbook and Subsidiary Ledger.

We recommend that the Municipality address the root cause of long outstanding accounts payable and availability of its supporting documents by communicating to the claimants on the status and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts once verified and found to be without valid claims, as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommend that Management to:
prioritize and implement the continuing projects of 20% Development Fund and require the Municipal Development Council to identify projects, programs and activities that are workable and implementable within the year to get maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials;
institute control mechanism to address the root cause of delay or non-implementation; and
enhance monitoring control to facilitate timely corrective measure during the implementation cycle of the projects, programs and activities.

We recommend that the Municipality should make a detailed review of the accounts with inappropriate balances, determine the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances.  Henceforth, exercise prudence in recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommend that Management to:
Direct the LDRRMO to facilitate local emergency responses through the implementation of P/P/As to reduce the level of risk or effects of disasters and geared towards the prevention of loss of life and reduction of loss of property brought by natural and human-induced disaster.
institute control mechanism to address the root cause of delay or non-implementation of Projects, Programs and Activities under the LDRRM Investment Plan; and
enhance monitoring control to facilitate timely corrective measure during the implementation cycle of the projects, programs and activities.

We recommend that the Municipality should address root cause long outstanding receivable by:
ascertaining  correctness and possibility of collection and exert effort which includes sending demand letters to persons who have known address; and
preparing request for write-off for account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
We recommend that Municipality direct the persons responsible in the issuance of Purchase Orders to include all the pertinent data to facilitate proper monitoring on the delivery of procured goods.

Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for failure to fully utilize the 70% LDRRMF; and
Effectively, efficiently and economically implement the priority PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular                No. 2012-73.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the LDRRMC to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 6.5 of NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and Sections 4.4, 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management submit valid written justification on the realignment/reprogramming made. Otherwise, administrative and/or civil sanctions shall be instituted against the persons held liable. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provision of Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 on the use of appropriated funds and savings.

Submit the needed documents for the evaluation of delivered heavy equipments: dumptrucks, hydraulic excavator and bulldozer pursuant to Annexes F & G of COA Circular 2009-001 as follows:
Certificate of Exclusive Distributorship, if applicable
Performance/Quality Test Result ( in good condition/operational)
Copy of Brochures/Manuals
Submit other needed documents, to wit:
Results of Test Analysis
Such other documents like:
c.2.1.  Certificate of Registration from Land Transportation Office
c.2.2.  Warranty Certificate
Authority from the Good Fortune International, Inc. for Mr. Edgar Torrecampo and Ms. Rona Ampane to receive payment, sign the Disbursement Vouchers and issue Official Receipt.
Submit Company ID of Ms. Rona Ampane, as applicable.
Henceforth, Management is directed to comply strictly with the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184, Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular 2012-001 and COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to the Audit Team for review/validation; and
Henceforth, monitor GAD activities and require the GAD Focal Person to perform constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report and submit a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in strict compliance with Sections 4.8 and 5.2 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW JC No. 2004-1 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

In view thereof, we recommended the following:
Direct the City Administrator to review LEE activities to ensure profitability and sustainability of its operations so that quality services could be assured to benefit the public.
Prepare updated Lease Rental Agreement with tenants based on realistic rates to protect the interest of both parties.
Procure the needed facilities to ensure desirable services to its clients by utilizing the appropriated funds for Capital Outlay of LEEs.
Acquire equipment and improve the LEE’s facilities as contemplated in             Section 5.1 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 Manual on the Setting-up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises that profits and income derived from LEE operation shall be applied to following priorities.
First, cost of improvement, repair and related expenses of the LEE;
Second, return of advances received by the LEE to the General Fund (GF); and
Any excess shall form part of the GF.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted;
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:
Creation of Inventory Committee
A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.
Property Identification and Coding
Government property shall be properly labeled and identified by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.
Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.
Inventory Reports
The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.
Direct the City GSO to secure and submit Certificate of Land Title on real properties and Certificate of Registration on motor vehicles, dump trucks and heavy equipment in the name of the City of Roxas on all assets acquired during the year and taken up in the books to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.

We recommended that management:
Direct the City Accountant to initiate the write-off of dormant accounts and undertake the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in Sections 6.0, 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005;
Conduct periodic monitoring and analysis for issuance of demand letter and initiate write-off if the borrowers are insolvent, cannot be located and dead; and
Pending application/approval of request for write-off, appropriate disclosure should be made on the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to:
Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts; and
Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a valid written justification on the charging made to 20% DF, which could have been a valid expenditure to Capital Outlay of the General Fund; and
Refrain from formulating programs, projects and activities which are not among those enumerated in Section 3.0 DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Human Resource Officer to review the staffing requirements of each department and recommend hiring of qualified personnel for regular appointment, if necessary, to regulate the employment of personnel under Job Order; and
Hire only JO personnel in accordance with the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs and assess the individual  assignments of all job order personnel to avoid redundant and duplicating work accomplishments.

We recommended that:

   a. OIC-Municipal Accountant should  serve  a  final  written  notice  and
      demand settlement      to personnel  with unliquidated  cash  advances
      aged 3 years and above;


   b. Request for write-off from the books,  in  case  diligent  efforts  to
      enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances  prove  futile,  after
      complying with the requirements of COA  Circular  No.  2016-005  dated
      December 19, 2016; and
   c. OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer,
      should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and  regulations
      in granting cash advances.


   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-02 (2018) dated January 17, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   The OIC- Municipal Accountant will deduct cash  advances  in  installment
   basis thru payroll deduction.

We recommended the following:


   a. Require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to send a written  notice  to  the
      payee at least one month before a check become stale, reminding him of
      the existence of the outstanding check;


   b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to  evaluate  outstanding  checks
      aging  above  six  months  which  are  considered   stale   per   Bank
      Reconciliation Statements as of October 31, 2018  and  make  necessary
      adjustments and prepare JEV to record  the  transactions  as  Accounts
      Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New  Government
      Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I;


   c. Require the OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to  coordinate  with  the  ICO-
      Municipal  Treasurer  in  the  preparation  of   Bank   Reconciliation
      statements so that any reconciling  items  can  be  taken-up   in  the
      books; and


   d. Maintain a separate Subsidiary  Ledger  to  facilitate  processing  of
      future claims.


   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-03 (2018) dated January 29, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   There was a partial adjustment made  for  stale  checks  and  will  fully
   implement in          CY 2019.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant:


   a. Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in  Annex  G  which
      are already overdue; and



   b. Henceforth, strictly comply Sections 70 and 73 of the  Manual  on  the
      NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-
      006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records
      of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-01 (2018) dated January 17, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   The  OIC-  Municipal  Accountant   will   strictly   comply   the   audit
   recommendations in CY 2019.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Section 6.7  of  COA
   Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and COA Circular  No.  2012-
   001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that all unexpended  balances  in  the
   Trust Fund will be remitted  back  to  the  source  national  government
   agencies or reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the  General  Fund
   as soon as the project has been completed.


   Henceforth, review and monitoring of status of funds received from other
   government agencies be regularly performed so that  appropriate  actions
   thereon could be made.


   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-04 (2018) dated January 17, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   Checks was already issued in February 2019 for the refund  of  unexpended
   balance to various NGAs.

We recommended that Management:

   a. Direct the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to prepare statement of  accounts
      for the Lessees, and require to pay the corresponding  unpaid  monthly
      rentals and penalty/ies before any renewal of the  contract  in  their
      favor;


   a. Require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to monitor strictly the collection
      of market rentals; and


   b. Strictly enforce compliance with the terms and  conditions  stipulated
      in the Lease contract.


   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-05 (2018) dated January 30, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   The LCE will call a meeting  with  all  stall  owners  concerned.  Demand
   letters was issued  and some stall lessor paid their obligation  in  Feb.
   2019.

We recommended that:


   a.  Property Officer prepare and submit Property Card, IIRUP and  WMR  to
      the           OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to  facilitate  dropping  of
      demolished Public Market from the agency’s books of accounts.


   b.   Create  Disposal  Committee  to  ensure  proper  disposal  of  LGU’s
property.


   c.   Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in  the  preparation  of  the
reports.

   d.  Henceforth, strictly comply laws, rules and regulations pertaining to
      disposal of government property.


   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-06 (2018) dated February 12, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   The Municipal Engineer has coordinated with  COA-TAS  Region  VI  in  the
   preparation of Waste Material Report for the demolished public market.

We recommended that Management:


   1. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct per department/office annual
     physical  inventory  of  the  Municipality’s  PPE  and  prepare/certify
     correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be approved by the Municipal Mayor;


      2.    Submit the same inventory report to the  Audit  Team  not  later
     than January 31 of   each year; and


      3. Reconciled RPCPPE with the accounting records  and  make  necessary
     adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.


   The  observation  and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered   by
                     AOM No. 2019-07 (2018) dated February 13, 2019.


   Management Comment:


   The head of the Inventory Committee already  prepared  the  consolidation
   report for submission to Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officers and employees with unliquidated cash advance to submit the liquidation documents to the Accountant for recording in the books and refund the excess balance, if any, to fully settle their outstanding accounts. Also, instruct the Accountant to monitor closely the liquidation of cash advances granted to Accountable Officers within the prescribed period to ensure prompt settlement of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Employees and Officials concerned liquidate and refund the cash advances

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track the construction of Sanitary Land Fill to ensure healthy environment and sanitation in the locality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already have a location for the sanitary landfill and constructed cell one.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the concerned responsible officer to submit a copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents and to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Only contracts pertaining to development projects were submitted to the auditor’s office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit copies to the Audit Team Leader for verification. The accountable forms should be strictly monitored and enforce remittance of collections before issuance of subsequent accountable forms to the Revenue Collectors.
observations.managementAction: The management already made the a RAAF

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Project Engineer to comply immediately with the recommendations of the Technical Audit Specialists to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management already comply with the recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  (a) ensure that collections of the collectors are within the maximum cash accountability allowed in their fidelity bond, if possible coordinate with the Bureau of Treasury for the increase in their fidelity bonds; (b) attach stubs to the RCDs to substantiate toll fee collections and refrain from issuing accountable forms to non-bonded employees pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445, Section 305(f) of RA No. 7160, Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V, EO No 292 and Bureau of Treasury, Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 5, 2009. Likewise, ensure that non-bonded employees are properly supervise by the bonded employee and install control measures in the collection of the non-bonded employees; (c) require the Liquidating Officer to verify whether the collections pertaining the used and all issued accountable forms are remitted before new series of receipts will be issued to collectors; and (d) submit comments/justification on the above noted observation in the collection and deposit, and issuance and use of accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of the issuance and remittance of accountable forms were carefully check by the personnel in-charge in the Treasury Office.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to assigned to collect toll fees and attach the stubs to support the collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Disbursing Officer provide proof of liquidation of the cash advances outstanding in the accounting record totaling P226,871.71 to the Municipal Accountant in order to facilitate reconciliation of the record; b. Municipal Accountant (i) require the officers and employees concerned to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period; (ii) ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,1997 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the complete documentation of liquidation for cash advances; (iii) avoid granting of cash advances to job order and contract of service personnel with no employer-employee relationship to avoid the possibility of non-liquidation in case of non-renewal of contract/job-order; (iv) exert effort to determine the details of the Due from NGOs/POs account; and (v) submit all the lacking documents relative the liquidation of cash advances of the Municipal officials and employees; c. Municipal Mayor temporarily discontinue granting livelihood assistance/ loans to its constituents until the Municipality has adopted an efficient and aggressive plan regarding the collections of past due accounts and has come-up with a well-established policy on the livelihood assistance program; and d. Municipal Treasurer refer to the subsidiary ledger maintained by the Accounting Office to prepare the list of debtors with outstanding loans from the livelihood assistance program, know their whereabouts and serve formal demand letters to facilitate the collections of the past due accounts.
observations.managementAction: A demand has been sent to concerned employees. A commitment letter to liquidate will be given to employees. Giving out financial assistance for livelihood and loans were discontinued. However, for existing payees who are in good standing, they are given a chance to continue.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Office, proper monitoring of cash advances was not made. Likewise, they failed to demand the outstanding cash advances from employees who are already not in government service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Designated-GSO and Inventory Team initially conduct the physical count of property and equipment in every office to facilitate the complete inventory of all property and equipment under the General and Special Education Funds, and submit the Inventory Report to the Municipal Accountant and to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year; (b) Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer (i) allot time and effort to coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of properties; and (ii) prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE account; (c) Municipal Accountant (i) analyze the nature of transactions and make an adjustment to properly classify the items to an appropriate account; and (ii) transfer the completed projects funded by TF to GF, and depreciate the same to properly value the PPE of the Municipality; (d) GSO-Designate (i) remove the expendable and semi-expendable properties from the RPCPPE and account all determined unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of IIRUP and other required supporting documents for disposal; (ii) prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map to account for the Local Roads and reconcile with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office; and (iii) collate the documents of the land owned by the Municipality that remained unregistered in its name and process the same; (e) Municipal Engineer maintain road map and provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; (f) Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the local road network to determine the current value of the road components to facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; and (g) Municipal Assessor and GSO-Designate collate pertinent documents to facilitate the transfer of title of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to preserve its legal rights over them.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and the office of the Municipal Accountant are preparing their property ledger and this will be subject to reconciliation. GSO and Accountant are in close coordination for this item. A partial list of Inventory Ledger has been completed. The management is also looking for training to enhance property management and reporting. GSO and accounting will conduct Inventory count at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel to do the task, GSO-Designate still failed to conduct physical inventory of all its PPE and prepare and submit the required RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Engineer provide explanation on how the project proceeded without preparing the required DED; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) be guided by the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 regarding the proper classification of accounts, debit appropriate construction in progress account pending completion and recognize the corresponding asset account upon completion; and (ii) return the materials and labor for the installation of streetlights to expense account or appropriate repair and maintenance in conformity with the new chart of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009; (c) BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the documents needed to validate the compliance of the Municipality with the provisions of the DILG MC No. 2016-49 dated April 6, 2016 and all the lacking documents and provide sufficient justification on the above noted observations in compliance with pertinent provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act.
observations.managementAction: For the current year and onwards, CIP account is being used. The unutilized balance is already liquidated. Set meeting with COA for proper adjustment of previous balances. Implemented retention money to all projects and securing all warranty certificate to suppliers for goods. Incurred. Currently gathering and collaborating with concerned department for the supporting documents needed.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower to properly monitor projects and its charges and maintain appropriate records and documents. Lack of knowledge of relevant rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant (i) be guided by the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 regarding the proper classification of accounts, debit appropriate construction in progress account pending completion and recognize the corresponding asset account upon completion; and (ii) return the excess funds totalling P5,982.00 to DBM pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular no. 94-013; and (b) BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the documents needed to validate the compliance of the Municipality with the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act and all the lacking documents and provide sufficient justification on the above noted observations.
observations.managementAction: For the current year and onwards, CIP account is being used. The unutilized balance is already liquidated. Set meeting with COA for proper adjustment of previous balances. Implemented retention money to all projects and securing all warranty certificate to suppliers for goods. Incurred. Currently gathering and collaborating with concerned department for the supporting documents needed.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required lacking documents, and voluminous workload of the Accounting Office that is equally important.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant (i) be guided by the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 regarding the proper classification of accounts, debit appropriate construction in progress account pending completion and recognize the corresponding asset account upon completion; and (ii) return the excess funds totalling P36,393.88 to the source agencies; and (b) BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required lacking documents, and voluminous workload of the Accounting Office that is equally important.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal Agriculturist, BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit satisfactory explanation/justification on the observations noted in the procurement, implementation and recording of the procurement of mechanical drying facility, hand tractor and transplanter, and rehabilitation of irrigation facility, and (b) Municipal Accountant demand Rose Petal Commercial & Computer Center to pay liquidated damages amounting to  P245,260.80 for late delivery of mechanical dryer, generator set and sewing machine; (ii) refund to the source agency the unexpended balance; and (ii) submit all the lacking supporting documents for audit purpose.
observations.managementAction: Meeting with Bukluran, supplier and LGU was done on July 7,2018. The machine was tested again and the supplier made a commitment to train the personnel of Bukluran in operating the machine. A follow up on monitoring and close coordination with Bukluran and the supplier are being implemented. The unexpended balance has been liquidated and refunded to the concerned agency. Currently gathering and collaborating with concerned department for the supporting documents needed.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required lacking documents, and voluminous workload of the Accounting Office that is equally important.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman and Municipal Agriculturist submit (i) satisfactory explanations on the observations noted in the procurement and distribution of fishing gears and paraphernalia; and (ii) all the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required lacking documents, and voluminous workload of the Accounting Office that is equally important.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) cause the refund of the concerned Municipal officials and employees of the excess payment of travelling expenses totaling P104,688.44; and (ii) submit acceptable justification on the noted observations regarding the claim for traveling expenses of the officials and employees of the Municipal Government. Henceforth, comply with the provision of Section 4 of EO No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 in preparing the claim for traveling expenses and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant asserted that the excess per diems claimed were necessary to the performance and completion of the employee’s assignment considering the distance of Roxas and travel time going to Manila/Airport, thus it is fair and just to claim the excess per diems. Henceforth, comply with the provision of Section 4 of EO No.298 dated March 23, 2004 in preparing the claim for traveling expenses and COA Circular No.2012-001 for the complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Municipality failed to effect the refund of the excess travel claims due to lack of available money of the concerned accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal School Board and Municipal Budget Officer follow the provision of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in preparing the SEF budget; and (b) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant require the concerned DepEd personnel to submit satisfactory justifications on the observations.
observations.managementAction: The district supervisor submitted justification dated June 5, 2018. She explained that the Roxas District Office is actually the office of the coordinating principal where the district supervisor meets with the school heads and process all the documents needed by the 38 schools. The noted charges in the name of the district office were errors admitted by the same as the old template were still used in making vouchers and proposals.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to prepare the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the establishment of MRF in their respective Barangays; (b) through the MENRO-Designate, comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels and by collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated; (c) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003; and (d) strictly implement Municipal Ordinance No. 01, series of 2018.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to enact the Environment Code of Municipality of Roxas, Oriental Mindoro on June 18, 2018. Moreover, its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission on December 6, 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to consistently monitor wastes in public areas and establish MRF in all areas of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) HRMO and Municipal Budget Officer justify the grant of overtime pay to the job order and contract of service personnel of the municipality who were not in the plantilla position such as the contractual and casual employees; (b) Concerned department to justify the necessity of the overtime services rendered as presented above; and (c) Municipal Accountant to submit Overtime Work Program and Quantified Overtime accomplishment of the employees who rendered and paid overtime pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Based on the justification of the Human Resource Management Officer II dated June 28, 2018, the grant of overtime pay for the Job Orders and Contract of Service was due to the simultaneous development programs/projects and voluminous routinary duties since the existing organizational structure that the LGU is using was the one approved way back 1999.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel to collate documents for the overtime claim, the same were not submitted to this Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant regularly prepare and submit the Statement of Cash Flows, Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity, Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts for the year then ended pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 and COA Circular No. 2016-004. Likewise, prepare subsidiary records to support the controlling records and to be able to prepare supporting schedules of the accounts in the financial statements, and ensure that year-end reports be submitted within 60-days from December 31 of each year for the timely verification the said statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the required financial reports.
observations.reason: Subsidiary records of some controlling accounts was not prepared due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Local Development Council, Implementing Office and MPDC in the planning stage (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and the climate or weather factor in stating the period of implementation of the PPAs; (ii) provide specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for 20% Development Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; and (iii) evaluate the viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; (b) Municipal Engineer and the concerned implementing department heads: (i) periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, (ii) submit a list of all completed projects to support the reprogramming to other developmental projects or reversion to the unappropriated surplus; and (iii) specify the planned implementation period of projects under the Annual Investment Plan to serve as a guide for the effective implementation of the projects; (c) Municipal Budget Officer and MPDC provides justification on the discrepancies noted in the projects planned in the AIP vis-à-vis the project funded in the Annual Budget; and (d) Management adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 7160 and ensure that there is a corresponding Municipal Development Council Resolution for every Sangguniang Bayan Resolution enacted to effect changes in the appropriations under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Close coordination with concerned units for proper and effective implementation of programs. Transferring of unimplemented capital outlay is discontinued. Proper accounting and monitoring is being implemented. Proper status reporting with MPDC, budget and accounting officers.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower to monitor the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) MPDC and MDRRMO provide (i) specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; (ii) specific period of implementation of the PPAs of MDRRM; (b) Municipal Accountant provide justification on the discrepancies between the general ledger of the Trust Liability-DRRM account and the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund; (c) MDRRM Officer evaluate the PPAs in the continuing appropriation and special trust fund and take appropriate actions thereon by reprogramming them to another MDRRM PPAs; and (d) MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman fast track the implementation of the PPAs under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented. Henceforth, ensure that all projects appropriated in MDRRM Fund be implemented during the year it was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO prepared and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to this Office. MDRRMO ensure that all the programs/projects for disaster be implemented during the year to ascertain the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity. Transferring of unimplemented capital outlay is discontinued. Proper accounting and monitoring is being implemented.
observations.reason: Close coordination with concerned units for proper and effective implementation of programs. Transferring of unimplemented capital outlay is discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Management require the immediate refund of excess payment of RATA to the Municipal Treasurer and the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees amounting P17,902.50 and double claim of TA amounting to P3,400.00; The Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management Officer instruct their staff in-charge in the preparation and review of payroll for RATA to consider actual work performance and ensure appropriate supporting documents were attached as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001; and The Municipal Accountant and MBO explain the variances noted between the paid payrolls and DVs, SAAOB and TB for CY 2015.
observations.managementAction: As informed by the Municipal Accountant, officials and employees had already refunded the excess RATA. The double claim was also determined due to consideration of double claim of TA amounting to P3,400.00
observations.reason: Supporting documents were not submitted, and the variance noted was still not explained by the Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Municipal Health Officer prepares the internal guidelines for approval of the Mayor of the specific criteria, administrative procedures, and standards on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to all entitled PHWs; The Municipal Health Officer instruct her staff in –charge in the preparation of the payroll to consider the actual exposure of public health workers to high and low risk hazards in preparing the claim of hazard pay pursuant to No.7.1 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1, series of 2012 dated November 29, 2012; and The Municipal Accountant submit the documents supporting the paid payrolls for the CY 2015 of the MHO in order to evaluate the propriety of the full grant of these allowances; otherwise these will be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the DTR of the MHO personnel.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to collate the documents needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Municipal Health Officer prepare the internal guidelines for approval of the Mayor of the specific criteria, administrative procedures, and standards on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to all entitled PHWs; The Municipal Health Officer instruct her staff in –charge in the preparation of the payroll to consider the actual exposure of public health workers to high and low risk hazards in preparing the claim of hazard pay pursuant to No.7.1 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1, series of 2012 dated November 29, 2012; and The Municipal Accountant submit the documents supporting the paid payrolls for the CY 2015 of the MHO in order to evaluate the propriety of the full grant of these allowances; otherwise these will be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the DTR of the MHO personnel.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to collate the documents needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor regularly serve written notice to all accountable officers and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if necessary.

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant must ensure that each provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted effort in serving demand letters to all accountable officers who have outstanding cash advances as at current date.
observations.reason:
A significant amount of cash advance still remained unliquidated aged over 5 years.

To ensure full implementation, this observation is reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and GSO to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: Management required the concerned officials to immediately identify those infrastructure assets for transfer to the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation between the result of the inventory of road networks and RPI is still on-going.

To ensure full implementation, this observation is reiterated in Finding No. 4 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) during the preparation of the annual budget to include only developmental PPAs which are feasible, specific and properly identified as to target date of execution and the official in-charge responsible for the implementation. The MDC should also coordinate with the Municipal Engineer so that PPAs are supported with program of works, plans and specifications to ensure optimum fund utilization and timely and efficient project implementation. 

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), together with the MDC, should coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of PPAs at the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan and properly render a report to the Municipal Mayor the status of every project, problems encountered and recommendations to address such problem.
observations.managementAction: The identified PPAs were either fully implemented or their implementation commenced in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Only 21 out of 41 PPAs or 51.22% programmed under the 20% Development Fund for CY 2018 were fully implemented during the year, as discussed in Finding No. 15 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and the BAC Secretariat strictly abide by the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS of all procurement opportunities, notices, awards and other related information, in compliance with Sections 8.2.1(a), 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management assured that it will require the members of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the said regulations. Management also assured that it will appropriate funds to upgrade its system for faster and efficient internet connection in the various offices of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Posting of all NOA and NTP for projects awarded in CY 2018 was still not fully complied based on validation in the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and the corresponding monthly monitoring report are submitted within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015. The monthly monitoring report should contain details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date duly reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Management required all the concerned officials and employees to strictly adhere with the said regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the required forms and reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind aids/donations for DRRM programs are maintained and submitted by the Property and Supply Unit, in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The GSO partially submitted the required forms and reports for CY 2018.
observations.reason: Monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/ utilization/ issuance of in-kind donations were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC, with the assistance of the GFPS, hasten the preparation of the Municipality’s GAD database, regularly update the same, and conduct gender analysis to serve as basis for addressing gender issues and concerns in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years, pursuant to the provisions of the Philippine Commission on Women, the DILG, the DBM and the National Economic and Development Authority JMC No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Management required the concerned officials and employees in the implementation of GAD Programs to exert its diligent effort to establish an updated GAD database.
observations.reason: The CBMS, CLUP and Annual Health Report are being used as basis in addressing gender issues. Updating of GAD is still on-going.
