observations.recommendation:
Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for the minimal implementation of its PPAs; and

Efficiently implement the priority PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.

Require the City Local Risk Reduction and Management Officer to hasten the formulation of the DRRM Implementing Plan and Manual of Operations that would provide the mechanism, rules and procedures and assessment and performance monitoring of the disaster risk reduction management and operation of the city government.

Require the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month duly certified by the City Accountant in conformity with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Require the SWM Board:

To revise the 10-Year SWM Plan and to incorporate in the CY’s AIP the following PPAs, to wit:

a.1Solid Waste Reduction/Recy-cling/Composting

a.1.1 Monitoring and implementation programs/activities for:

•	proper segregation of wastes and maintenance of storages/re-ceptacles for 
  Anti-Littering Law and No Plastic Policy;
•	Barangay based Compos-ting Centers;
•	Estab-lishment of barangay based Material Recovery Facilities and Barangay Processing Centers;

a.1.2 Repair/Reha-bilitation of damaged composting facilities; and

a.1.3 Purchase of a new composting facility which could have better  processing capacity.

a.2 Collection

a.2.1 Intensify SWM Collection activities to assess compliance of solid waste collection routes with schedules and targeted areas.
a.2.2 Provision of necessary training to collectors and  personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste. 

a.2.3 Direct the Project Coordinator of SWM to approve the Drivers Trip Ticket on the basis of gasoline consumption for the day of the particular area to be served based on approved garbage collection route and as scheduled to be attached to the Monthly Report of Official Travel of Motor Vehicles and Fuel Consumption Report.

a.3 Transfer 

Program for the establishment of Transfer Stations pursuant to Section 25, Article III of RA 9003. 

a.4 Disposal

Post-closure plan for the safe closure of Sibaguan Dumpsite and rehabilitation of the controlled dump site indicating the time frame and financial requirements.

Direct the Solid Waste Management Board to formulate concrete measures and guidelines as basis for the implementation of PPAs in all components of SWM particularly in solid reduction, collection, monitoring and evaluation, such as, but not limited to the following:
	
b.1Intensify stricter implementation of segregation of solid waste at the barangay level;

b.2Create a monitoring and evaluation committee to assess the implementation  effectiveness and efficiency of SWM; 

Direct the GSO to properly assess the capacity of eight (8) garbage trucks if it can effectively carry out the previous year’s 17 collection routes per SWM Plan, if not, design and come up with thorough collection measures of waste based on realistic schedules to improve delivery of basic service to its clientele; and

Direct the Budget Officer to propose budgetary allocations for all PPAs identified in the SWM Plan for an effective program implementation, such as, but not limited on the following:

d.1Solid waste collection, specifically for the following:

•	Lack of Garbage Truck
•	Fuel, Gasoline and Oil
•	Repairs and Maintenance of transportation equipment
•	Other General Services

d.2Solid waste disposal, particularly the procurement of machineries to  complement the need in the new Sanitary Landfill.

Refrain from including expenses that cannot be charged to the GAD Budget as provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and

Give priority to emergency and/or continuing issues and concerns in identifying GAD PPAs giving emphasis to gender issues that can effectively addressed by GAD interventions  following the standards laid down in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, and other applicable guidelines on GAD in the agency’s PPAs.

Require the Roxas City Council for the Protection of Children to:

a. 	Regularly monitor the status on the implementation of PPAs; and 

b. 	Enforce the implementation of PPAs in accordance with specified time frame per Annual Work and Financial Plan to ensure the full protection of children against all forms violence and overall development of children to their fullest potential.

a. 	Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year in compliance with  Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 which prescribes the Rules and Regulations on  Supply and Property Management in the LGUs; 

b. 	Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.

Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)

An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.

Inventory Reports

The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.

c. 	Reconcile the inventory reports with the accounting and property records; and

d. 	Direct the City GSO to secure and submit Certificate of Land Title on real properties and Certificate of Registration (CR) on motor vehicles in the name of the City of Roxas on all assets taken up in the books to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.

a. 	Require the Human Resource Officer to review the staffing requirements of each department and recommend hiring of qualified personnel for regular appointment, if necessary, to regulate the employment of personnel under Job Order; and

b. 	Hire only JO personnel in accordance to the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs and assess the individual  assignments of all job order personnel to avoid redundant and duplicating work accomplishments.

We recommended that OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the claims for RATA should be properly reviewed and are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended the following:
Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank;
Make representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:
Demand the return of P300,000.00 or whatever bank balance of PROMEMCO and be deposited in the trust account of the Municipality;
Request for write-off from the books, in case efforts to enforce settlement of Loan Receivable prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
Make necessary adjusting entries to adjust the Accounts affected in case the request for write-off will be granted.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with Section 2.2 and Sub-sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, to ensure that the public is informed of any subsequent projects that will be implemented for transparency and accountability and also to encourage public participation.

We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof. Furthermore, Management should also explore options in obtaining lot which could be used as site for the construction of MRF.

We recommended that:
Stop issuing check in the name of the School Principal-in-charge of the District;
Disbursement of SEF which can be directly paid to the payees should not be granted as Financial Assistance to the School Principal-in-charge rather the LGU will undertake the disbursement; and
OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements against Special Education Fund should be properly reviewed and are supported with complete documentation in order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that Management:
Follow-up the release of the remaining budget with the Department of Agriculture to fully implement the Vermi Composting Facility and facilitate the utilization of the project.
Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under fund transfers from NGAs.

We recommended that Management:
Make representation with the LGU Chief to immediately hire Crisis Center manager to facilitate the maintenance and operation of the center; and
GAD Focal Person should closely monitor and supervise the implementation of PAPs, as required by the PCW and strictly implement all programs, activities, and projects embodied in the Municipalities approved Annual Investment Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the GAD Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to facilitate the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU as well as the establishment of GAD Code to support the LGUs efforts; and
Direct the GAD Focal Person to assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be in place.

observations.managementAction: No action

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor.

The Municipal Treasurer should maintain property cards for PPE with complete information. These property cards should be reconciled with other property records like Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE).

The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property subsidiary ledgers contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that monthly depreciation is recognized in the books so that the cost of all the PPEs are allocated over the estimated useful life of the assets.

In case of loss of property, the officer immediately accountable thereof should file an application for relief from accountability. The request should be accompanied by the documents required under Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Management through the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer committed to submit the status of the inventory count before the end of the current year.
observations.reason: Management had not yet submitted the status of its inventory count. Moreover, the incomplete inventory report and the absence of property cards hinder proper reconciliation between the physical count of PPE and accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to require the concerned officials to identify those infrastructure assets that should be transferred to the appropriate PPE accounts.
observations.reason: The concerned officials had not yet submitted the corresponding reports as PPE reconciliation is still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the Notes to FS the (a) changes between the original and final budgets; (b) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (c) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management ensures that all material changes in the original budget and financial statements shall be properly disclosed at the end of the year in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed by PPSAS no. 24.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not provide additional disclosures for CY 2016 and 2017 financial statements.

Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.

Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)

Inventory Reports

Reconcile the inventory reports with the accounting and property records.

We recommended that Management require the City Local Risk Reduction and Management Officer to hasten the formulation of the DRRM Implementing Plan and Manual of Operations that would provide the mechanism, rules and procedures and assessment and performance monitoring of the disaster risk reduction management and operation of the city government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  all
       concerned officers and employees with unliquidated cash  advance  to
       submit the liquidation documents to the Accountant for recording  in
       the books and refund the excess balance, if  any,  to  fully  settle
       their outstanding accounts. Also, instruct the Accountant to monitor
       closely the liquidation of  cash  advances  granted  to  Accountable
       Officers within the prescribed period to ensure prompt settlement of
       accounts.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer   (a)  ensure   that
collections of the collectors are within  the  maximum  cash  accountability
allowed in their fidelity bond, if possible coordinate with  the  Bureau  of
Treasury for the increase in their fidelity bonds; (b) attach stubs  to  the
RCDs  to  substantiate  toll  fee  collections  and  refrain  from   issuing
accountable forms to non-bonded employees pursuant  to  Section  101  of  PD
1445, Section 305(f) of RA No. 7160, Section  50,  Chapter  9,  Subtitle  B,
Book V, EO No 292 and Bureau of  Treasury,  Treasury  Circular  No.  02-2009
dated August  5,  2009.  Likewise,  ensure  that  non-bonded  employees  are
properly supervise by the bonded employee and install  control  measures  in
the collection of the non-bonded  employees; (c)  require  the  Liquidating
Officer to verify whether  the  collections  pertaining  the  used  and  all
issued accountable forms are remitted before new series of receipts will  be
issued to collectors; and (d) submit  comments/justification  on  the  above
noted observation in the collection and deposit, and  issuance  and  use  of
accountable forms.

We  reiterated  our  previous  recommendation  that   the   Municipal
Accountant prioritize the preparation of Bank reconciliation Statements  for
all funds by assigning one employee that will focus on the analysis of  bank
statements  and  accounting  records  and  preparation  bank  reconciliation
statements, and submit the same to this office. Once the  reconciling  items
were identified and analyzed,  make  the  necessary  adjustments  pertaining
thereto.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant (i) demand  for  the
liquidation of cash advances outstanding as of  December  31,  2017  of  the
erring accountable officer, if they fail to liquidate  their  cash  advances
after demand,  withholding  of  their  salary  for  payment  of  their  cash
advances can be instituted, pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No.  97-
002; (ii) ensure that only permanent employees of the  Municipal  Government
will be given cash advances; (iii) adjust the  differences  of  balances  of
cash advance in the RUCA and General;  and  (iv)  ensure  strict  compliance
with the provisions of COA Circular No. No. 97-002; (b) Municipal Mayor  (i)
revisit the policy on the granting of livelihood assistance/ loans  and  set
an efficient  and  aggressive  plan  regarding  the  collections  and  well-
established policy on the livelihood assistance program;  and  (ii)  require
the Municipal Accountant  and  Municipal  Treasurer  to  work  together  and
exhaust all the available resources to  collate  the  names  and  amount  of
outstanding loan of the defaulted debtors and issue  formal  demand  letters
requiring them to settle their obligations immediately.  If  the  effort  is
vain,  the  Municipal  Mayor  may  file  a  request  for  write-off  to  the
Commission on Audit, taking into consideration the  guidelines  provided  by
COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal  Mayor  create  and  direct  the
Inventory  Team  to  conduct  the  inventory  of  all  property,  plant  and
equipment owned by the Municipal Government, and  reconcile  the  result  of
the inventory with  records of the  Municipal  Accountant,  and  submit  the
reconciled RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on or before  January  31  of
each year. The designated property custodians  conduct  the  physical  count
of property and  equipment  in  every  office  to  facilitate  the  complete
inventory of all PPE of the  Municipality;  (b)  Municipal  Accountant   (i)
maintain ledger cards; (ii) work back and trace the  available  records  for
PPEs acquired in prior years to be included in the existing property  ledger
cards; and (iii) record the materials and  labor  for  the  installation  of
streetlights to expense account or appropriate  repair  and  maintenance  in
conformity with the new chart of accounts as prescribed under  COA  Circular
No. 2015-009; (c) GSO designate  (i)  maintain  stock/  property  cards  and
start the preparation of   property  records  based  on  the  deliveries  of
supplies or property for CY 2017 to date on the basis of the AIR  and  other
supporting documents, (ii) account all determined  unserviceable  properties
to  facilitate  the  preparation  of  Inventory  and  Inspection  Report  of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and other required supporting  documents  for
disposal; and (iii) prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map  to  account  for
the Local Roads and reconcile with the Registry  of  Public  Infrastructures
maintained by the Accounting Office; (d)  created  Inventory  Committee  (i)
request  for  trainings  regarding  the  proper  management  of   government
property, plant and equipment and preparation of reports, if  not  possible,
ask  the  assistance  of  the  Provincial  General  Services  Office  (PGSO)
regarding this matter; (ii) conduct a complete physical count and  technical
valuation of the local road network to determine the current  value  of  the
road components to  facilitate  the  preparation  of  the  RPCLRN;  and  (e)
Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and  the  GSO  with  the
complete description and  cost  segregation  of  road  components  for  road
projects.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant make representation  with  the
bank for the reconciliation of the record of the Loans payable and make  the
necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s accounting books.

We recommended that the: (a)  Municipal Engineer  provide  explanation
on how the  project  proceeded  without  preparing  the  required  DED;  (b)
Municipal Accountant (i) be  guided  by  the  pertinent  provisions  of  COA
Circular No. 2015-009  regarding  the  proper  classification  of  accounts,

maintain appropriate records to keep track of the progress of  the  projects

Engineer submit the documents needed  to  validate  the  compliance  of  the
Municipality with the provisions of the DILG MC No. 2016-49 dated  April  6,
2016 and all the lacking documents and provide sufficient  justification  on
the above noted observations in compliance with pertinent provisions of  the
Government Procurement Reform Act.

We recommended that the  (a) Municipal Accountant  (i)  be  guided  by
the pertinent provisions of COA  Circular  No.  2015-009  regarding  the
proper  classification  of  accounts,  debit  appropriate  construction  in
progress account pending completion and recognize  the  corresponding  asset

We recommended that the (a)  Municipal  Agriculturist,  BAC  Chairman,
Municipal   Engineer   and   Municipal   Accountant   submit    satisfactory
explanation/ justification on the observations  noted  in  the  procurement,

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant,  BAC  Chairman  and
Municipal  Agriculturist  submit  (i)  satisfactory  explanations   on   the
observations noted in the procurement and distribution of fishing gears  and
paraphernalia; and (ii) all the lacking documents.  Moreover, the  Municipal
Agriculturist  immediately  distributes  the  remaining  fishing  gears  and
paraphernalia so that the targeted beneficiary may attain the  benefit  that
could be derived therefrom.

We  recommended  that   the   Municipal   Mayor   submit   acceptable
justification on the lapses noted in the  acquisition  of  1,000  sq.m.  lot
located at Brgy Paclasan, Roxas, for a  total  consideration  of  P2million.
Likewise,  the  Municipal  Accountant  submit  all  the  lacking   documents
pertaining to the procurement of land.

We recommended that the (a)  BAC  Chairman  and  Municipal  Accountant
submit acceptable explanation of the delay in the execution of contracts  in

Municipal Accountant submit satisfactory justification on  the  observations
noted and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant  (a)
submit  acceptable  justification  on  the   observations   noted   in   the
procurement  and  payment  of  the  rescue  vehicle  purchased  and  backhoe
totaling P2.68 million; (b) submit  all  the  lacking  documents  for  audit
purposes; and (c) ensure strict compliance with the provisions  of   (i)  RA
No. 9184 specifically in the posting of performance security;  (ii)  Section
74 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, inclusion of  complete
details in PO which should be printed the same with  other  details  in  the
PO; and (iii) Section 86 of PD 1445, issuance of CAF to all  procurement  of
the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) cause the  refund  of  the
concerned Municipal officials and employees  of  the  excessive  claims  for
travelling  expenses  totaling  P104,688.44;  and  (ii)  submit   acceptable
justification on the noted observations regarding the  claim  for  traveling
expenses of  the  officials  and  employees  of  the  Municipal  Government.
Henceforth, comply with the provision of Section  4  of  EO  No.  298  dated
March 23, 2004 in preparing  the  claim  for  travelling  expenses  and  COA
Circular No. 2012-001 for the complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the (a)  Municipal  School  Board  and   Municipal
Budget Officer follow the provision of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular  No.  1
s. of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in  preparing  the  SEF  budget;  and (b)
Municipal Budget Officer and  Municipal  Accountant  require  the  concerned
DepEd personnel to submit satisfactory justifications  on  the  observations
noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Liga  ng
mga Barangay,  encourage  active  participation  of  barangay  officials  to
support the solid waste management program of the  government  by  educating
their respective constituents on  the  proper  segregation  of  reusable  or
recyclable materials  from  compostable  wastes  and  the  establishment  of
Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in their respective Barangays; (b)  through
the MENRO, comply with the requirements of the law on waste  segregation  by
placing trash bins with proper labels and by collecting only  those  garbage
that are properly segregated; (c) recommend to the  Sangguniang  Bayan  (SB)
policies and possible measures that  will  ensure  full  compliance  by  the
barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; (d) initiate  the  passage  of
Anti-Littering policy ordinances as provided  by  Section  48  of  R.A.  No.
9003; and (e) see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is  properly
controlled and complied with  Rule XIII- Operation  of  Controlled  Dumpsite
of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the (a)  Municipal  Budget  Officer  allocate  the
required 1% of their IRA for LCPC programs/projects/activities  as  mandated
by Section 15 of RA No.  9344;  and  (b)  MCPC  abide  with  the  rules  and
regulations  with  regards  to  the  formulation  of  LDPC  and   LIPC   and
utilization of the 1% LCPC fund as provided by DILG Memorandum Circular  No.
2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that Municipal Budget Officer and  MSWDO  ensures  that
programs/projects/activities that address the concerns  of  senior  citizens
and persons with  disability  be  included  in  the  Annual  Budget  of  the
Municipality,  if  possible  appropriate  at   least   1%   of   the   total
appropriation pursuant to DILG MC No. 2014-59 dated May 9,  2014.  Likewise,
the MSWDO annually prepare report relative the  attainment  of  the  planned
PPAs.

We recommended that the (a) HRMO and Municipal Budget Officer  justify
the grant of  overtime  pay  to  the  job  order  and  contract  of  service
personnel of the municipality who were not in the  plantilla  position  such
as the contractual and casual employees of the municipality;  (b)  Concerned
department to justify the necessity of the  overtime  services  rendered  as
presented above; and  (c)  Municipal  Accountant  to  submit  Overtime  Work
Program  and  Quantified  Overtime  accomplishment  of  the  employees   who
rendered and paid overtime pursuant to COA Circular no. 2012-001 dated  June
14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  regularly  prepare  and
submit  the  Statement  of  Cash  Flows,  Statement  of   Changes   in   Net
Assets/Equity, Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts for  the
year then ended pursuant to of COA Accounting Circular Letter  No.  2007-002
and COA Circular No.  2016-004.  Likewise,  prepare  subsidiary  records  to
support the controlling  records  and  to  be  able  to  prepare  supporting
schedules of the accounts in the financial statements, and ensure that year-
end reports be submitted within 60-days from December 31 of  each  year  for
the timely verification the said statements.

We recommended that the (a) MPDC  and  MDRRMO  provide  (i)  specific,
measurable,  achievable,  realistic  and  time-bound   (SMART)   performance
indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily  quantify  the
attainment of the planned PPAs; and (ii) specific period  of  implementation
for which the PPAs of MDRRM will be implemented; (b)  Municipal  Accountant,
Budget Officer, Treasurer stop the practice of transferring  the  unexpended
capital outlay for MDRRMF in the General Fund to Trust Fund, since this  can
be continued in the succeeding year in the General Fund and (c)  MDRRMO  and
Municipal    Engineer    fast-tract    the     implementation     of     the
programs/projects/activities under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring  the  period
for which the PPAs will be implemented.

We  recommended  that  the  (a)  Municipal  Accountant  and  Municipal
Agriculturist submit satisfactory  explanation/  justification  on  how  the
supplier was paid even though the dryers  were  still  non-operational;  and
(b) Municipal Mayor enforce the warranties cited in the Purchase Order to
the supplier in order for the dryers to be operational  and  to  attain  the
target objective of the project.

We  recommended  that  the  (a)  Municipal  Treasurer  devise  various
strategies and activities to facilitate effective revenue collection by  (i)
assigning more personnel in delivering the  notice  of  delinquencies,  with
specific instructions to serve notices to actual owner of  the  property  or
to the actual occupants and institute follow-up actions in case  of  failure
to  reply;  (ii)  comprehensive  tax   information   campaign;   and   (iii)
integration of tax mapping process into the iTAX system.  Moreover,  provide
the  certified list of all real property tax  delinquencies   to  the  Local
Sanggunian and the  Municipal  Accounting  Office  in  accordance  with  the
relevant rules and regulations and implement remedy provided  under  Section
256 of RA No. 7160 and explore all possible remedies as  provided  under  RA
No. 7160 and relevant regulations to collect those delinquent  accounts  for
more than five years; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and  Municipal  Accountant
exert extra efforts to reconcile their records pertaining to  real  property
taxes, effect the  necessary  adjustments,  if  any  and  ensure  consistent
adjustment of real property tax receivable upon collection.

We  recommended  that  the   GAD   Focal   Person   (MSWDO)   provide
justification on why they failed to  submit their GAD Plan  and  Budget  for
CY 2017 and ensure that (i) GAD Plan and Budget  in  the  prescribed  format
for the succeeding years will be submitted to the PPDO  and  DILG-Provincial
Office, and secure DILG certificate of endorsement pursuant to  Section  5.0
(2) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular  No.  2016-01;  and  (ii)
GAD accomplishment in the prescribed format be submitted to COA  Audit  Team
five working days from the end of January of the preceding year pursuant  to
Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person.
The GSO should maintain complete and updated property cards for PPE with complete information and ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of third year after issue.
The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property subsidiary ledgers contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee.
In case of loss of property, the officer immediately accountable thereof should file an application for relief from accountability. The request should be accompanied by the documents required under Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to regularly serve written notice to all accountable officers and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if necessary.
In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant must ensure that each provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and GSO to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) during the preparation of the annual budget to include only developmental PPAs which are feasible, specific and properly identified as to target date of execution and the official in-charge responsible for the implementation. The MDC should also coordinate with the Municipal Engineer so that PPAs are supported with program of works, plans and specifications to ensure optimum fund utilization and timely and efficient project implementation.
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, together with the MDC, should coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of PPAs of the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan and properly render a report to the Municipal Mayor the status of every project, problems encountered and recommendations to address such problem.

We recommended for the BAC and the BAC Secretariat to strictly abide by the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS of all procurement opportunities, notices, awards and other related information, in compliance with Sections 8.2.1(a), 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and the corresponding monthly monitoring report are submitted within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015. The monthly monitoring report should contain details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date duly reconciled with the accounting records.
The Audit Team did not receive any response from the management relative to the above audit observations and recommendations.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the required forms and reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind aids/donations for DRRM programs are maintained and submitted by the Property and Supply Unit, in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended for the MPDC, with the assistance of the GFPS, to hasten the preparation of the Municipality’s GAD database, regularly update the same, and conduct gender analysis to serve as basis for addressing gender issues and concerns in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years, pursuant to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.

We recommended that Management:
Enhance closer monitoring activities on fund transfer and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement without delay all on-going/not started projects to attain the program’s objectives;
Direct the City Accountant to conduct regular  periodic verification and reconciliation of the unexpended balance of fund transfers Due to NGAs Account, determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims, and cause the immediate return/remittance of any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and
Direct the City Accountant to book-up as assets completed BUB projects in the General Fund at acquisition cost.

We recommended that Management:
Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for the minimal implementation of its PPAs; and
Efficiently implement the priority PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMC to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 6.5 of NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC                   No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and Sections 4.4, 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management require the City Local Risk Reduction and Management Officer to hasten the formulation of the DRRM Implementing Plan and Manual of Operations that would provide the mechanism, rules and procedures and assessment and performance monitoring of the disaster risk reduction management and operation of the city government.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month duly certified by the City Accountant in conformity with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management require the SWM Board:
To revise the 10-Year SWM Plan and to incorporate in the CY’s AIP the following PPAs, to wit:
a.1 Solid Waste Reduction/Recycling/Composting
a.1.1 Monitoring and implementation programs/activities for:
proper segregation of wastes and maintenance of storages/receptacles for
Anti-Littering Law and No Plastic Policy;
Barangay based Composting Centers;
Establishment of barangay based Material Recovery Facilities and Barangay Processing Centers;
a.1.2 Repair/Rehabilitation of damaged composting facilities; and
a.1.3 Purchase of a new composting facility which could have better  processing capacity.
a.2 Collection
a.2.1 Intensify SWM Collection activities to assess compliance of solid waste collection routes with schedules and targeted areas.
a.2.2 Provision of necessary training to collectors and  personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste.
a.2.3 Direct the Project Coordinator of SWM to approve the Drivers Trip Ticket on the basis of gasoline consumption for the day of the particular area to be served based on approved garbage collection route and as scheduled to be attached to the Monthly Report of Official Travel of Motor Vehicles and Fuel Consumption Report.
a.3 Transfer
Program for the establishment of Transfer Stations pursuant to Section 25, Article III of RA 9003.
a.4 Disposal
Post-closure plan for the safe closure of Sibaguan Dumpsite and rehabilitation of the controlled dump site indicating the time frame and financial requirements.
Direct the Solid Waste Management Board to formulate concrete measures and guidelines as basis for the implementation of PPAs in all components of SWM particularly in solid reduction, collection, monitoring and evaluation, such as, but not limited to the following:
b.1 Intensify stricter implementation of segregation of solid waste at the barangay level;
b.2 Create a monitoring and evaluation committee to assess the implementation  effectiveness and efficiency of SWM;
Direct the GSO to properly assess the capacity of eight (8) garbage trucks if it can effectively carry out the previous year’s 17 collection routes per SWM Plan, if not, design and come up with thorough collection measures of waste based on realistic schedules to improve delivery of basic service to its clientele; and
Direct the Budget Officer to propose budgetary allocations for all PPAs identified in the SWM Plan for an effective program implementation, such as, but not limited on the following:
d.1 Solid waste collection, specifically for the following:
Lack of Garbage Truck
Fuel, Gasoline and Oil
Repairs and Maintenance of transportation equipment
Other General Services
d.2 Solid waste disposal, particularly the procurement of machineries to  complement the need in the new Sanitary Landfill.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2018-003 (2017) dated February 8, 2018 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require DepEd, Roxas City Division to submit report on the implementation and utilization of the funds transferred from the City of Roxas pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 as follows:
Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements certified correct by the Accountant, approved by the Head of IA, and duly audited by the Auditor of the IA;
Copy of Credit Notice issued by the Auditor of the IA; and
Copy of OR issued for the refund of unexpended/unutilized balance of fund transferred, if any; and
Ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of appropriated funds for priority programs and projects and reinforce the budgeting priorities by preparing the SEF Budget based on the DepEd approved SIP and DEDP.

We recommended that Management:
Refrain from including expenses that cannot be charged to the GAD Budget as provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
Give priority to emergency and/or continuing issues and concerns in identifying GAD PPAs giving emphasis to gender issues that can effectively addressed by GAD interventions  following the standards laid down in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, and other applicable guidelines on GAD in the agency’s PPAs.

We recommended that Management require the Roxas City Council for the Protection of Children to:
Regularly monitor the status on the implementation of PPAs; and
Enforce the implementation of PPAs in accordance with specified time frame per Annual Work and Financial Plan to ensure the full protection of children against all forms violence and overall development of children to their fullest potential.

We recommended that Management:
Prepare updated Lease Rental Agreement with tenants based on updated rates to protect the interest of both parties;
Provide the needed facilities to ensure proper collection of post-mortem inspection fees based on weights; and
Acquire equipment and improve the LEE’s facilities as contemplated in Section 5.1 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. VI dated June 10, 2016 Manual on the      Setting-up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises that profits and income derived from LEE operation shall be applied to following priorities.
First, cost of improvement, repair and related expenses of the LEE;
Second, return of advances received by the LEE to the General Fund (GF); and
Any excess shall form part of the GF.

We recommended that Management:
Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year in compliance with  Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 which prescribes the Rules and Regulations on  Supply and Property Management in the LGUs;
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:
Creation of Inventory Committee
A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.
Property Identification and Coding
Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.
Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.
Inventory Reports
The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.
Reconcile the inventory reports with the accounting and property records; and
Direct the City GSO to secure and submit Certificate of Land Title on real properties and Certificate of Registration on motor vehicles in the name of the City of Roxas on all assets taken up in the books to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.

We recommended that management:
Direct the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer to reconcile the charges to Donations against the sources and/or appropriated fund in Calendar Year 2017;
Direct the management to come-up with clear and specific guidelines on the grant of Emergency Assistance Program specifically on Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation, taking into consideration:
Limit/ceiling of aid to individual in crisis.
Limitations on the frequency of availment (ex: once a year/etc.) for a specific period, and
Priorities on the basis of grant to indigents as far as approval of amount;
Direct the payment of Donations thru checks for better control and only those small payments which cannot be paid conveniently by check shall be paid from the petty cash fund.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2018-001-300 (2017) dated January 24, 2018 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.
Management Comment:
In response to AOM No. 2018-001-300 (2017) dated January 24, 2018, the following documents were submitted by the Management on March 13, 2018:
Guidelines/policies for the implementation of AICS program in the City Mayor’s Office;
Affidavit executed by City of Roxas department heads who have availed of the AICS program together with some of their personnel; and
AICS Citizen Charter (portion) which provides that individuals and families on extreme difficulty brought about by a crisis and stressful situations that prevent them from functioning normally can avail of financial assistance even if they are gainfully employed.

The recommendation text appears in the content above under "In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:" and is therefore included verbatim in the content. If a separate recommendation field is required, it is:
Require the Human Resource Officer to review the staffing requirements of each department and recommend hiring of qualified personnel for regular appointment, if necessary, to regulate the employment of personnel under Job Order; and
Hire only JO personnel in accordance to the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs and assess the individual  assignments of all job order personnel to avoid redundant and duplicating work accomplishments.

observations.managementAction: Management conducted physical inventory but no report submitted to COA
A reconciliation was already made between the books of the LGU with that of the PGI books.
Demand Letter was issued by the Municipal Accountant to concerned officers and employees to liquidate their cash advances. Some settled already.
The Municipality is acting for the completion of the SLF as stated in the Municipality’s ESWM 10 year plan.

The management have already submitted a request for the dropping/removal of the Disallowance because the subject person can no longer be found. The said account has been in the books for more than 30 years now. The disallowance was traced back to the former Treasurer, Clarita De Guzman, who was charged with criminal offenses and was dismissed from public office. Amount adjusted to Government Equity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for Local Chief Executive to demand payment from A.P. Consebido Construction and Trading for the cost deficiency of ₱35,605.60 and uncollected liquidated damages (LD) of ₱18,769.37 (COA TAS LD computation less LD already collected from the final billing), otherwise, concerned officials can be held personally liable for the amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and schedules.

We also recommended for the management to strictly comply with the design standards prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 11 in the programming and implementation of Farm to Market Road projects and to appropriate funds for concreting of roads instead of road regravelling since this type of project offers no long term improvement on road conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to strictly observe the proper review procedure of GPB in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. Lastly, PPAs to be implemented should be based on duly approved GPB by the DILG Regional Office.

observations.recommendation:
Require the submission of Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) detailing management action on how settlement on unliquidated cash advances to SDO and to officers and employees shall be enforced.

Direct the City Accountant to submit proof that all remedies have been exhausted relative collection and cause the immediate liquidation by the concerned Special Disbursing Officers on all unliquidated cash advances granted. Enforce administrative sanctions to persons with accountabilities;

Adhere strictly on the liquidation of Advances to Officers and Employees within 30 days after travel. For those advances not used for a period of two (2) months it must be returned or refunded immediately; and

Direct the City Accountant to withhold salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for their unliquidated cash advances as provided in Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Require the City Accountant to prepare schedule, analyze the nature of accounts and determine to whom these “liabilities” Due to LGUs are payable.

Prepare adjusting entry to reclassify the recorded to Due to LGUs and Due to PhilHealth as of  December 31, 2016, and make necessary adjustments, if needed as follows:

To record the receipt of amount held in trust for specific purpose to Trust Liabilities following the prescribed accounting entry.

Cash in Bank           			xxx.x
			Trust Liabilities			xxx.xx

To adjust, as appropriate and to properly recognize Trust Liabilities already         recorded to Due to LGUs and Due to PhilHealth following the adjusting entry shown:

	Due to LGUs			xxx.xx
	Due to Philhealth			xxx.xx
	Trust Liabilities			xxx.xx

To adjust, as appropriate receipts credited to Due to LGU in Trust Fund that partakes Service, Business or Miscellaneous Income of the General Fund, the following adjusting entry is recommended:

Due to LGU			xxx.xx

Direct the City Accountant to conduct periodic verification and validation of the existence of Due to NGAs amounting to ₱19.404 million as of              December 31, 2016 and determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims. Return/remit to Source Agency any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
Direct the City Accountant to reconcile with Source Agency unused fund transfers, prepare adjusting entry for reconciling items and initiate the filing of write-off for undocumented dormant accounts taking into consideration the requirements as provided in COA Circular 2016-005 dated                   December 19, 2016;
Direct the City Accountant to comply strictly on documentary requirements to secure Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreements and approved project proposal to form part of supporting documents depending on the nature of transaction in the implementation and liquidation of fund transfers as provided in Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.; and
Direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay funded programs of various NGAs to attain the program’s objectives by creating Project Monitoring Committee to oversee all fund transfer for BuB Projects, as well as for other projects.

Include in the LDRRMP the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended   LDRRMF  of the previous years; and

Review revise and update the current year’s LDRRMP and AIP for PPAs funded by the national government, other LGUs, and other organizations.

Strengthen the implementation of priority PPAs identified in the LDRRMP; and

Revise and update the LDRRMP and AIP if needed and refrain from implementation of PPAs not programmed in the approved LDRRMP.

Establish measures and guidelines to intensify solid waste collection including the nine collection routes  not covered by the regular solid waste collection trucks to ensure promotion of public health and environment; and 

Control and monitor fuel utilization in every solid waste collection route.

Require the City Accountant to record fund transfer to DepEd, Roxas City Division following the prescribed accounting entry:

Due from NGAs (10303010 )                        	XXX.XX
		Cash In Bank   (10102010)		    XXX.XX
Make the necessary adjustments to properly recognize fund transfers to DepEd, Roxas City Division following the adjusting entry as shown:

Due from NGA			XXX.XX
	Subsidy to NGA			       XXX.XX
	Due to other NGA’s		       XXX.XX
Require DepEd, Roxas City Division to submit report on the implementation and utilization of the funds received from the City government pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular no. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 as follows:

Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements certified correct by the Accountant, approved by the Head of IA, and duly audited by the Auditor of the IA;

Copy of Credit Notice issued by the Auditor of the IA;

Copy of OR issued for the refund of unexpended/unutilized balance of fund transferred; and 

Submit Project Expenditures/Estimated Expenses upon request for a Fund Transfer.

Immediately stop the utilization of SEF for purposes other than those provided in the Letter of Instruction No. 1462, series of 1985, implementing guidelines of RA 5447; Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160; DECS-DBM-DILG nos. 01-A, series of 2000 and 01-B, series of 2001 and Section 83 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
Implement fully the programs, activities and projects identified in the Annual Local School Board Budget to attain the objectives of SEF of providing financial support to intended beneficiaries as embodied in RA No. 5447.
We recommended that Management study on how to mainstream GAD related activities, prepare attribution and if necessary, revise the GAD Plan and Budget and provide budget for GAD priority areas.

Require the City General Services Officer to consolidate all requisitions for supplies and materials of similar items quarterly for procurement through public bidding;  

Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 10 and 48.2 of the Revised 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184, and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated                   October 20, 1992.

Regularly monitor the status on the implementation of PPAs; and 


Enforce the implementation of PPAs in accordance to specified time frame per CY’s Annual Work and Financial Plan to ensure the full protection of children against all forms violence and overall development of children to their fullest potential.

On the Implementation of the FMR Projects

The Roxas City Government should:

Comply strictly with DPWH DOs in preparing quantity and cost estimates to reflect the accurate project cost in the ABC, POW and detailed cost estimates.;

Implement FMR projects in accordance with approved POW, plans and specifications. Any revision or deviations from approved POW made during project implementation should be properly documented and reflected in revised program of work and supported with as-built plan;

Require the concerned City Officials to refund the project deficiencies amounting to  ₱3,142,685.21; and

Thoroughly review documents supporting project expenditures to ensure that project funds are utilized exclusively for the intended purpose.

On Information/Publicity about the Project

The City should comply strictly with Section 2.2 and Sub-sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, to ensure that the public is informed of any subsequent projects that will be implemented to foster transparency and accountability and also to encourage public participation.

      advances. Stop granting cash advance  to  those  whose  previous  cash
      advance has not yet been  fully  liquidated  and  or  settled.  Follow

      002.

      concerned  responsible  officer  to  submit  a  copies  of  government
      contracts, purchase orders  and  their  supporting  documents  and  to

      February 12, 2009.

      supporting documents in  the  books.  Also,  financial  statements  be
      submitted on time.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure the periodic physical inventory of PPE, reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor.
The Municipal Treasurer should maintain property cards for PPE with complete information. These property cards should be reconciled with other property records like Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE).
The Municipal Accountant should ensure that property subsidiary ledgers contain complete details/description of the PPE to facilitate proper reconciliation with the inventory report of the inventory committee. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that monthly depreciation is recognized in the books so that the cost of all the PPEs are allocated over the estimated useful life of the assets.
In case of loss of property, the officer immediately accountable thereof should file an application for relief from accountability. The request should be accompanied by the documents required under Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to regularly update the subsidiary ledger for each depository account and to strictly observe the monthly preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements for the determination of the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements, in compliance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to serve a written notice to all accountable officers and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if necessary.
In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant must ensure that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
Lastly, grant of cash advances to individuals who are not employed in the municipal government must likewise be stopped as the Municipal Mayor has no administrative supervision over them and thus withholding of salaries cannot be instituted against them.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to transfer the fund maintained at PNB and FCB in the total amount of ₱1,442,495.92 to AGDB, in compliance with DOF Department Circular No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015, as amended by DOF Department Circular Nos. 003-2015 dated August 24, 2015 and 002-2016 dated July 8, 2016.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that: a) all the insurable assets have been identified/inventoried and classified as such; b) all insurable assets and properties are adequately covered/insured with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS; and c) adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums are provided and paid to the GSIS.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the Notes to FS the (a) changes between the original and final budgets; (b) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (c) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the BAC should adhere with the provisions of Section 12.2 and must prepare the PMR in its prescribed format for the first and second semesters of 2016 for the approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the GPPB and shall be done on each ensuing semester thereafter.

We recommended for Local Chief Executive to demand payment from A.P. Consebido Construction and Trading for the cost deficiency of ₱35,605.60 and uncollected liquidated damages (LD) of ₱18,769.37 (COA TAS LD computation less LD already collected from the final billing), otherwise, concerned officials can be held personally liable for the amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and schedules.
We also recommended for the management to strictly comply with the design standards prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 11 in the programming and implementation of Farm to Market Road projects and to appropriate funds for concreting of roads instead of road regravelling since this type of project offers no long term improvement on road conditions.

We recommended for the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to strictly observe the proper review procedure of GPB in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The GFPS should ensure that all recommendations of the DILG Regional Office are properly observed before incorporating the GPB in the Annual Budget. Lastly, PPAs to be implemented should be based on duly approved GPB by the DILG Regional Office.

Recommendations:
Require all concerned officials and employees to liquidate and/or settle their cash advances immediately.  Details are shown in the attached Annex “A”.
Also, send follow-up and/or demand letters requiring the personnel concerned to liquidate, otherwise, impose the withholding of salaries and other emoluments due to the cash advances.  For those who are no longer connected with the Municipality, demand letters are to be sent again to their present addresses or stations.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall maintain subsidiary ledger, schedules and retrieve records that will support the account Due from Officers and Employees consisting claims from the agency personnel. Further, take-up the necessary adjustments/corrections, provided there is/are substantial proof/s to support of the said adjustments/corrections in order to establish accuracy and existence of the affected account.

Recommendations:
1. In the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality, please see to it that it should clearly specify in detail the exact description of the project, location and project cost.
2.  Fast-track the implementation of projects which are already identified and evaluate the viability of funds to determine the cause/s of non-implementation.
In the implementation of projects by administration, consider the existing manpower of the Municipal Engineering Office and the availability of equipment.  Funds are allocated on the basis of approved programs and projects.  Thus, when specific projects are identified, it is expected that the need for the project must be met within the time duration for it completion.
During the budget preparation, coordinate with the barangay officials in identifying the barangay constituents needs, which may be funded-out of the 20% development fund as an aid to be considered in the plan.

Recommendations:
1. Proper coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by the municipality.
2. See to it that reported PPAs are reconciled against the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office before submission to COA for audit/validation.
3. Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
4. Provide the Office of the Auditor of any corrections/revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for our validation/monitoring.

Recommendation:
We recommend that basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the Local Government Code should be considered in the implementation of fund so as not to deprive the constituents of its benefit.

Recommendations:
1. Fast-track the implementation of projects which are identified and evaluate the process of project implementation which are started during the year despite availability of funds to determine the cause/s of its non-implementation.
2. Require the office concerned to comply the terms and conditions of the MOA especially on the implementation of the project.

Recommendations:
The members of the BAC has to follow the conditions sets forth in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as to the implementation of alternative procurement in order to ensure transparency and competitiveness even with the use of such methods.
Consolidation of all the PPMPs submitted by the various end-user offices must be pursued with the use of APP as one of the procurement strategy in the procurement of foods and infrastructure projects to be able the BAC to determine the extent of quantities, quality and cost.
Establish procurement timeliness in consonance with project schedules, including a cut-off date for processing requisitions and issuance of purchase orders to ensure receipt of most procured items in times for the undertaking of projects, programs and activities, with provisions for exceptions only in case of emergencies.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management that the Municipal SWM Board should exercise their duties and responsibilities as what has been contemplated in the 10 year SWM Plan to ensure the effective implementation of SWM Programs.
The SWM Board shall also oversee the execution of SWM operations so as to determine whether practices required under R.A. 9003 which are not being followed and lapses in SWM operation as well can be immediately noted and corrected.

Recommendations:
We recommend that management shall strictly adhere to the provisions of National Budget Circular No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 regarding procurement of second hand vehicle so as to avoid wastage of government funds.

Recommendation:
We recommend that in the succeeding fiscal years, management shall include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities as required under RA 9442 amending RA 7277 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2014-59.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management of the Municipality of President Manuel Roxas should insure all insurable assets with the GSIS to cover for the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event, and to comply with Section 489 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I and COA No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that Management:
Require the submission of Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) detailing management action on how settlement on unliquidated cash advances to SDO and to officers and employees shall be enforced.
Direct the City Accountant to submit proof that all remedies have been exhausted relative collection and cause the immediate liquidation by the concerned Special Disbursing Officers on all unliquidated cash advances granted. Enforce administrative sanctions to persons with accountabilities;
Adhere strictly on the liquidation of Advances to Officers and Employees within 30 days after travel. For those advances not used for a period of two (2) months it must be returned or refunded immediately; and
Direct the City Accountant to withhold salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for their unliquidated cash advances as provided in Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2016-21	dated September 23, 2016 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year in compliance with  Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 which prescribes the Rules and Regulations on  Supply and Property Management in the LGUs;
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:
Creation of Inventory Committee
A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.
Property Identification and Coding
Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.
Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.
Inventory Reports
The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.
Reconcile the inventory reports with the accounting and property records.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-016 (2016) dated February 16, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the City Accountant to prepare schedule, analyze the nature of accounts and determine to whom these “liabilities” Due to LGUs are payable.
Prepare adjusting entry to reclassify the recorded to Due to LGUs and Due to PhilHealth as of  December 31, 2016, and make necessary adjustments, if needed as follows:

The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-018-300 (2016) dated February 20, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the City Accountant to conduct periodic verification and validation of the existence of Due to NGAs amounting to ₱19.404 million as of              December 31, 2016 and determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims. Return/remit to Source Agency any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
Direct the City Accountant to reconcile with Source Agency unused fund transfers, prepare adjusting entry for reconciling items and initiate the filing of write-off for undocumented dormant accounts taking into consideration the requirements as provided in COA Circular 2016-005 dated                   December 19, 2016;
Direct the City Accountant to comply strictly on documentary requirements to secure Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreements and approved project proposal to form part of supporting documents depending on the nature of transaction in the implementation and liquidation of fund transfers as provided in Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.; and
Direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Head to implement immediately without delay funded programs of various NGAs to attain the program’s objectives by creating Project Monitoring Committee to oversee all fund transfer for BuB Projects, as well as for other projects.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-004-300 (2016) dated January 20, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the City Engineer to:
Publicize in the form of tarpaulin/signboards all infrastructure projects to be implemented  in their project location as prescribed in Item 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013;
Report the status of all infrastructure projects in the quarterly report of the infrastructure projects for easy monitoring and validation;  and
Inform the audit team, for purposes of monitoring and validation, within ten (10) days after the award/implementation of all infrastructure projects implemented.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-08 (2016) dated January 26, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC to submit within five (5) days all approved POs with supporting bidding documents to the Audit Team;
Direct the City Engineer to use as basis  the Standard Specifications for Public Works in the preparation of POW for infrastructure projects particularly on Mobilization, EAO, and project duration;  and
Direct the City Engineer to inform and submit to the Audit Team within ten (10) days the awarded contracts and the infrastructure projects executed.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-09 (2016) dated January 27, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Include in the LDRRMP the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended   LDRRMF  of the previous years; and
Review revise and update the current year’s LDRRMP and AIP for PPAs funded by the national government, other LGUs, and other organizations.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-02 (2016)    dated January 12, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.
We recommended that Management require the City Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to:
Strengthen the implementation of priority PPAs identified in the LDRRMP; and
Revise and update the LDRRMP and AIP if needed and refrain from implementation of PPAs not programmed in the approved LDRRMP.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-03 (2016) dated January 12, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.
We recommended that Management require the City Local Risk Reduction and Management Officer to hasten the formulation of the DRRM Implementing Plan and Manual of Operations that would provide the mechanism, rules and procedures and assessment and performance monitoring of the disaster risk reduction management and operation of the city government.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-03 (2016) dated January 12, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.
