The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-011 (2016) dated February 8, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Immediately stop the utilization of SEF for purposes other than those provided in the Letter of Instruction No. 1462, series of 1985, implementing guidelines of RA 5447; Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160; DECS-DBM-DILG nos. 01, series of 1998, supplemented and clarified per  DECS-DBM-DILG nos. 01-A, series of 2000 and 01-B, series of 2001 and    Section 83 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; 
In case of fund transfers, a MOA between the transferor and transferee should be made; and
Instruct the members of the Local School Board to formulate PPAs in accordance with the above stated provisions of law.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-012 (2016) dated February 8, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management study on how to mainstream GAD related activities, prepare attribution and if necessary, revise the GAD Plan and Budget and provide budget for GAD priority areas.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-006-100 (2016) dated January 26, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the City General Services Officer to consolidate all requisitions for supplies and materials of similar items quarterly for procurement through public bidding;
Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 10 and 48.2 of the Revised 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184, and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated                   October 20, 1992.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-019 (2016) dated February 27, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Indicate the nature of work to be performed in the Job Order Contract and submit Accomplishment Reports for actual work performed;
Require the Human Resource Officer to review the staffing requirements of each department and recommend hiring of qualified personnel for regular appointment, if necessary, to regulate the employment of personnel under Job Order Contract; and
Hire only JO personnel in accordance to the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs and assess the individual  assignments of all job order personnel to avoid redundant and duplicating work accomplishments.
The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-13 (2016) dated February 27, 2017 for the foregoing observations and recommendations.

We recommend that Management require the Roxas City Council for the Protection of Children:
Regularly monitor the status on the implementation of PPAs; and
Enforce the implementation of PPAs in accordance to specified time frame per CY’s Annual Work and Financial Plan to ensure the full protection of children against all forms violence and overall development of children to their fullest potential.

We recommended the following courses of action to management:
Notify the AOs concerned for the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004.
Impose sanctions prescribed in the said Circulars for non-compliance thereof, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them.
Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries to correct the Cash in Bank balances reflecting in the financial statements. Henceforth, prepare and submit the BRS timely in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain updated and properly accomplished stock cards as prescribed in the NGAS Manual; and
The Local Chief Executive shall create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete actual physical count of inventories. The result of the physical count shall be reported in RPCI and are promptly reconciled with accountabilities recorded in the books of accounts and the stock cards and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
We recommended that the management to refrain from charging expenditures not within the bounds of laws and regulations on SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books of accounts for PCF to provide more effective control over the fund. Likewise, strictly adhere to the provisions of PhilHealth Circular No. 40 series of 2000 on the management of Philhealth Capitation Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality refrain from granting communication allowance, rather set a policy regarding the grant of communication expense identifying the allowed officials and employees, the amount to be granted and the plan to be used in order to identify the most favorable option for the municipality’s scarce resources, and submit complete documents like bills, official receipt for the telephone bills and certification that all calls are official for the paid communication expenses and ensuing claim for the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the management to observe economy and austerity measures in the use of government funds. Instead, focus the utilization of the government funds for purposes which are more beneficial to the constituents

We recommended for the management to refrain from incurring unnecessary expenditures and channel its resources to the implementation of projects and programs which are more beneficial to the constituents of the municipality.
We recommended for the management to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is utilized strictly in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to serve a written notice to all accountable officers and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if necessary.

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant must ensure that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

Lastly, grant of cash advances to individuals who are not employed in the municipal government must likewise be stopped as the Municipal Mayor has no administrative supervision over them and thus withholding of salaries cannot be instituted against them.

We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of the aforementioned laws. These laws enjoin all public official or employee to avoid conflict of interest at all times. Any public officer and employee must refrain from having direct or indirect financial or pecuniary interest in any business, contract or transaction with the LGU in which he/she is an official or employee.

We recommended for the management to 1) appropriate funds for concreting of roads instead of road reshaping and gravelling since maintenance projects offer no long term improvement on road conditions, 2) ensure that no infrastructure projects shall be undertaken without the required detailed engineering investigations, surveys and plans and 3) monitor and prepare a report on project accomplishments duly supported by equipment and fuel utilization reports and photographs taken before, during and after project implementation.

We recommend that the Agency Head will request all offices to conduct their physical count and submit the required reports to the property officer for validation/ consolidation purposes.  These actions assist the inventory committee to comply its task easily. Require the inventory committee to prepare and submit the report to the Local Chief Executive and copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Actual Physical inventory was already conducted, however, the committee are still in process of reconciling with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall: Direct all concerned officials and employees to settle immediately their unliquidated cash advances. Failure to do so shall constitute cause for the filing of malversation charge under Article 217. (b)  Require the withholding of salaries and other emoluments to those officials and employees who are still in service effective immediately and apply the said withheld amounts in full satisfaction of their respective unliquidated outstanding cash advances. (c)  Instruct the Municipal Accountant and department heads to ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances per COA Circular Nos. 97-002.  Require the Municipal Accountant to issued demand letters to all concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. (d)  Request for write-off for unliquidated cash advances and other receivables which have been outstanding and dormant for more than ten (10) years which qualify on the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of Dormant Unliquidated Cash Advances and Receivables Accounts. (e)   Remind all accountable officers that pursuant to the SOLANA Covenant, the Commission on Audit, Civil Service Commission and the Office of the Ombudsman shall initiate the appropriate administrative and criminal charges based on COA Auditor’s report on government officials and employees with long outstanding unliquidated cash advances.

We recommend that the Supply and General Services Office conduct physical inventory and verify the recorded balances for reconciliation as presented in the Financial Position to present the correct amount. Update all supporting documents and records as mentioned in the above cited provision in order to have a reliable and correct financial condition and operation of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Reports from the implementin
g offices were already submitted for the booking up of the actual inventory on hand, however, not yet 100%.

observations.recommendation: 1.  We recommend that the concerned Accountable Officer should turn-over her collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer as mandated in Sections 29 of NGAS, Volume I, to ensure the prevention of possible loss, unauthorized use and misappropriation. 2.   Further, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant institute measure to monitor all concerned accountable officers to adhere to the existing policies on proper cash management of the agency.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to make a detailed analysis and reconciliation of recorded transactions of account Cash Local Treasury in the General Ledger and Cashbook to reconcile the discrepancy of P1,032,090.99. Henceforth, reconciliation between the Accounting and Accountable Officer’s records should be made once a month to ensure the accuracy of recorded data in both records.

We recommend to the management particularly the MSWDO to prepare and submit the PR to the BAC office within two (2) weeks or one (1) month in order to facilitate the feeding as per activity design/program for the SFP. Also, we further recommend to the management that the conduct of bidding for goods which will be used for the SFP, if possible, be done as soon as the PR will be received by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality should conduct gender analysis and assessment so that they can formulate policies and plans which can address gender issues in the LGU and mainstream the program, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.
